source.subject: Request: Extended Research Credits + Governance Docs for CivicData Hub Pilot
messages:
From: Dr. Anna R. Patel <anna@civicdata.org>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Cc: CivicData Hub Legal <legal@civicdata.org>
Date: Mon, 02 Jul 2028 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot credits request and governance docs

Hi Alex,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the Redwood runtime looks promising for our transit anonymization pipeline. CivicData Hub runs a volunteer-driven pilot with three mid-size cities; we want to run a 3-month analysis of ridership patterns and anonymized origin-destination matrices.

Two asks:
- Can Redwood extend trial credits for the pilot beyond the standard 30 days? We're estimating ~1M tokens for the 3-month run.
- We need governance documentation (SOC2 attestation or equivalent, data handling and retention policy) to satisfy our board and city legal teams.

Attached is a short SOW and a one-page pilot description. If you need a formal Letter of Intent I can share that too.

Best,
Anna
Dr. Anna R. Patel
Research Director, CivicData Hub
anna@civicdata.org | +1 415-555-0189
[attachment: CivicData_SOW.docx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document, 48KB)]

From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Dr. Anna R. Patel <anna@civicdata.org>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 03 Jul 2028 10:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot credits request and governance docs

Hi Anna — great to meet last week, and thanks for the SOW.

Short answer: we can support a pilot beyond the standard 30 days, but I need to confirm the credit amount with our promotions and partnerships teams. Quick plan: 
- I can pre-approve a 30-day extension + 500k token credit from our pilot pool.
- For the full 3-month, 1M token ask, I'll escalate for an additional credit grant and coordinate with Marissa and Ben.
- I attached the basic SOC2 readiness letter we can share while you complete vendor paperwork. For anything formal your legal team needs, we can provide the SOC2 PDF and a short data retention attestation.

Can you confirm the start date you want for the pilot and the primary contact for DPA/legal discussions? Also: any constraints on bring-your-own-encryption or region?

I'll loop Ben and Marissa for approval.

Thanks,
Alex Martinez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
alex@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0123
[attachment: Redwood_SOC2_prelim_letter.pdf (application/pdf, 58KB)]

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 04 Jul 2028 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot credits request and governance docs

Alex / Marissa —

Looping in here. Quick details I pulled from HubSpot (RDW-DEAL-2028-118): CivicData Hub is a non-profit consortium, low ACV; pilot could convert to a paid contract in Q4 if the cities commit. Given the public-sector risk profiles, approving an accelerated credit grant (1M tokens) is ok from a GTM POV but needs a signed MOA that includes an explicit data handling attestation and a limited usage clause.

Ask: Marissa, can partnerships approve 1M tokens + 90-day extension if we require MOA + SOC2 attestation and limit credits to the pilot org (no redistribution)? If yes I will tag this in the promotions queue and notify finance for negative budget impact (low).

Ben
Director, Growth — Redwood Inference

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 05 Jul 2028 11:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot credits request and governance docs

Ben / Alex —

Approve as requested with conditions. Approve 1,000,000 tokens and a 90-day extension contingent on:
1) Signed MOA (limited to CivicData Hub pilot entities)
2) Receipt of a vendor questionnaire / SOC2 letter on file (we can accept an attestation from your auditor if SOC2 report is in-process)
3) Quarterly check-in and a short usage report after 45 days

Alex — please prepare the MOA and attach the SOC2 letter we already provided. I'll sign off and request final finance tagging. I'll also ask Legal to confirm the DPA language if their legal team proposes edits (cc Legal on the outbound).

Marissa Cole
Head of Partnerships, Redwood Inference

From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Dr. Anna R. Patel <anna@civicdata.org>
Cc: CivicData Hub Legal <legal@civicdata.org>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 09 Jul 2028 11:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot credits request and governance docs

Anna — good news. Per our internal approvals, Redwood will provide the following for the CivicData Hub transit pilot:

- 1,000,000 free token credits (applied to pilot account)
- Pilot window: 90 days from the agreed start date
- Conditions: signed MOA limiting use to the named pilot participants, and SOC2 attestation or equivalent on file.

Attached: Pilot_MOA.pdf (please review, sign via DocuSign), SOC2_letter.pdf (Redwood preliminary attestation), and a templated SOW (CivicData_SOW.docx) populated with the pilot cities and timelines.

Next steps:
1) Review MOA and sign via DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/en/example/rdw-civicdata-moa
2) Send the signed MOA + vendor questionnaire to legal@redwood.com
3) Confirm desired pilot start date (we can backdate credit activation to the first data ingest date if needed)

If your legal wants edits to the DPA, please flag them and cc Marissa — we'll route any redlines to our Legal AG.

Thanks — excited to get this pilot running.

—Alex
Alex Martinez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
alex@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0123
[attachment: Pilot_MOA.pdf (application/pdf, 142KB)]

source.subject: Syncware renewal kickoff — timeline & QBR availability
messages:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.ai>
To: Sam Holland <sam.holland@syncware.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 29 Jun 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Syncware renewal kickoff — timeline & QBR availability

Hi Sam / Priya —

Thanks for the heads-up that your contract is up in September. I'd like to kick off the renewal and capacity conversation so we can lock an efficient path for the +30% throughput you're planning next year. Proposed agenda below; please confirm stakeholders and availability for a QBR during the first two weeks of July.

Quick agenda (30–45m):
- Current usage & headroom (engineering)
- Capacity forecast & burst readiness (ops)
- Contract/term options and pricing impact (sales/finance)
- Timeline & mutual action plan (MAP) + next steps

Suggested QBR windows (PDT):
- Tue, Jul 5 — 10:00–10:45
- Wed, Jul 6 — 14:00–14:45
- Thu, Jul 7 — 09:00–09:45

Can you confirm: 1) who from Syncware will attend (procurement + engineering + exec)? 2) who owns procurement questions / PO timing? 3) whether there are any upcoming forecasted spikes we should plan for?

I've attached a draft Renewal MAP (Renewal-MAP-2028.xlsx) and a short QBR agenda doc. I can also spin up a short technical pre-call with Ben if you want to review capacity numbers before the QBR.

Next steps once we lock a date: send calendar invite, share MAP link, and capture legal/finance evidence items.

Thanks — looking forward to aligning.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
---
Attachment: Renewal-MAP-2028.xlsx (spreadsheet stub)
Attachment: qbr-agenda-draft.docx (doc stub)
From: Sam Holland <sam.holland@syncware.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Rao <priya@syncware.com>, procurement@syncware.com
Date: Fri, 29 Jun 2028 10:35:00 -0700

source.subject: Pilot sizing & deployment economics clarification
messages:
From: Elena Morales <elena@brightcrafted.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 14 Jun 2028 08:23:00 -0700
Subject: Follow-up: deployment path and cost model questions

Hi Aisha — thanks for the productive demo earlier today. Quick follow-up from our side as we start to map internal approvals and the procurement ask.

Recap of our priorities: low 100–200ms tail latency for user-facing chat, data residency in US, and predictable monthly cost for budgeting. We are evaluating three options you outlined — Hosted API, Dedicated capacity (reserved GPU pool), and Private VPC/on-prem for the sensitive subset.

To get us to an internal cost-committee meeting next week, can you confirm: 1) what inputs Redwood needs to produce an apples-to-apples cost worksheet; 2) a simple threshold (monthly tokens or QPS) where Dedicated becomes demonstrably cheaper than per-token Hosted; and 3) any minimum commitment term for Dedicated pilot pricing?

Rough usage signals from our telemetry (this is an early estimate): average 1.2M user messages/month, avg prompt+response = 350 tokens, peak concurrent users ~120 (p95 steady-state QPS ~3.5), expect growth 20% q/q if demo looks good. A small subset (~7%) will require the private-data workflow.

If you can get a short cost sketch and next steps by EOD Monday (US), that helps our budget owner. Also include any legal/PO triggers for starting a Dedicated pilot.

Thanks —
Elena Morales
Head of Product
BrightCrafted AI

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@brightcrafted.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 14 Jun 2028 10:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Follow-up: deployment path and cost model questions

Hi Elena — great to meet you today, and thanks for the clear priorities. Below is a concise plan and the inputs we used to build the attached cost workbook (redwood_pilot_pricing_workbook.xlsx). I kept the assumptions conservative; if you can confirm the usage numbers you quoted we’ll tighten the math.

1) Inputs we need from you to finalize a precise worksheet:
- Avg tokens per request (you provided 350 — please confirm);
- Expected monthly active requests (1.2M messages × usage multiplier if bot-initiated follow-ups count);
- Peak sustained QPS and p99 latency SLO;
- Percentage of traffic requiring private-data handling (you said ~7%);
- Desired SLA/uptime and whether burst capacity is acceptable.

2) Quick threshold guidance (ballpark):
- Hosted (per-token): best for proof-of-concept and low-to-medium volume with unpredictable bursts. Example ballpark math: if Hosted list price = X per 1k tokens, with your numbers -> ~420M tokens/month (1.2M messages × 350 tokens) => hosted_cost ≈ (420,000k tokens / 1k) * X.
- Dedicated (reserved capacity): starts to make sense when sustained throughput requires a standing GPU pool (rough proxy: when sustained qps and p50 token rate push you into continuous GPU usage for >10–14 hours/day). For many customers we see crossover at ~10–20M tokens/month depending on model size and microarchitecture. For your profile (420M tokens/month is significantly above that), Dedicated will likely lower unit cost once we size the pool correctly.
- Private VPC/on-prem: recommended when data residency, audit logs, or strict network isolation are non-negotiable. It carries additional setup and infra costs (network egress, customer-managed KMS/HSM) and typically requires a longer onboarding window.

3) Commitment/terms for Dedicated pilot:
- We can do a 3-month pilot with a discounted committed pool (30–50% discount off committed pricing) to validate performance/cost; requires a simple MSA addendum and a purchase order for the first month. If procurement needs a shorter commercial instrument we can do a trial credit on Hosted while we finalize Dedicated terms.

Attached is a starter workbook with two tabs: Hosted-vs-Reserved (uses your quoted token numbers) and Dedicated-sizing (maps GPU counts to expected sustained tokens/QPS). The workbook lists assumed model family and quantization that drive GPU throughput — we’ll refine after Vivek reviews your latency/throughput goals.

Next steps I propose:
- You confirm the token averages and whether the 1.2M messages is counting user-only messages or includes system-initiated messages.
- I’ll book a 30-min call between your infra owner + Vivek to review latency and sizing details (Vivek can propose the recommended Dedicated SKU/throughput).
- Ben will share the pilot commercial outline and the mutual action plan doc.

If that works I’ll send the calendar invite and update the workbook based on any clarifications.

Best —
Aisha Rahman
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@brightcrafted.ai>
Cc: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 15 Jun 2028 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Follow-up: deployment path and cost model questions

Elena — quick note from commercial: we can put together two purchase paths for you to present to finance: 1) Hosted PO (pay-as-you-go) and 2) Dedicated 3-month pilot (committed GPU pool). For the Dedicated pilot we typically ask for a one-month advance PO to reserve capacity; the attached mutual_action_plan.docx frames milestones and decision gates (proof of latency targets, cost per 1M tokens vs hosted, and security checklist for private flows).

To help move this along we can offer a one-time pilot credit that offsets setup for Dedicated (we’ll cover X% of the first month) — I’ll call that out in the formal quote once we confirm sizing. Monica from CS is looped for onboarding details and integration docs.

Attachments: redwood_pilot_pricing_workbook.xlsx (see Hosted/Dedicated tabs)

—Ben
Director, Mid-Market Sales
Redwood Inference

From: Elena Morales <elena@brightcrafted.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, procurement@brightcrafted.ai
Date: Sat, 16 Jun 2028 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Follow-up: deployment path and cost model questions

Thanks Aisha / Ben — that plan looks good. Confirming the numbers you used are correct for our initial ask: 1.2M messages/month counting user messages only (no system-initiated follow-ups), avg 350 tokens, and peak ~3.5 QPS. The 7% private-data traffic is accurate.

We’ll review the workbook over the weekend. Can you send the 30-min technical call invite for next week (include Vivek + Monica + our infra lead Tom Reyes)? Our procurement team is available to review the pilot PO template on Tuesday afternoon.

Also please surface any specific compliance artifacts we’ll need for the private-flow approval (SOC 2 in-scope, audit-logs sample, KMS integration notes).

Elena Morales
Head of Product
BrightCrafted AI

On Fri, Jun 15, 2028 at 09:02 AM Redwood Inference <ben@redwood.com> wrote:
> Elena — quick note from commercial: we can put together two purchase paths for you to present to finance: 1) Hosted PO (pay-as-you-go) and 2) Dedicated 3-month pilot (committed GPU pool). For the Dedicated pilot we typically ask for a one-month advance PO to reserve capacity; the attached mutual_action_plan.docx frames milestones and decision gates (proof of latency targets, cost per 1M tokens vs hosted, and security checklist for private flows).
>
> —Ben

text:
From: Elena Morales <elena@brightcrafted.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 14 Jun 2028 08:23:00 -0700
Subject: Follow-up: deployment path and cost model questions

Hi Aisha — thanks for the productive demo earlier today. Quick follow-up from our side as we start to map internal approvals and the procurement ask.

Recap of our priorities: low 100–200ms tail latency for user-facing chat, data residency in US, and predictable monthly cost for budgeting. We are evaluating three options you outlined — Hosted API, Dedicated capacity (reserved GPU pool), and Private VPC/on-prem for the sensitive subset.

To get us to an internal cost-committee meeting next week, can you confirm: 1) what inputs Redwood needs to produce an apples-to-apples cost worksheet; 2) a simple threshold (monthly tokens or QPS) where Dedicated becomes demonstrably cheaper than per-token Hosted; and 3) any minimum commitment term for Dedicated pilot pricing?

Rough usage signals from our telemetry (this is an early estimate): average 1.2M user messages/month, avg prompt+response = 350 tokens, peak concurrent users ~120 (p95 steady-state QPS ~3.5), expect growth 20% q/q if demo looks good. A small subset (~7%) will require the private-data workflow.

If you can get a short cost sketch and next steps by EOD Monday (US), that helps our budget owner. Also include any legal/PO triggers for starting a Dedicated pilot.

Thanks —
Elena Morales
Head of Product
BrightCrafted AI



From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@brightcrafted.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 14 Jun 2028 10:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Follow-up: deployment path and cost model questions

Hi Elena — great to meet you today, and thanks for the clear priorities. Below is a concise plan and the inputs we used to build the attached cost workbook (redwood_pilot_pricing_workbook.xlsx). I kept the assumptions conservative; if you can confirm the usage numbers you quoted we’ll tighten the math.

1) Inputs we need from you to finalize a precise worksheet:
- Avg tokens per request (you provided 350 — please confirm);
- Expected monthly active requests (1.2M messages × usage multiplier if bot-initiated follow-ups count);
- Peak sustained QPS and p99 latency SLO;
- Percentage of traffic requiring private-data handling (you said ~7%);
- Desired SLA/uptime and whether burst capacity is acceptable.

2) Quick threshold guidance (ballpark):
- Hosted (per-token): best for proof-of-concept and low-to-medium volume with unpredictable bursts. Example ballpark math: if Hosted list price = X per 1k tokens, with your numbers -> ~420M tokens/month (1.2M messages × 350 tokens) => hosted_cost ≈ (420,000k tokens / 1k) * X.
- Dedicated (reserved capacity): starts to make sense when sustained throughput requires a standing GPU pool (rough proxy: when sustained qps and p50 token rate push you into continuous GPU usage for >10–14 hours/day). For many customers we see crossover at ~10–20M tokens/month depending on model size and microarchitecture. For your profile (420M tokens/month is significantly above that), Dedicated will likely lower unit cost once we size the pool correctly.
- Private VPC/on-prem: recommended when data residency, audit logs, or strict network isolation are non-negotiable. It carries additional setup and infra costs (network egress, customer-managed KMS/HSM) and typically requires a longer onboarding window.

3) Commitment/terms for Dedicated pilot:
- We can do a 3-month pilot with a discounted committed pool (30–50% discount off committed pricing) to validate performance/cost; requires a simple MSA addendum and a purchase order for the first month. If procurement needs a shorter commercial instrument we can do a trial credit on Hosted while we finalize Dedicated terms.

Attached is a starter workbook with two tabs: Hosted-vs-Reserved (uses your quoted token numbers) and Dedicated-sizing (maps GPU counts to expected sustained tokens/QPS). The workbook lists assumed model family and quantization that drive GPU throughput — we’ll refine after Vivek reviews your latency/throughput goals.

Next steps I propose:
- You confirm the token averages and whether the 1.2M messages is counting user-only messages or includes system-initiated messages.
- I’ll book a 30-min call between your infra owner + Vivek to review latency and sizing details (Vivek can propose the recommended Dedicated SKU/throughput).
- Ben will share the pilot commercial outline and the mutual action plan doc.

If that works I’ll send the calendar invite and update the workbook based on any clarifications.

Best —
Aisha Rahman
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

Hi Lena — great to meet earlier. Quick recap + suggested next steps from our side on security/compliance so your security team can triage: 

- Ask list from ZenithCare: SOC 2 Type II report (most recent period), bridge letter if the new report is in progress, and temporary access to our trust portal for evidence download.
- We can start the Vendor Security Questionnaire (DSQ) — attaching a prefilled template to accelerate (please confirm if you want it in a different format).

Thanks Aditya — that was helpful. A couple notes from our security team: 

1) We need the last SOC 2 Type II report covering the 2026 period. If you already have a 2027/2028 report in progress, a signed bridge letter that covers control continuity until the new report is issued will work for initial procurement review.
2) Please provision trust portal access for our security mailbox (security@zenithcare.com) with download rights for artifacts.
3) We use a standard DSQ spreadsheet — can you send the completed template back and we’ll queue it for technical review?

Primary contact for the intake will be Samir Desai (copied). He’ll coordinate uploads to our internal tracker.

One ask: does Redwood have an S3 or SFTP fallback for evidence if the portal is not immediately accessible to us?

Appreciate the quick turnaround.
Lena

ZenithCare

- Jordan: can you provision a trust portal invite for security@zenithcare.com (download rights) and create an S3 presigned link as fallback? If portal invite will take >24 hours, please provide S3 fallback.

Thanks — want to get back to them by EOD Monday.
Aditya

[Attachment: Bridge_Letter_Redwood_Template.docx (docx)]

2) Trust portal: Jordan has provisioned a temporary invite for security@zenithcare.com — access link below. Credentials and MFA setup are sent in a separate secure message from our trust system. Portal link: https://trust.redwood.com/zenithcare/invite/ACCESS-9a8b7c

Thanks — looking forward to moving this along.

Aditya Rao

source.subject: Hosted API commit top-up + rateband and latency SLA request
messages:
From: Alicia Moreno <alicia@cartly.io>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-05-28T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Hosted API commit top-up & rateband adjustment

Hi Aditya / Ben,

Quick note from Cartly — over the last 6 weeks we've grown a lot faster than forecast and are hitting throttles and increased p95 latencies on the hosted endpoint (we're seeing spikes during both PST morning and late-EMEA evening windows). We reviewed our May usage (attached) and we'd like to discuss:

- Increasing our monthly commit from 1.2M tokens to 2.5M tokens (starting June billing period).
- Moving up one usage tier so we aren't subject to the current 60 req/min soft rate during peak windows — ideally a 120 req/min sustained cap with short burst allowance to 240 for 10m windows.
- Clarification on latency SLA at the new rateband (p95 target) and whether there's a temporary grace period while we migrate traffic.

We can sign an amendment to move to the higher commitment if we can get predictable rate limits and a short-term latency mitigation plan. Attached: usage_report_may.csv and a trimmed latency log (qos_latency_log.txt).

Can we set up a call this week? We are flexible on Thursday morning PST.

Thanks,
Alicia Moreno
Head of Platform - Cartly Inc.

To: Alicia Moreno <alicia@cartly.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.ai>

Subject: Re: Hosted API commit top-up & rateband adjustment

Hi Alicia — thanks for the heads up and for the attachments. Appreciate the detail.

Short answers up front: yes, we can support a higher monthly commit and a higher sustained rate tier; we typically model pricing in three components (base commit, sustained rate tier, burst capacity). We'll need a couple of specifics from your side to map to the right rateband and to give an SLA commitment:

1) Peak distribution: what % of traffic occurs in the two PST / EMEA windows you mentioned?
2) Are the spikes coming from retries or new user sessions?
3) Do you have any planned launch / marketing events in June that could further increase burst risk?

Kimberly, can you review Cartly's latency logs and propose an interim mitigation (routing/batching/priority lane) we can offer during the first 30 days after the amendment goes live?

Alicia — I can take a short call Thursday 08:30-09:30 PT. If that works I’ll send a calendar invite. Assuming the numbers look like the May report, I’ll draft an amendment with a stepped commit (2.5M month 1, 2.8M month 2) and an explicit burst window clause.

— Aditya

From: Alicia Moreno <alicia@cartly.io>

Subject: Re: Hosted API commit top-up & rateband adjustment

Thanks Aditya — appreciate the quick response.

Answers:
- ~58% of token volume happens in the PST morning window (07:00-10:30 PT), ~22% in the evening EMEA window (17:00-20:00 PT), the rest is distributed.
- Spikes are mostly new sessions as we rolled a small product change last month — retry rates are low (~1.2%).
- No major launches planned in June beyond some organic promotions.

We can share our traffic_profile.yaml if that helps with sizing. On the SLA question: we need a documented p95 latency target that is achievable with the stepped commit — even a 45-day mitigation window would be workable while we smooth traffic.

Alicia

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.ai>
To: Alicia Moreno <alicia@cartly.io>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-05-29T10:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Hosted API commit top-up & rateband adjustment

Hi Alicia — I dug into the logs and your traffic profile. A few technical notes and an interim plan: 

- Root cause: traffic concentration around session start is increasing KV cache misses and fragmentation across worker shards which raises p95.
- Short-term mitigation (30–45 days): enable request affinity (session pinning) + a temporary routing priority for Cartly to the higher-throughput pool during PST morning windows. This reduces cross-shard cache churn.
- Config: sustain 120 req/min with a 10-minute burst to 240 req/min, plus a soft backoff at 300 req/min enforced for 2 minutes. We can instrument a per-customer priority lane that gives you lower queueing latency for the commit period.

I recommend this config and we can include it in the amendment as an operational attachment (we'll list the exact routing rule ids). Note: priority lanes are part of the Dedicated offering by default, but we can provide a hosted priority lane as a short-term add-on while you ramp. Pricing impact below.

Estimated commercials (high level) — ballpark: +$18k/mo on top of existing commit for the higher sustained rate and the priority-lane add-on. Final numbers in the amendment.

If you approve the config I’ll prepare an ops runbook and the language to include in the 30–45 day mitigation clause.

— Kimberly Park
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
kimberly@redwood.ai

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Alicia Moreno <alicia@cartly.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-05-30T11:05:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Hosted API commit top-up & rateband adjustment — proposal and next steps

Alicia — thanks for the details and Kim's review. Proposal summary below (if this looks good I’ll kick off a DocuSign amendment):

- Commit: monthly committed tokens increase from 1.2M -> 2.5M starting 2028-06-01. Step increase: 2.5M month 1, 2.8M month 2, then flat.
- Rateband: move to Sustained-2 tier (supports 120 req/min sustained). Burst: 240 req/min for 10 minutes, emergency cap 300 req/min with soft backoff.
- SLA: target p95 <= 420ms for the Hosted API under the Sustained-2 rateband.
- Mitigation window: 45-day operational grace after amendment goes live. Redwood will enable session affinity + hosted priority lane during PST morning window. We will run joint telemetry for the first 14 days and review results.
- Commercials: incremental $18,000/month on top of existing commit; 12-month minimum term for the new commit. First invoice will be pro-rated for June.

Next steps:
1) Alicia — confirm you accept the configuration and the 45-day mitigation window.
2) We will create an amendment (DocuSign) with the exact service-level language and billing update (I’ll send the link to you and your procurement team).
3) Kim’s team will schedule the routing change and a pre-cutover test window.

If you confirm we’ll aim to have the amendment sent today and the call to schedule the cutover tomorrow morning.

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.ai

Attachment: amendment_draft.pdf (pdf)

source.subject: Adoption Symphony: MAP draft, owners & ops handoff
messages:
From: Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.com>
To: Elena Vega <elena@clearsight.ai>, Chris Holden <chris.holden@northbridge.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 2 May 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Adoption Symphony: MAP draft, owners & ops handoff

Hi Elena & Chris,

Thanks again for the time yesterday — great alignment on priorities. I promised a concise Mutual Action Plan (MAP) that lays out the milestones, owners, dates, and the immediate ops handoff so your team can schedule resources. Attached is v1 of the MAP (spreadsheet) and a draft SOW PDF.

High level asks from our side:
- Please review milestone dates in the MAP and flag any conflicts by EOD Friday.
- Confirm the list of up-to-3 kickoff participants from Clearsight (PM, Dev lead, Security lead).
- Approve the proposed go-live window (currently targeting 2028-09-15 to 2028-09-22).

On the Redwood side: Ben will lead the technical delivery, Monica will be your CS lead for adoption cadence, and Marissa will manage executive check-ins and billing coordination. After you sign off we’ll do a 60-minute kickoff to lock the first two sprint objectives.

Proposed next step: 45–60 min kickoff week of May 8 — does May 10 or May 11 work for your team?

Attachments: Redwood-MAP-Clearsight-v1.xlsx (MAP), SOW-draft-Clearsight.pdf

Thanks,
Priyom
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
pri y om@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0132

From: Elena Vega <elena@clearsight.ai>
To: Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.com>
Cc: Chris Holden <chris.holden@northbridge.com>
Date: Tue, 2 May 2028 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Adoption Symphony: MAP draft, owners & ops handoff

Priyom — thanks for the clear doc. Quick notes after a fast pass:
- Overall timeline looks good but the go-live week overlapping with our internal product release (09/18) is risky. Can we nudge to target 09/25–09/29?
- I’ll confirm kickoff attendees by EOD Thursday (likely: myself, Mateo (platform lead), and Kara (security)).
- We will need a short appendix in the MAP for data retention controls and log export cadence. Can your team add one?

May 10 is tight for us (customer panels); May 11 looks OK after 2pm PT.

Appreciate it — attaching a calendar placeholder for May 11 2:30pm PT.

Best,
Elena Vega
Head of Product Operations | Clearsight AI
elena@clearsight.ai

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 2 May 2028 13:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Adoption Symphony: MAP draft, owners & ops handoff

Folks — quick internal sync on the MAP items Elena flagged.

Notes / questions for the delivery plan:
- I’ll own the infra and integration milestones (agent onboarding, API keys, rate-limit configs).
- Need to know Clearsight’s preferred staging window for load tests (ask: two 2-hour windows during PST business hours).
- Monica — can you confirm adoption measurements we’ll track (DAU target, latency SLAs, and user satisfaction check-ins)?
- On security appendix: I can produce a log-export schedule (daily/weekly) but need clarity on retention length (30/90/180 days).

I’ll start a version-managed MAP sheet (internal column) and push an updated version by EOD tomorrow with the security appendix and a suggested slide for the kickoff.

-Ben
Principal Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com

From: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 3 May 2028 09:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Adoption Symphony: MAP draft, owners & ops handoff

Thanks Ben — quick update with commitments I captured after chatting with CS and Ops:

Updated milestone table (highlights):
- Kickoff: 2028-05-11 14:30 PT — owners: Priyom (AM), Monica (CS), Ben (SE), Elena (Clearsight PM)
- Sprint 0 (Infra + Access): 2028-05-15 to 2028-05-28 — owner: Ben (SE); deliverables: staging keys, schema mapping, prefix routing
- Sprint 1 (Core integration): 2028-06-01 to 2028-06-21 — owner: Ben (SE)/Clearsight Eng; deliverables: inference pipeline, tokenization parity tests
- Adoption pilots (alpha): 2028-07-10 to 2028-08-07 — owner: Monica (CS); deliverables: pilot cohort, UX feedback loop, metric dashboard
- Go/No-Go decision: 2028-09-01 — owners: Clearsight exec + Redwood execs (Marissa)
- Revised target go-live: 2028-09-25 to 2028-09-29 (per Clearsight request)

Security appendix: proposed retention options 30/90/180 days. Ben to indicate recommended default; Monica to capture Clearsight selection at kickoff.

I attached a short adoption checklist (one-pager) to the internal MAP tab. I’ll publish MAP v1.1 to the shared Drive and include a change log.

-Monica
Senior Customer Success Manager | Redwood Inference
monica@redwood.com

From: Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.com>
To: Elena Vega <elena@clearsight.ai>, Chris Holden <chris.holden@northbridge.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 4 May 2028 11:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Adoption Symphony: MAP draft, owners & ops handoff

Elena / Chris — attaching MAP v1.1 (internal change log + security appendix added) and the SOW draft with clarified milestones and acceptance criteria.

Key updates since v1:
- Kickoff confirmed for May 11, 14:30 PT (calendar invite sent).
- Go-live window moved to 09/25–09/29.
- Added security appendix and two recommended retention defaults: 90 days (recommended) and 180 days (enterprise option).
- Included a short ops runbook for incident triage and on-call rotation during pilots.

Links: MAP v1.1 (Drive): https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3cREDWOOD_MAP_CLEARSIGHT
HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/deal/67890

Please review SOW and MAP and reply with either A) sign-off on MAP + SOW, B) requested changes, or C) approval to route SOW to legal for a redline pass. If we don’t hear back by EOD Monday 5/8 we’ll assume the timeline is tentative and proceed with kickoff as planned but block the go-live week as tentative.

Thanks — looking forward to the kickoff.
Priyom
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
pri y om@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0132

Attachments: Redwood-MAP-Clearsight-v1.1.xlsx, SOW-draft-Clearsight.pdf

From: Elena Vega <elena@clearsight.ai>
To: Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.com>
Cc: Chris Holden <chris.holden@northbridge.com>
Date: Fri, 5 May 2028 16:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Adoption Symphony: MAP draft, owners & ops handoff

Thanks Priyom & team — confirmed.

- Kickoff: confirmed May 11 14:30 PT (we’ll join with Mateo and Kara).
- We accept the revised 09/25–09/29 go-live window, pending pilot results and the Go/No-Go on 2028-09-01.
- Security retention: we will default to 90 days; Kara will provide the final confirmation during kickoff.
- SOW: please route to our procurement (procurement@clearsight.ai). We want to take a single round of redlines before DocuSign.

Elena
Head of Product Operations | Clearsight AI
elena@clearsight.ai

Subject: Adoption Symphony: MAP draft, owners & ops handoff

Hi Elena & Chris,

Thanks again for the time yesterday — great alignment on priorities. I promised a concise Mutual Action Plan (MAP) that lays out the milestones, owners, dates, and the immediate ops handoff so your team can schedule resources. Attached is v1 of the MAP (spreadsheet) and a draft SOW PDF.

High level asks from our side:
- Please review milestone dates in the MAP and flag any conflicts by EOD Friday.
- Confirm the list of up-to-3 kickoff participants from Clearsight (PM, Dev lead, Security lead).
- Approve the proposed go-live window (currently targeting 2028-09-15 to 2028-09-22).

On the Redwood side: Ben will lead the technical delivery, Monica will be your CS lead for adoption cadence, and Marissa will manage executive check-ins and billing coordination. After you sign off we’ll do a 60-minute kickoff to lock the first two sprint objectives.

Proposed next step: 45–60 min kickoff week of May 8 — does May 10 or May 11 work for your team?

Attachments: Redwood-MAP-Clearsight-v1.xlsx (MAP), SOW-draft-Clearsight.pdf

Thanks,
Priyom

source.subject: Re: CommercialPilot post-demo — prompt ingest & eval roadmap
messages:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
To: Sam Patel <sam@commercialpilot.ai>, Kylie Morris <kylie@commercialpilot.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 20 Apr 2028 16:12:00 -0700
Subject: CommercialPilot demo recap + prompt ingestion request

Hi Sam / Kylie —

Thanks again for taking the time today — great conversation and a clear signal that CommercialPilot has a pragmatic production use case for automated contract summarization and clause extraction. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we keep momentum: 

1) Model fit & benchmarks (short list):
   - Candidate models to evaluate: redwood-llama-13b-chat-qat, mistral-instruct-7b, and redwood-quant-13b (FP16+8-bit expermental).
   - Benchmarks we plan to run: extraction precision/recall, hallucination rate on labeled clauses, and 99th-percentile latency on a 1k concurrent throughput profile.

2) Prompt / sample data request: 
   - Can you share a seed set of ~75–125 real user prompts/examples (examples of: "summarize clause X", "extract payment terms", and a handful of noisy OCR cases)? CSV is perfect (one prompt per row).
   - If any prompts include PII/PHI, please flag them or replace with <REDACTED>. If you prefer, send a separate file called sensitive_prompts.csv and we will treat it with stricter handling.

3) Redaction guidance & edge cases:
   - We typically ask customers to: (a) mark tokens that must be removed, (b) provide examples of heavily redacted outputs you accept, and (c) flag any legalese you want preserved verbatim.

4) Next milestones & timing:
   - You send seed prompts by Tue 2028-04-24.
   - We run an initial model-fit sweep and return top-line results + a short eval deck by Thu 2028-04-27.

Attachments: demo_highlights_0420.mp4 (clips from today), seed_prompts_20280420.csv (example template).

If that plan looks right I'll open a HubSpot task and assign an internal owner to the prompt ingestion.

Thanks — looking forward to the seed prompts.

—
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aisha_rahman@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0187

From: Sam Patel <sam@commercialpilot.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Kylie Morris <kylie@commercialpilot.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 20 Apr 2028 17:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: CommercialPilot demo recap + prompt ingestion request

Thanks Aisha — this is helpful and aligns with our expectations. A few quick items from our side: 

- We'll compile ~100 prompts from our support backlog (mix of clean and OCR/noisy documents). We'll strip direct customer names, but there will still be examples with account numbers and limited PII. If you prefer, we can send two files (public_prompts.csv + sensitive_prompts.csv).
- On redaction: for now we prefer that your process redacts account numbers and SSNs. Date ranges and currency amounts can stay if they're critical to the extract.
- Question: do you need original documents (PDFs) for OCR edge cases, or are text snippets from the OCR output sufficient for the first pass?

We should have the CSVs ready by Mon 2028-04-24 EOD. Thanks — Sam

Sam Patel
Head of ML Ops, CommercialPilot
sam@commercialpilot.ai

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 20 Apr 2028 18:05:00 -0700
Subject: Internal: CommercialPilot — eval plan & ingestion checklist

Aisha / team — plan to own the ingestion pipeline for CP. Notes / asks: 

- Prompt ingestion format: prefer CSV with columns: prompt_id, prompt_text, source_type (support/email/ocr), redaction_flag (yes/no), notes. Use seed_prompts_20280420.csv as template.
- If CP sends original PDFs for OCR edge cases, we will store them in a restricted bucket and run an OCR + noisy-token-normalization pass prior to evaluation. For first sweep text snippets suffice — ask them to include at least 20 noisy OCR samples.
- Evaluation harness: we'll run three model candidates with the following metrics: extraction F1, hallucination rate (manual sample), normalized latency @ p99, and token cost per 1k requests. I'll attach the draft eval-plan.md (attached).
- Model recommendations: start with redwood-llama-13b-chat-qat (best balance), fall back to mistral-instruct-7b for faster, cheaper runs. Try 8-bit quantized variant for cost tests.
- Ops: propose a 48-hour canary in Dedicated with kv-cache enabled and a small persistent cache for repeated prompts. Karthik, can you spin a canary image and get perf numbers?

I'll draft the HubSpot task and the evaluation deck outline tonight.

—Ben
Principal Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.ai

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
To: Sam Patel <sam@commercialpilot.ai>, Kylie Morris <kylie@commercialpilot.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 21 Apr 2028 09:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: CommercialPilot demo recap + prompt ingestion request

Hi Sam — thanks for the quick turnaround. 

Summary & confirmed next steps: 
- You: send two CSVs by Mon 2028-04-24 EOD (public_prompts.csv — ~80 rows, sensitive_prompts.csv — ~20 rows flagged). If you prefer to include PDFs for OCR samples, upload to the Drive folder linked below and mark filenames in the CSV.
- Us: Ben/Karthik will run the initial model-fit sweep (3 candidates) and return a short eval deck by Thu 2028-04-27. We'll call out redaction behavior and a small set of counter-examples to iterate on.

Links / tasks: HubSpot deal (https://app.hubspot.com/deal/CP-10824) — I created a task "Ingest prompt seeds" assigned to me. Drive folder: https://drive.redwood.ai/drive/folders/CommercialPilot-eval-0420

If anything changes on your timing just shout — otherwise we'll expect the CSVs Monday and will start the sweep on Tuesday morning.

Thanks again — talk soon.

—
Aisha
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aisha_rahman@redwood.ai

- You: send two CSVs by Mon 2028-04-24 EOD (public_prompts.csv — ~80 rows, sensitive_prompts.csv — ~20 rows flagged). If you prefer to include PDFs for OCR samples, upload to the Drive folder linked below and mark filenames in the CSV.
- Us: Ben/Karthik will run the initial model-fit sweep (3 candidates) and return a short eval deck by Thu 2028-04-27. We'll call out redaction behavior and a small set of counter-examples to iterate on.

Links / tasks: HubSpot deal (https://app.hubspot.com/deal/CP-10824) — I created a task "Ingest prompt seeds" assigned to me. Drive folder: https://drive.redwood.ai/drive/folders/CommercialPilot-eval-0420

If anything changes on your timing just shout — otherwise we'll expect the CSVs Monday and will start the sweep on Tuesday morning.

Thanks again — talk soon.

—
Aisha
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aisha_rahman@redwood.ai
text:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
To: Sam Patel <sam@commercialpilot.ai>, Kylie Morris <kylie@commercialpilot.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 20 Apr 2028 16:12:00 -0700
Subject: CommercialPilot demo recap + prompt ingestion request

Hi Sam / Kylie —

Thanks again for taking the time today — great conversation and a clear signal that CommercialPilot has a pragmatic production use case for automated contract summarization and clause extraction. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we keep momentum: 

1) Model fit & benchmarks (short list):
   - Candidate models to evaluate: redwood-llama-13b-chat-qat, mistral-instruct-7b, and redwood-quant-13b (FP16+8-bit expermental).
   - Benchmarks we plan to run: extraction precision/recall, hallucination rate on labeled clauses, and 99th-percentile latency on a 1k concurrent throughput profile.

2) Prompt / sample data request: 
   - Can you share a seed set of ~75–125 real user prompts/examples (examples of: \"summarize clause X\", \"extract payment terms\", and a handful of noisy OCR cases)? CSV is perfect (one prompt per row).
   - If any prompts include PII/PHI, please flag them or replace with <REDACTED>. If you prefer, send a separate file called sensitive_prompts.csv and we will treat it with stricter handling.

3) Redaction guidance & edge cases:
   - We typically ask customers to: (a) mark tokens that must be removed, (b) provide examples of heavily redacted outputs you accept, and (c) flag any legalese you want preserved verbatim.

4) Next milestones & timing:
   - You send seed prompts by Tue 2028-04-24.
   - We run an initial model-fit sweep and return top-line results + a short eval deck by Thu 2028-04-27.

Attachments: demo_highlights_0420.mp4 (clips from today), seed_prompts_20280420.csv (example template).

If that plan looks right I'll open a HubSpot task and assign an internal owner to the prompt ingestion.

Thanks — looking forward to the seed prompts.

—
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aisha_rahman@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0187


From: Sam Patel <sam@commercialpilot.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Kylie Morris <kylie@commercialpilot.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 20 Apr 2028 17:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: CommercialPilot demo recap + prompt ingestion request

Thanks Aisha — this is helpful and aligns with our expectations. A few quick items from our side: 

- We'll compile ~100 prompts from our support backlog (mix of clean and OCR/noisy documents). We'll strip direct customer names, but there will still be examples with account numbers and limited PII. If you prefer, we can send two files (public_prompts.csv + sensitive_prompts.csv).
- On redaction: for now we prefer that your process redacts account numbers and SSNs. Date ranges and currency amounts can stay if they're critical to the extract.
- Question: do you need original documents (PDFs) for OCR edge cases, or are text snippets from the OCR output sufficient for the first pass?

We should have the CSVs ready by Mon 2028-04-24 EOD. Thanks — Sam

Sam Patel
Head of ML Ops, CommercialPilot
sam@commercialpilot.ai


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 20 Apr 2028 18:05:00 -0700
Subject: Internal: CommercialPilot — eval plan & ingestion checklist

Aisha / team — plan to own the ingestion pipeline for CP. Notes / asks: 

- Prompt ingestion format: prefer CSV with columns: prompt_id, prompt_text, source_type (support/email/ocr), redaction_flag (yes/no), notes. Use seed_prompts_20280420.csv as template.
- If CP sends original PDFs for OCR edge cases, we will store them in a restricted bucket and run an OCR + noisy-token-normalization pass prior to evaluation. For first sweep text snippets suffice — ask them to include at least 20 noisy OCR samples.
- Evaluation harness: we'll run three model candidates with the following metrics: extraction F1, hallucination rate (manual sample), normalized latency @ p99, and token cost per 1k requests. I'll attach the draft eval-plan.md (attached).
- Model recommendations: start with redwood-llama-13b-chat-qat (best balance), fall back to mistral-instruct-7b for faster, cheaper runs. Try 8-bit quantized variant for cost tests.
- Ops: propose a 48-hour canary in Dedicated with kv-cache enabled and a small persistent cache for repeated prompts. Karthik, can you spin a canary image and get perf numbers?

I'll draft the HubSpot task and the evaluation deck outline tonight.

—Ben
Principal Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.ai

source.subject: Security discovery and vendor questionnaire — MedBridge private deployment options
messages:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Tom Ellis <tellis@medbridgehealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 09 Apr 2028 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Security discovery and vendor questionnaire — MedBridge private deployment options

Tom — thanks again for taking time today. Quick recap and proposed next steps from our side: 

Recap from the call
- Objective: qualify security posture and feasibility for a private/VPC or air-gapped deployment to host models that may process PHI.
- Questions outstanding: SOC 2 status and timeline, GDPR/HIPAA applicability, procurement/BAA timing, and preferred network isolation (VPC peering vs. physically air-gapped).

Immediate asks / next steps
1) I attached a short vendor questionnaire and a network diagram template (see attached). Please have your security or infra team fill the questionnaire and drop a diagram of your expected topology (firewalls, NAT, egress controls, data flow).
2) Tell us whether you consider MedBridge a covered entity for HIPAA or if you'll be a business associate — we need BAA requirements to surface on our side.
3) If air-gap is likely, flag which data classes and expected timelines; we can share an air-gap feasibility checklist.

Timing: if you can return the questionnaire + diagram in 3–5 business days that would be ideal — our security team (Ben and Sergio cc'd) will do a targeted review and come back with any clarifying asks. We'll also scope a short follow-up (30 min) to go over technical items.

Attachments: Vendor questionnaire (Excel), network diagram template (draw.io), short deployment checklist (PDF).

Thanks — I’ll follow up internally to queue security review.

—
Aisha Rahman
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
attach: [MedBridge_Vendor_Questionnaire_v1.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)]
From: Tom Ellis <tellis@medbridgehealth.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: MedBridge Security <security@medbridgehealth.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Apr 2028 11:33:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Security discovery and vendor questionnaire — MedBridge private deployment options

Hi Aisha — appreciated the clear rundown. Quick answers from our side to help you triage: 

- SOC 2: We are currently SOC 2 Type II in progress; control testing is underway with an expected attestation date of 2028-06-15. We can provide the auditor contact and interim control summaries.
- HIPAA/GDPR: We handle PHI for a subset of our customers and will require a BAA. GDPR applies to our EU users; we have data residency constraints for EU workloads.
- Deployment preference: For workloads that process identifiable PHI we prefer a private deployment inside our VPC with strict egress controls. For some small workloads we may consider an isolated air-gapped option — we need a feasibility checklist.

Security contact: 
- Name: Priya Kapoor (security@medbridgehealth.com) — she’ll take point on the questionnaire and network diagram. I’ve cc’d our security alias above.

I’ll aim to have the filled questionnaire and a basic network sketch back to you by Friday (Apr 13). If that slips, we’ll aim early next week.

Thanks,
Tom
Director of Product Operations | MedBridge Health

On Mon, Apr 9, 2028 at 10:12 AM Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com> wrote:
> Tom — thanks again for taking time today. Quick recap and proposed next steps from our side: 
> ...

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Tom Ellis <tellis@medbridgehealth.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: Sergio Alvarez <sergio@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Apr 2028 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Vendor questionnaire & network template (initial)

Tom / Aisha — filing the attachments we discussed. A few notes from Redwood security review checklist to help Priya fill the forms: 

Key items we’ll need completed in the questionnaire
- Data classification: which datasets will contain PHI vs. pseudonymized telemetry.
- Encryption: at-rest KMS (customer-managed key vs. Redwood-managed) and TLS termination points.
- Network egress: list of outbound IP ranges, allowed ports, any enterprise proxies.
- KV/cache persistence and retention periods (token/embedding caches).
- Audit & logging: required retention window for audit logs; do you need us to forward logs to your SIEM?

Air-gap specific notes
- If an air-gapped deployment is required we will need: a) physical environment specs (kvm/cabinet availability), b) offline image transfer method, and c) regular attestation windows for patching and model updates.

Attachments included (stubs):
- [MedBridge_Vendor_Questionnaire_v1.xlsx (Excel)]
- [redwood_private_deploy_network_template_v2.drawio (application/x-drawio)]
- [deployment_diagram_sketch.png (image/png)]

Can we schedule a 30-minute follow-up with Priya next week to walk the questionnaire? I have availability Wed 4/17 10–11am PT or Thu 4/18 1–3pm PT.

—
Ben Carter
Security Solutions Lead | Redwood Inference
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Tom Ellis <tellis@medbridgehealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Apr 2028 09:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Security discovery and vendor questionnaire — MedBridge private deployment options

Tom — great, thanks for the quick answers and for looping Priya in. Confirming next steps on our side: 

1) Priya/MedBridge: please return the completed questionnaire + a network sketch (can be a rough draw.io export) — we received the diagram sketch you uploaded to the ticket and will review it.
2) Redwood security (Ben, Sergio): we’ll do an initial pass and flag any high-priority blockers within 3 business days of receiving the packet. Expect clarifying questions about KMS and egress controls.
3) Scheduling: Ben proposed Wed 4/17 10–11am PT / Thu 4/18 1–3pm PT — Tom, can Priya make one of those windows? If not, propose 2 alternatives.

I’ve created a HubSpot task and attached the questionnaire link here: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/9832/company/medbridge-health — I’ll mark it as security-review pending.

Thanks — looking forward to the packet.

—
Aisha Rahman
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
> On Thu, Apr 11, 2028 at 09:02 AM Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com> wrote:
> > Tom / Aisha — filing the attachments we discussed. A few notes from Redwood security review checklist to help Priya fill the forms: 
> > ...

source.subject: Novasys AI — scaling snapshot & traffic profile request
messages:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
To: Samir Shah <samir@novasys.ai>, Lina Gomez <lina.gomez@novasys.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Mar 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Novasys AI — scaling snapshot & traffic profile request

Hi Samir / Lina —

Thanks again for the demo yesterday. To make the Dedicated proposal actionable we need a compact traffic profile from your side so we can map to GPU SKUs and provide a concrete concurrency/throughput plan. Can you confirm the items below and attach any representative logs or a short sample payload set?

Quick ask (should take 10–20 minutes):
- Estimated concurrent active users at peak (users making live requests that expect sub-500ms replies)
- Average and 95th percentile tokens generated per request (prompt + response)
- Typical request mix: classification / chat / generation / embedding (percentages)
- Traffic distribution by hour (simple 24h vector if possible)
- Any strict latency SLOs for particular endpoints (e.g., interactive chat <250ms)
- Security/compliance notes (do requests contain PII? must stay in VPC?)

I attached a one-page checklist to make this faster. If it's easier we can hop on a 30-minute call — Ben and I are available tomorrow morning.

Thanks,

— Aisha
Aisha Rahman
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
attached: novasys_traffic_sample.json (example payload), fleet-sizing-template.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Samir Shah <samir@novasys.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: Lina Gomez <lina.gomez@novasys.ai>
Date: Fri, 23 Mar 2028 10:05:12 -0700
Subject: Re: Novasys AI — scaling snapshot & traffic profile request

Hi Aisha —

Appreciate the clear list. Attaching a short sample and some ballpark numbers below. Lina and I pulled this from our staging logs (last 30 days) — cleansing applied.

Summary (ballpark):
- Peak concurrent active users: ~420 (sustained peaks ~10–15 minutes on marketing pushes)
- Average tokens per request: prompt 120 / response 380 (avg total ~500)
- 95th percentile tokens: ~1,100 (long-form summarization + code gen)
- Request mix: chat/generation 65%, classification 20%, embeddings 15%
- Traffic distribution: 60% of traffic between 09:00–18:00 PT, with a secondary peak 20:00–21:00 PT
- Latency SLOs: interactive chat <350ms preferred; batch inference endpoints are fine with higher latency
- Compliance: Some requests include customer-identifying metadata—Novasys requires VPC/private deployment for production.

source.subject: Demo follow-through: evaluation blueprint & next actions
messages:
From: Rohan Mehta <rohan@redwood.ai>
To: Alicia Park <apark@stellargas.com>, Tom Nguyen <tnguyen@stellargas.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 15 Mar 2028 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Demo follow-through: evaluation blueprint & next actions

Hi Alicia / Tom —

Thanks again for your time today — appreciated the candid discussion on where Stellargas is trying to get with conversational assistants for field ops. Quick recap from our demo and a proposed evaluation blueprint so your team can review and confirm.

What we demoed (short):
- Real-time command parsing and slot extraction from voice transcripts
- Structured output modes for downstream systems (JSON + strong typing)
- Latency contours and throughput with continuous batching enabled

Proposed success criteria (please confirm / tweak):
1) Latency: 95th percentile under 150ms for up to 512-token requests
2) Throughput: sustained 50 qps with 2x burst to 120 qps for short periods
3) Accuracy: intent+entity F1 >= 0.88 on your baseline test set
4) Integration: can write outputs to your S3 sink and call existing webhook within our auth flow

High-level POC plan (3 weeks):
- Week 0 (kickoff, 1 day): environment access, credentials, sample data handoff (owner: Rohan)
- Week 1 (ingest + smoke): baseline runs, initial tuning, validate end-to-end path (owner: Marissa)
- Week 2 (scale + regression): load tests, latency tuning, KV/cache gating, optionally try Private deployment path if needed (owner: Marissa + Ben)
- Week 3 (eval & decision): produce final metrics, short retro, next-step recommendations (owner: Rohan)

Open questions for you before we lock dates:
- Can you share a 1–2 hour sample set (or synthetic equivalent) and a preferred S3 path for ingest?
- Are there any redaction/PII requirements we need to enforce for the test data?
- Is on-prem / private deployment a hard requirement for the POC or an advanced option?

Attachments: proposed-poc-outline.pdf (PDF, 128KB) — includes timeline with checkpoints and sample eval scripts.

If this looks reasonable, can you confirm: (a) success criteria above, (b) tentative kickoff on Tue Mar 20, (c) primary technical contact for data handoff?

Thanks — I can circulate a one-page mutual action plan once you confirm.

Best,
Rohan Mehta
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
rohan@redwood.ai
mobile: +1 (415) 555-0132

From: Alicia Park <apark@stellargas.com>
To: Rohan Mehta <rohan@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Nguyen <tnguyen@stellargas.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Mar 2028 16:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Demo follow-through: evaluation blueprint & next actions

Thanks Rohan — great demo and succinct plan. A few quick clarifications from our side: 

- Success criteria tweak: throughput goal is 50 qps sustained but we need 95th percentile latency <= 150ms on payloads that average ~420 tokens (we have some longer transcripts occasionally). Can you confirm whether your numbers are with batching enabled?
- Accuracy target looks fine but we need to map your F1 to our intent taxonomy — I can share a mapping doc.
- On security: we are required to run a light security review before any data leaves our environment. Attached is our security questionnaire and a short list of must-have controls (network isolation, KMS/HSM support, audit logs).

We are interested in starting the POC. Tentative kickoff on Tue Mar 20 works; prefer an AM slot. For data handoff, Tom will own the S3 location and initial masking. Tom — can you share the S3 path and a sample file by EOD Monday?

Alicia Park
Head of AI, Stellargas
apark@stellargas.com

On Thu, Mar 15, 2028 at 3:02 PM Rohan Mehta <rohan@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Alicia / Tom —
> 
> Thanks again for your time today — appreciated the candid discussion on where Stellargas is trying to get with conversational assistants for field ops. Quick recap from our demo and a proposed evaluation blueprint so your team can review and confirm.
> 
> [message truncated for thread]

From: Rohan Mehta <rohan@redwood.ai>
To: Alicia Park <apark@stellargas.com>, Tom Nguyen <tnguyen@stellargas.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 15 Mar 2028 18:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Demo follow-through: evaluation blueprint & next actions

Appreciate the quick reply, Alicia — that's all helpful. A few commitments/follow-ups from our side: 

1) Batching/latency note: the numbers I showed included our continuous batching layer tuned for throughput with a 10–30ms extra tail in steady state. For 420–512 tokens we should still meet your 150ms 95th in our dedicated profile; we'll validate this in Week 1 with your sample set.

2) Security path: we can run the initial POC with sanitized samples in your S3 and do a Private deployment (VPC connect) in Week 2 if you want a deeper audit. We've reviewed the questionnaire at a high level and will return a completed copy by EOD Monday.

3) Updated mutual action plan: I've put together a short doc with owners and dates — link: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1a2b3c-MutualActionPlan (editable by your team). I've also attached an updated-poc-plan.xlsx with checkpoints and test vectors.

Action items — please:
- Tom: share S3 path + one masked sample file by EOD Mon (owner: Tom)
- Alicia: confirm AM kickoff preference and whether you want us to provision a Private env in Week 2 (owner: Alicia)
- Rohan/Marissa: we'll circulate an updated PoC plan and security answers by Mon end (owners: Rohan + Marissa)

Attachments: updated-poc-plan.xlsx (XLSX, 42KB)

I'll pencil in Tue Mar 20, 10:00–11:00 AM PT as a tentative kickoff — if that works I'll send a calendar invite and a short agenda.

Best,
Rohan Mehta
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
rohan@redwood.ai

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Rohan Mehta <rohan@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Alicia Park <apark@stellargas.com>
Date: Fri, 16 Mar 2028 10:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Demo follow-through: evaluation blueprint & next actions

Looping in from CS side — happy to own the infra/load validation during Week 2. Rohan / Marissa: I have availability Tue Mar 20, Wed Mar 21 afternoons for an initial infra sync (30–45 min). Alicia / Tom: can you suggest two 30-minute windows that work for your infra folks?

Quick note on capacity: given the 50 qps sustained target we should plan on a small dedicated pool for the load test (I can outline cost/instance count after I review your sample).

Ben Carter
Director, Customer Success | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.ai
phone: +1 (212) 555-0199

Subject: Demo follow-through: evaluation blueprint & next actions

Hi Alicia / Tom —

Thanks again for your time today — appreciated the candid discussion on where Stellargas is trying to get with conversational assistants for field ops. Quick recap from our demo and a proposed evaluation blueprint so your team can review and confirm.

What we demoed (short):
- Real-time command parsing and slot extraction from voice transcripts
- Structured output modes for downstream systems (JSON + strong typing)
- Latency contours and throughput with continuous batching enabled

Proposed success criteria (please confirm / tweak):
1) Latency: 95th percentile under 150ms for up to 512-token requests
2) Throughput: sustained 50 qps with 2x burst to 120 qps for short periods
3) Accuracy: intent+entity F1 >= 0.88 on your baseline test set
4) Integration: can write outputs to your S3 sink and call existing webhook within our auth flow

High-level POC plan (3 weeks):
- Week 0 (kickoff, 1 day): environment access, credentials, sample data handoff (owner: Rohan)
- Week 1 (ingest + smoke): baseline runs, initial tuning, validate end-to-end path (owner: Marissa)
- Week 2 (scale + regression): load tests, latency tuning, KV/cache gating, optionally try Private deployment path if needed (owner: Marissa + Ben)
- Week 3 (eval & decision): produce final metrics, short retro, next-step recommendations (owner: Rohan)

Open questions for you before we lock dates:
- Can you share a 1–2 hour sample set (or synthetic equivalent) and a preferred S3 path for ingest?
- Are there any redaction/PII requirements we need to enforce for the test data?
- Is on-prem / private deployment a hard requirement for the POC or an advanced option?

messages:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Mark Connors <mark.connors@sabletx.com>, Elaine Singh <elaine.singh@sabletx.com>
Date: Mon, 20 Feb 2028 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Handoff protocols & resourcing scenario for Sable Therapeutics private VPC pilot

Team / Mark / Elaine —

Thanks for the sync last week. Sable has confirmed they want a short pilot (6 weeks) in our EU region to validate the VPC/private-control-plane model before deciding on Dedicated capacity. They asked for a minimal-handoff protocol and a clear staffing scenario that shows who needs to be involved at each milestone (network setup, image validation, SOC evidence exchange, day-1 runbook).

I attached a first-pass ops_runbook_outline.pdf and staffing_scenarios.xlsx — please treat these as draft. My asks:
- Karthik: can you validate the network handshake steps and call out any nonstandard infra we need?
- Jonas: ownership for the control plane handoff (runbooks, monitoring, incident playbooks).
- Naomi: procurement/contracting checklist items to start (exceptions/PO timeline).
- Ben: CS/solutions lead for day-1 onboarding and acceptance tests.

Mark / Elaine — appreciate confirmation of the pilot scope below and any constraints we should account for (on-prem airgap? specific KMS/HSM?). If you can upload the Sable requirements doc I will reconcile with our ops outline.

Objective: produce a 1-page handoff protocol + a single staffing scenario with primary/backup owners by Friday (2028-02-24). If that timeline is tight, flag now and I'll push the ask.

Thanks,
Aisha
Aisha Rahman
Director, Product Operations | Redwood Inference
email: aisha_rahman@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0137

Attachment: ops_runbook_outline.pdf (attachment)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Mark Connors <mark.connors@sabletx.com>, Elaine Singh <elaine.singh@sabletx.com>
Date: Mon, 20 Feb 2028 13:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Handoff protocols & resourcing scenario for Sable Therapeutics private VPC pilot

Aisha — quick technical notes from my side. I sketched these into staffing_scenarios.xlsx under "Network/Infra assumptions" but calling out here as well: 

1) Connectivity model: Sable confirmed they want our standard VPC-peering path with private control plane (no internet egress for model traffic). If they require an air-gapped option we will need extra images and an offline sync process (adds ~2w to pilot).
2) KMS integration: if they want BYOK with their HSM, we need the KMS hooks enabled on the control plane side and a short security review. Timeline estimate: +1 week for config + SOC evidence update.
3) Image validation: we will need a small validation cluster (1 GPU) in their region to smoke-test model startup and KV cache behavior; Ops can run this but will need a dedicated engineer for the initial 72h window.

Open questions for Sable: which KMS provider, and are there any corporate IP allowlist constraints?

I flagged owners in the sheet: infra lead (Karthik) primary, infra backup (Jonas), ops onboarding (Ben).

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Senior Staff, Infrastructure | Redwood Inference

On Mon, Feb 20, 2028 at 09:12 Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team / Mark / Elaine —
>
> Thanks for the sync last week. Sable has confirmed they want a short pilot (6 weeks) in our EU region to validate the VPC/private-control-plane model before deciding on Dedicated capacity. They asked for a minimal-handoff protocol and a clear staffing scenario that shows who needs to be involved at each milestone (network setup, image validation, SOC evidence exchange, day-1 runbook).
>
> I attached a first-pass ops_runbook_outline.pdf and staffing_scenarios.xlsx — please treat these as draft. My asks:
> - Karthik: can you validate the network handshake steps and call out any nonstandard infra we need?
> - Jonas: ownership for the control plane handoff (runbooks, monitoring, incident playbooks).
> - Naomi: procurement/contracting checklist items to start (exceptions/PO timeline).
> - Ben: CS/solutions lead for day-1 onboarding and acceptance tests.
>
> Mark / Elaine — appreciate confirmation of the pilot scope below and any constraints we should account for (on-prem airgap? specific KMS/HSM?). If you can upload the Sable requirements doc I will reconcile with our ops outline.
>
> Objective: produce a 1-page handoff protocol + a single staffing scenario with primary/backup owners by Friday (2028-02-24). If that timeline is tight, flag now and I'll push the ask.
>
> Thanks,
> Aisha
> Attachment: ops_runbook_outline.pdf (attachment)

From: Mark Connors <mark.connors@sabletx.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@naomi_feldman@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Elaine Singh <elaine.singh@sabletx.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Feb 2028 10:28:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Handoff protocols & resourcing scenario for Sable Therapeutics private VPC pilot

Thanks, Aisha / team — good summary. A few clarifications from Sable: 

- KMS: we will be using KeySafe HSM (on-prem). BYOK required for all customer data keys. We can provide the onboarding paperwork this week.
- Airgap: not required for the pilot, but we do need to validate control-plane-only traffic over private peering; no public model endpoints.
- Timeline: target pilot start = 2028-03-11 (we need two weeks to finish internal procurement and security signoff). That pushes the pilot end to ~2028-04-22.
- Acceptance: we will run a short acceptance test suite (provided in sable_requirements_v1.docx). We expect Redwood to document the runbook and provide a single point-of-contact for the first 30 days.

We uploaded the requirements doc to your Drive folder and included a short note on pre-production constraints. Let us know if the revised pilot start works.

Regards,
Mark Connors
Head of IT Operations | Sable Therapeutics
mark.connors@sabletx.com

Attachment: sable_requirements_v1.docx (attachment)

From: Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Mark Connors <mark.connors@sabletx.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Feb 2028 10:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Handoff protocols & resourcing scenario for Sable Therapeutics private VPC pilot

All — consolidating next steps and owners for the handoff doc so Aisha can produce the 1-page protocol by EOD Friday: 

Milestone 0 (Pre-proc & security): Naomi to confirm PO window and list of contracting exceptions (owner: Naomi; backup: Aisha). Target done by 2028-03-01.
Milestone 1 (Network & KMS): Karthik primary, Jonas backup. Tasks: private peering validation, KMS BYOK hooks, allowlist verification. Estimate effort: 1.5 engineer-weeks total.
Milestone 2 (Image & smoke validation): Infra validation cluster run; owner: Karthik; ops support: Ben (first 72h).
Milestone 3 (Control plane handoff & runbook): Jonas to author runbook + monitoring hooks; Aisha to own acceptance checklist. Deliverable: ops_runbook_outline.pdf -> final runbook.
Milestone 4 (Pilot SLOs & acceptance): Ben to coordinate CS acceptance tests with Sable; Mark to provide acceptance test artifact.

Blockers / risks: KMS BYOK integration may require minor control-plane changes (risk +1 week). Procurement timing seems to be the gating path for the start date.

Attachments referenced: staffing_scenarios.xlsx (I updated owners column), ops_runbook_outline.pdf, sable_requirements_v1.docx.

Next: Aisha will stitch this into a one-pager and post to the Drive link. If anyone has an objection to owners/timeline, reply-all within 24h.

—Jonas
Jonas Weber
Principal Engineer, Runtime | Redwood Inference

On Tue, Feb 21, 2028 at 10:28 AM Mark Connors <mark.connors@sabletx.com> wrote:
> Thanks, Aisha / team — good summary. A few clarifications from Sable: 
>
> - KMS: we will be using KeySafe HSM (on-prem). BYOK required for all customer data keys. We can provide the onboarding paperwork this week.
> - Airgap: not required for the pilot, but we do need to validate control-plane-only traffic over private peering; no public model endpoints.
> - Timeline: target pilot start = 2028-03-11 (we need two weeks to finish internal procurement and security signoff). That pushes the pilot end to ~2028-04-22.
> - Acceptance: we will run a short acceptance test suite (provided in sable_requirements_v1.docx). We expect Redwood to document the runbook and provide a single point-of-contact for the first 30 days.
>
> Regards,
> Mark
> Attachment: sable_requirements_v1.docx (attachment)

Milestone 1 (Network & KMS): Karthik primary, Jonas backup. Tasks: private peering validation, KMS BYOK hooks, allowlist verification. Estimate effort: 1.5 engineer-weeks total.
Milestone 2 (Image & smoke validation): Infra validation cluster run; owner: Karthik; ops support: Ben (first 72h).
Milestone 3 (Control plane handoff & runbook): Jonas to author runbook + monitoring hooks; Aisha to own acceptance checklist. Deliverable: ops_runbook_outline.pdf -> final runbook.
Milestone 4 (Pilot SLOs & acceptance): Ben to coordinate CS acceptance tests with Sable; Mark to provide acceptance test artifact.

Blockers / risks: KMS BYOK integration may require minor control-plane changes (risk +1 week). Procurement timing seems to be the gating path for the start date.

Attachments referenced: staffing_scenarios.xlsx (I updated owners column), ops_runbook_outline.pdf, sable_requirements_v1.docx.

Next: Aisha will stitch this into a one-pager and post to the Drive link. If anyone has an objection to owners/timeline, reply-all within 24h.

—Jonas

Principal Engineer, Runtime | Redwood Inference

text:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Mark Connors <mark.connors@sabletx.com>, Elaine Singh <elaine.singh@sabletx.com>
Date: Mon, 20 Feb 2028 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Handoff protocols & resourcing scenario for Sable Therapeutics private VPC pilot

Team / Mark / Elaine —

Thanks for the sync last week. Sable has confirmed they want a short pilot (6 weeks) in our EU region to validate the VPC/private-control-plane model before deciding on Dedicated capacity. They asked for a minimal-handoff protocol and a clear staffing scenario that shows who needs to be involved at each milestone (network setup, image validation, SOC evidence exchange, day-1 runbook).

I attached a first-pass ops_runbook_outline.pdf and staffing_scenarios.xlsx — please treat these as draft. My asks:
- Karthik: can you validate the network handshake steps and call out any nonstandard infra we need?
- Jonas: ownership for the control plane handoff (runbooks, monitoring, incident playbooks).
- Naomi: procurement/contracting checklist items to start (exceptions/PO timeline).
- Ben: CS/solutions lead for day-1 onboarding and acceptance tests.

Mark / Elaine — appreciate confirmation of the pilot scope below and any constraints we should account for (on-prem airgap? specific KMS/HSM?). If you can upload the Sable requirements doc I will reconcile with our ops outline.

Objective: produce a 1-page handoff protocol + a single staffing scenario with primary/backup owners by Friday (2028-02-24). If that timeline is tight, flag now and I'll push the ask.

Thanks,
Aisha
Aisha Rahman
Director, Product Operations | Redwood Inference
email: aisha_rahman@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0137

Attachment: ops_runbook_outline.pdf (attachment)


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Mark Connors <mark.connors@sabletx.com>, Elaine Singh <elaine.singh@sabletx.com>
Date: Mon, 20 Feb 2028 13:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Handoff protocols & resourcing scenario for Sable Therapeutics private VPC pilot

Aisha — quick technical notes from my side. I sketched these into staffing_scenarios.xlsx under \"Network/Infra assumptions\" but calling out here as well: 

1) Connectivity model: Sable confirmed they want our standard VPC-peering path with private control plane (no internet egress for model traffic). If they require an air-gapped option we will need extra images and an offline sync process (adds ~2w to pilot).
2) KMS integration: if they want BYOK with their HSM, we need the KMS hooks enabled on the control plane side and a short security review. Timeline estimate: +1 week for config + SOC evidence update.
3) Image validation: we will need a small validation cluster (1 GPU) in their region to smoke-test model startup and KV cache behavior; Ops can run this but will need a dedicated engineer for the initial 72h window.

Open questions for Sable: which KMS provider, and are there any corporate IP allowlist constraints?

I flagged owners in the sheet: infra lead (Karthik) primary, infra backup (Jonas), ops onboarding (Ben).

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Senior Staff, Infrastructure | Redwood Inference
