From: Mark Connors <mark.connors@sabletx.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@naomi_feldman@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Elaine Singh <elaine.singh@sabletx.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Feb 2028 10:28:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Handoff protocols & resourcing scenario for Sable Therapeutics private VPC pilot

Thanks, Aisha / team — good summary. A few clarifications from Sable: 

- KMS: we will be using KeySafe HSM (on-prem). BYOK required for all customer data keys. We can provide the onboarding paperwork this week.
- Airgap: not required for the pilot, but we do need to validate control-plane-only traffic over private peering; no public model endpoints.
- Timeline: target pilot start = 2028-03-11 (we need two weeks to finish internal procurement and security signoff). That pushes the pilot end to ~2028-04-22.
- Acceptance: we will run a short acceptance test suite (provided in sable_requirements_v1.docx). We expect Redwood to document the runbook and provide a single point-of-contact for the first 30 days.

We uploaded the requirements doc to your Drive folder and included a short note on pre-production constraints. Let us know if the revised pilot start works.

Regards,
Mark Connors

mark.connors@sabletx.com

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Tim Ryan <tim.ryan@brightwell.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Feb 2028 10:02:00 -0500
Subject: MSA + Order Form — redlines + signature plan

Hi Tim —

Thanks for taking the time to review the draft MSA and order form. Attached are our consolidate redlines (tracked) and a short execution manifest outlining proposed signing order and artifact delivery:

- Attachment: MSA_Brightwell_redlines_20280214.docx (tracked changes)
- Attachment: Execution_Manifest_20280214.pdf

Open items we want to confirm before we prepare the DocuSign envelope:
1) Authorized signatory at Brightwell (name & title) for the Order Form vs. MSA — is it the same person?
2) Preferred signing order (procurement contact first, CFO, then Redwood exec is typical)
3) Whether Brightwell requires witness/notary for signature (usually not for SaaS)
4) Any required PO number to be placed on invoice/billing

If you can confirm #1-#4 and whether DocuSign is acceptable, we can prepare a clean-order form and send an envelope with the requested signing order. Aim to have final signatures by Thu 2/16 if that works on your end.

Thanks —
Alex Martinez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com | (415) 555-0123
[Attachment: MSA_Brightwell_redlines_20280214.docx (docx, 134 KB)]

From: Sarah Mills <contracts@brightwell.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>, Tim Ryan <tim.ryan@brightwell.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Feb 2028 14:18:32 -0500
Subject: Re: MSA + Order Form — redlines + signature plan

Alex / Tim —

We reviewed the tracked redlines and added inline comments. A couple of notes from our side:
- We have five substantive items in Section 6 (data handling) that our privacy team wants clarified; we'll return a marked-up copy by EOD Friday (2/18).
- For execution: Procurement (Tim) will acknowledge commercial terms, but the legal signature needs to come from our CFO, Denise Cooper (denise.cooper@brightwell.com).
- We are comfortable with DocuSign for signatures. Please ensure the envelope allows sequential signing and shows the final PDF to all signers.
- We do not require notarization or witness for contracts under $5M.
- Please add the Brightwell PO number BW-9421 to the invoice and the order form when you send the final clean copy.

Attached: Brightwell_comments_20280214.pdf (PDF, 46 KB) — includes a redline view and comment list.

Thanks,
Sarah Mills
Senior Counsel, Contracts | Brightwell Health
contracts@brightwell.com
[Attachment: Brightwell_comments_20280214.pdf (pdf, 46 KB)]

>

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Feb 2028 09:11:05 -0500
Subject: RE: MSA + Order Form — signature orchestration & final clean copy plan

Team — looping you in so we can lock execution logistics. Summary of Brightwell ask: sequential DocuSign, signers are Tim (procurement) then Denise Cooper (CFO). They require PO# BW-9421 on invoice. No notary required.

My proposal:
- Redwood prepares final clean MSA + Order Form (no tracked changes) and uploads native + flattened PDF to the envelope.
- DocuSign signing order: 1) Tim Ryan (procurement) 2) Denise Cooper (CFO, Brightwell) 3) Marcus Lin (SVP, Business Ops) — Marcus to sign for Redwood.
- Envelope will include one final "Acknowledgement" checkbox that Tim must check for procurement confirmation.
- After completion, we export the signed PDF and native Word clean copy and place both in the shared Drive folder and route to billing with the PO number.

Action items:
- Ben: confirm billing/PO handling and whether finance needs any additional invoice metadata.
- Laura: please confirm Marcus is authorized to sign the Order Form/MSA for Redwood, or advise if we need exec escalation for signature authority. Also confirm the acknowledgement text is legally acceptable.
- Priya: prep the clean native + PDF and upload to Drive link below; prepare the DocuSign envelope with the sequential routing.

Target timeline: I will draft and attach the clean copies today (2/15). Aim to have envelope out by 2/15 afternoon for signatures on 2/16.

Thanks —
Alex
[Attachment: Execution_Manifest_20280214.pdf (pdf, 39 KB)]

From: Tim Ryan <tim.ryan@brightwell.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sarah Mills <contracts@brightwell.com>, Denise Cooper <denise.cooper@brightwell.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Feb 2028 16:03:12 -0500
Subject: Re: MSA + Order Form — signature orchestration & final clean copy plan

Alex / Team —

All good on our end. Confirming:
- Signing order approved: Tim Ryan (procurement) -> Denise Cooper (CFO) -> Marcus Lin (Redwood)
- Denise has signing authority for the MSA/order form.
- DocuSign is fine; please set envelope to sequential and include the two files (native Word + flattened PDF).
- Please put BW-9421 on the invoice and the order form header.
- After Marcus signs, please share the signed PDF + native clean copy to contracts@brightwell.com and to our procurement inbox procurement@brightwell.com.

Please include a brief "who to contact" line in the envelope for post-exec questions (Ben Carter — ben.carter@redwoodinference.com).

We will watch for the envelope and aim to sign same day.

Thanks —
Tim Ryan
Director, Strategic Procurement | Brightwell Health
[DocuSign: https://app.docusign.com/envelope/status/ABCDEFG123456789]

source.subject: Quick follow-up: Redwood demo + deployment fit
messages:
From: Carmen Lee <carmen.lee@nextech.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir.patel@nextech.ai>
Date: 2027-12-05T16:12:00-08:00
Subject: Redwood demo follow-up + a few clarifying Qs

Thanks again for the demo today — quick note while things are fresh. NexTech's engineering lead (Samir) and I discussed the three deployment modes you outlined (Hosted API, Dedicated reserved capacity, Private VPC/on-prem). A few items we need clarified before we can lock in a short trial: 

- Expected QPS for a hosted eval: we want to simulate production burst patterns (10–20 qps sustained with spikes to 80 qps for 2–5 minutes). What rate limits and burst allowances would you expect during a hosted trial?
- Endpoint strategy: can we get separate endpoints per environment (staging/prod) and per model variant, or is routing done via API key + model param?
- Data handling: any persistence or logs kept by Redwood during hosted eval? We need confirmation on log retention and redaction options.
- Quickstart access: our infra team will need a simple runbook/endpoint list, and ideally a sandbox API key with predictable limits.

Also attaching our SOW template and a small network diagram showing that we may want Private later for compliance. Timeline: we want to run an initial hosted trial next week (start 2027-12-08) if we can get the sandbox access + ratecap agreed. Can you confirm?

Thanks,
Carmen
Carmen Lee
Director of Platform Product
NexTech Analytics

Attachment(s): NexTech_SOW_template.pdf, network_diagram.png

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
To: Carmen Lee <carmen.lee@nextech.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-12-05T18:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Redwood demo follow-up + a few clarifying Qs

Carmen — really appreciate the thoughtful write-up and the timeline. Quick recap and answers below; I also included Ben (AE) and Evelyn (Solutions) to scope the trial and Jordan (Support) for the sandbox setup.

Summary answers (high level):
- Hosted eval rate limits: default sandbox policy is 50 qps sustained per org with burst to 120 qps for short durations; we can temporarily raise to 200 qps for the trial window if you share expected traffic windows.
- Endpoints: we provide named endpoints per environment (staging/prod) in Hosted mode. You receive separate API keys scoped to each endpoint; model variant routing is via the endpoint configuration so you can pin a model or swap behind the same URL.
- Data handling: for Hosted evals we persist logs for 30 days by default; we can enable request redaction and restrict logs to metadata only for trial accounts on request (Evelyn will outline options).
- Quickstart: we will provision a sandbox org, two endpoints (staging/prod), and a trial API key. Expect a one-page runbook (auth, endpoints, typical cURL samples) and a checklist from us by EOD tomorrow.

Next steps I propose:
1) Carmen — confirm trial start date (you suggested 2027-12-08).
2) Samir — please share a short traffic profile (expected sustained qps, peak durations, average token length) for the trial period; that will let us tailor the temporary rate raise.
3) Ben/Evelyn/Jordan — I'll kick off provisioning once we have the traffic profile and Carmen's confirmation.

I attached a short quickstart checklist we use internally. If it's helpful, Ben can draft an estimated cost for the trial configuration (so procurement knows potential spend).

Best,
Aditya
Aditya Rao
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

Attachment(s): redwood-quickstart-checklist.docx

From: Samir Patel <samir.patel@nextech.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Carmen Lee <carmen.lee@nextech.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-12-06T09:48:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Redwood demo follow-up + a few clarifying Qs

Aditya/Carmen — quick traffic profile from our side (high level):
- Baseline staging: 12 qps, avg 18 tokens per request.
- Baseline prod expected ramp: 18–22 qps, avg 24 tokens.
- Burst pattern: occasional bursts up to ~75–90 qps for 3–6 minutes during batch inference windows.
- Latency target: p95 < 400ms for user-facing endpoints.
- Data notes: we will not send PHI during the trial but will want to confirm retention and redaction settings as Carmen noted.

We are happy to start the trial 2027-12-08. Please proceed with sandbox provisioning and temporary rate increase.

Thanks,
Samir
Samir Patel
Lead Software Engineer
NexTech Analytics

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
To: Carmen Lee <carmen.lee@nextech.ai>, Samir Patel <samir.patel@nextech.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-12-07T09:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Redwood demo follow-up + a few clarifying Qs

Great — thanks for the profile, Samir. Quick operational checklist and next steps (we'll act on these today):

- Provisioning: Jordan will create a NexTech sandbox org, two endpoints (staging / prod), and API keys. Action owner: Jordan. ETA: 2027-12-07 5pm PT.
- Rate limits: we will set the sandbox to 50 qps sustained with a temporary trial window rule to allow 200 qps bursts for 10-minute windows across 2027-12-08 to 2027-12-15. Action owner: Ben.
- Data & logs: Evelyn will send a short doc outlining the 30-day default retention, redaction toggles, and an opt-in to limit logs to metadata only for trial accounts. Action owner: Evelyn. ETA: EOD 2027-12-07.
- Runbook & test plan: Aditya to deliver a one-page runbook + sample curl commands and a minimal test plan for Samir's team.
- Procurement: Ben will include an estimated trial cost sheet and an optional quote for Dedicated capacity if NexTech decides to pursue reserved capacity after the trial.

If anything above doesn't match your expectations, call it out and we'll adjust. Carmen — can you confirm a single Slack or email point we should use for urgent trial issues? Also, please confirm whether NexTech wants the logs-redaction option enabled for the trial (yes/no).

Thanks — looking forward to the trial kickoff.

-Aditya

---
Aditya Rao
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Mobile: (415) 555-0198
Schedule time: https://cal.redwood.com/aditya/30

source.subject: Kestrel Health — log retention / proof-of-delete clarifications
messages:
From: Samir Patel <secops@kestrelhealth.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
Cc: Emily Zhou <emily.zhou@kestrelhealth.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Nov 2027 09:07:00 -0800
Subject: Initial: Log retention for tenant-scoped debug artifacts

Hi Aisha,

Thanks for the demo last week — Kestrel's infra team had one follow-up for procurement/security: for our dedicated/private deployment, we need clarity on which server-side logs and transient artifacts Redwood retains, for how long, and what a customer-facing "proof of deletion" looks like after a data purge request. Specifically: 

- Which classes of logs are tenant-scoped (i.e., include customer identifiers) vs. global/system traces?
- What's the default retention window for tenant-scoped inference traces and KV cache snapshots?
- For SOC 2 / SIG evidence we may be asked for a sample proof-of-delete or a play-by-play that shows deletion completed for a tenant — can you point us to what Redwood can provide (audit IDs, export of deletion job run, preserved manifests)?

Legal asked that any sample evidence be redacted and delivered via a secure portal. Can you confirm whether this is supported and who on your side we should coordinate with?

Appreciate a short reply or a proposed time for a 30-minute workshop with your security contact and our SecOps.

Regards,
Samir Patel
Security Operations
Kestrel Health

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <secops@kestrelhealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Nov 2027 11:15:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Initial: Log retention for tenant-scoped debug artifacts

Hi Samir — thanks for the clear list. Looping in Priyom (security) and Ben (account lead) so we can get you concrete artifacts and propose next steps. Short answers up front:

- Tenant-scoped logs: inference request metadata, routing headers, and debug traces tied to a tenant id are considered tenant-scoped. Global/system traces (node health, infra metrics) are not emitted with tenant IDs.
- Default retention: hosted API: 30 days for request traces (configurable); Dedicated/Private: defaults to 90 days but can be configured per-contract.
- Proof-of-delete: we can provide a deletion job manifest (job id + timestamps) and an audit record showing the purge completed. For private deployments we can run an on-site evidence collection with redaction.

We have a standard mapping matrix (SIG / CAIQ rows -> artifact types) that Priyom will attach. Ben — can you propose 2 windows for a 30-min workshop with Kestrel's SecOps?

Thanks,
Aisha
Account Executive — Redwood Inference

From: Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <secops@kestrelhealth.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Nov 2027 13:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Initial: Log retention for tenant-scoped debug artifacts

Samir / Aisha — attaching a compact mapping and a short description of artifacts we can produce. High level: 

1) Mapping (attached: kestrel_log_mapping_20271122.xlsx) — maps common SIG/CAIQ entries to Redwood artifacts (audit log, deletion manifest, retention policy config, export of redaction script).

2) Deletion artifacts we can deliver:
   - Deletion job manifest (job_id, start/end timestamps, operator id)
   - Storage tombstone / object lifecycle events where applicable (for S3-backed metadata)
   - Audit events showing the purge command and completion
   - Optional redacted sample of the deleted row set (if you want to validate contents)

3) For Dedicated / Private: we support either a self-service evidence export (customers pull from the evidence portal) or a secured delivery from Redwood. We will redact PII in any samples before delivery.

Notes / caveats:
- Proof-of-delete for ephemeral KV caches: we rotate and zero-out ephemeral caches; we can provide a hashed manifest confirming the zeroing operation, but not a raw dump of ephemeral memory.
- For customers on air-gapped/private installs, evidence collection may require a job run and signed handoff that Kestrel ops signs off on.

I'll attach the mapping now. If you want, we can cover the deletion manifest fields during the workshop and run through a sample that has all redactions applied.

--
Priyom Das
Security & Compliance, Redwood Inference

From: Samir Patel <secops@kestrelhealth.com>
To: Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.ai>, Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
Cc: Emily Zhou <emily.zhou@kestrelhealth.com>, Kestrel Health Legal <legal@kestrelhealth.com>
Date: Tue, 23 Nov 2027 08:40:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Initial: Log retention for tenant-scoped debug artifacts

Thanks Priyom — that mapping looks useful. A couple of follow-ups before we schedule: 

- For the deletion manifest, can you confirm the minimum fields we will see? Specifically: job_id, operator, bucket/path affected, object keys (hashed), start/end, and the per-tenant item count.
- For KV-cache zeroing: is the hashed manifest guaranteed stable across runs (i.e., we can compare a pre/post hash)? Or is there any non-determinism we should call out?
- Customer-specific exception: our Legal would like to know whether Kestrel can request a temporary retention extension for troubleshooting (30–90 days) and, if approved, how Redwood documents and enforces that exception for audit purposes. Who signs off on that exception and what artifact proves the exception existed?

If you can reply with the minimum manifest schema and the exception workflow, we'll confirm participants for the workshop (SecOps + Legal + procurement).

Samir

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <secops@kestrelhealth.com>
Cc: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 23 Nov 2027 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Initial: Log retention for tenant-scoped debug artifacts

Samir — appreciate the specific asks. Next steps I propose:

1) Workshop slots: Wed 11/24 11:00–11:30 PT or Thu 11/25 09:30–10:00 PT. Please pick one and we'll send a calendar invite.
2) Priyom will post the minimum manifest schema in the workshop invite and attach the redacted sample (sample_proof_of_delete.txt).
3) Exception workflow: Redwood documents any approved retention exception as a signed addendum (operator id + approval timestamp + scope + TTL). That addendum becomes part of the audit trail; we also add an "exception" flag in the per-tenant retention config and the audit events reflect the approval. I'll include our standard exception addendum template in the portal.

Delivery: we'll upload artifacts to the secure evidence portal (https://redwood-inference.app/evidence-portal/kstl-12345) after the workshop. Ben

--
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales — Redwood Inference
Mobile: +1 415-555-0192
(attachments referenced in thread: kestrel_log_mapping_20271122.xlsx, sample_proof_of_delete.txt)

Date: Mon, 22 Nov 2027 09:07:00 -0800
Subject: Initial: Log retention for tenant-scoped debug artifacts

Hi Aisha,

Thanks for the demo last week — Kestrel's infra team had one follow-up for procurement/security: for our dedicated/private deployment, we need clarity on which server-side logs and transient artifacts Redwood retains, for how long, and what a customer-facing "proof of deletion" looks like after a data purge request. Specifically: 

- Which classes of logs are tenant-scoped (i.e., include customer identifiers) vs. global/system traces?
- What's the default retention window for tenant-scoped inference traces and KV cache snapshots?
- For SOC 2 / SIG evidence we may be asked for a sample proof-of-delete or a play-by-play that shows deletion completed for a tenant — can you point us to what Redwood can provide (audit IDs, export of deletion job run, preserved manifests)?

Legal asked that any sample evidence be redacted and delivered via a secure portal. Can you confirm whether this is supported and who on your side we should coordinate with?

Appreciate a short reply or a proposed time for a 30-minute workshop with your security contact and our SecOps.

Regards,
Samir Patel
Security Operations
Kestrel Health



From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <secops@kestrelhealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Nov 2027 11:15:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Initial: Log retention for tenant-scoped debug artifacts

Hi Samir — thanks for the clear list. Looping in Priyom (security) and Ben (account lead) so we can get you concrete artifacts and propose next steps. Short answers up front:

- Tenant-scoped logs: inference request metadata, routing headers, and debug traces tied to a tenant id are considered tenant-scoped. Global/system traces (node health, infra metrics) are not emitted with tenant IDs.
- Default retention: hosted API: 30 days for request traces (configurable); Dedicated/Private: defaults to 90 days but can be configured per-contract.
- Proof-of-delete: we can provide a deletion job manifest (job id + timestamps) and an audit record showing the purge completed. For private deployments we can run an on-site evidence collection with redaction.

We have a standard mapping matrix (SIG / CAIQ rows -> artifact types) that Priyom will attach. Ben — can you propose 2 windows for a 30-min workshop with Kestrel's SecOps?

Thanks,
Aisha
Account Executive — Redwood Inference


From: Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <secops@kestrelhealth.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Nov 2027 13:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Initial: Log retention for tenant-scoped debug artifacts

Samir / Aisha — attaching a compact mapping and a short description of artifacts we can produce. High level: 

1) Mapping (attached: kestrel_log_mapping_20271122.xlsx) — maps common SIG/CAIQ entries to Redwood artifacts (audit log, deletion manifest, retention policy config, export of redaction script).

2) Deletion artifacts we can deliver:
   - Deletion job manifest (job_id, start/end timestamps, operator id)
   - Storage tombstone / object lifecycle events where applicable (for S3-backed metadata)
   - Audit events showing the purge command and completion
   - Optional redacted sample of the deleted row set (if you want to validate contents)

3) For Dedicated / Private: we support either a self-service evidence export (customers pull from the evidence portal) or a secured delivery from Redwood. We will redact PII in any samples before delivery.

Notes / caveats:
- Proof-of-delete for ephemeral KV caches: we rotate and zero-out ephemeral caches; we can provide a hashed manifest confirming the zeroing operation, but not a raw dump of ephemeral memory.
- For customers on air-gapped/private installs, evidence collection may require a job run and signed handoff that Kestrel ops signs off on.

I'll attach the mapping now. If you want, we can cover the deletion manifest fields during the workshop and run through a sample that has all redactions applied.

--
Priyom Das
Security & Compliance, Redwood Inference


From: Samir Patel <secops@kestrelhealth.com>
To: Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.ai>, Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
Cc: Emily Zhou <emily.zhou@kestrelhealth.com>, Kestrel Health Legal <legal@kestrelhealth.com>
Date: Tue, 23 Nov 2027 08:40:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Initial: Log retention for tenant-scoped debug artifacts

Thanks Priyom — that mapping looks useful. A couple of follow-ups before we schedule: 

- For the deletion manifest, can you confirm the minimum fields we will see? Specifically: job_id, operator, bucket/path affected, object keys (hashed), start/end, and the per-tenant item count.
- For KV-cache zeroing: is the hashed manifest guaranteed stable across runs (i.e., we can compare a pre/post hash)? Or is there any non-determinism we should call out?
- Customer-specific exception: our Legal would like to know whether Kestrel can request a temporary retention extension for troubleshooting (30–90 days) and, if approved, how Redwood documents and enforces that exception for audit purposes. Who signs off on that exception and what artifact proves the exception existed?

If you can reply with the minimum manifest schema and the exception workflow, we'll confirm participants for the workshop (SecOps + Legal + procurement).

Samir



From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <secops@kestrelhealth.com>
Cc: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 23 Nov 2027 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Initial: Log retention for tenant-scoped debug artifacts

Samir — appreciate the specific asks. Next steps I propose:

1) Workshop slots: Wed 11/24 11:00–11:30 PT or Thu 11/25 09:30–10:00 PT. Please pick one and we'll send a calendar invite.
2) Priyom will post the minimum manifest schema in the workshop invite and attach the redacted sample (sample_proof_of_delete.txt).
3) Exception workflow: Redwood documents any approved retention exception as a signed addendum (operator id + approval timestamp + scope + TTL). That addendum becomes part of the audit trail; we also add an "exception" flag in the per-tenant retention config and the audit events reflect the approval. I'll include our standard exception addendum template in the portal.

Delivery: we'll upload artifacts to the secure evidence portal (https://redwood-inference.app/evidence-portal/kstl-12345) after the workshop. Ben

--
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales — Redwood Inference
Mobile: +1 415-555-0192


(attachments referenced in thread: kestrel_log_mapping_20271122.xlsx, sample_proof_of_delete.txt)

source.subject: Async job finalization SLA carveout — proposed service-level language
messages:
From: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@borealis-analytics.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: SRE Team <sre@borealis-analytics.com>, Procurement <procurement@borealis-analytics.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Nov 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Nightly async job SLA — completion window request

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for yesterday's call. As we discussed, Borealis runs a suite of nightly batch inference jobs (approx. 1.2M documents / night) that feed our downstream analytics pipelines. Our compliance and downstream SLAs require us to have these jobs finalized within a 6-hour window after job start; repeated late completions are a showstopper.

We understand Redwood's standard async SLA is oriented around best-effort completion with monitoring and credits for uptime, but we need a documented carveout/exception that: 1) guarantees job finalization for our "nightly" workload within 6 hours at P95 under normal utilization, 2) defines retry behavior and idempotency semantics so we can safely re-run parts of the job without duplication, and 3) clarifies monitoring/alerting and the credit remediation formula if the carveout is violated.

Can you send a proposed service-level amendment and a short operational runbook for our SREs (idempotency tokens, safe retry windows, and recommended client backoff behavior)? Procurement wants the clause to be explicit about credits (not just "commercially reasonable").

Appreciate any examples of how Redwood has handled similar carveouts for other enterprise customers.

Best,
Tom
Tom Ellis
Head of Data Platform — Borealis Analytics
--
Attachment: nightly-job-metrics.csv (stub, 1.2M rows)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@borealis-analytics.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 10 Nov 2027 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Nightly async job SLA — completion window request

Hi Tom — thanks for the clear ask and the metrics sample. I'm looping in Laura (Commercial Contracts) and Rachel (Product — SLOs) to draft an actionable carveout.

Quick clarifying q's for the SRE runbook:
- Job start signal: do you always kick these jobs from your side (single scheduled orchestrator) or do you rely on many independent triggers?
- Do you have an existing idempotency token per document or per job-run?

We'll assemble: a proposed amendment (SLA language + credit formula), a short runbook for safe retries and idempotency guidance, and a monitoring/alerting checklist. Expect a first draft by Monday.

Cheers,
Aditya Rao
Sr. Account Executive — Redwood Inference
--
Attachment: none

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 11 Nov 2027 08:30:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Nightly async job SLA — carveout language (internal draft)

Folks — drafting language and commercial remedy. Draft below for review; I'll clean up into a tracked Word doc for procurement.

----- Forwarded message -----
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 11 Nov 2027 08:28:00 -0800
Subject: Proposed SLA carveout for nightly async job finalization

Proposed clause (summary):
1) Service-level objective: For the scheduled "nightly-batch" job (document count ~1.2M), Redwood will target job finalization within six (6) hours from job start at a P95 level, measured over calendar-month rolling windows, provided the customer adheres to the configuration and preconditions in Section 2.

2) Preconditions and operational controls: The following must be true for the target to apply:
   - Customer triggers the job via a single orchestrator endpoint and includes a consistent run_id in the request payload.
   - Customer attaches per-document idempotency_token (UUID) for any re-submitted item; for partial replays, the same idempotency_token must be used.
   - Requests use the documented chunking (<= 10k documents per RPC) and recommended concurrency profile; Redwood will provide a batch adapter config.

3) Retry & idempotency behavior:
   - Redwood will attempt server-side retries for transient infra errors up to 3 attempts with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s) for RPC-level failures.
   - Customer-driven retries are supported; Redwood guarantees idempotent processing provided idempotency_token rules above are followed. Duplicate detection window: 72 hours after initial processing.

4) Monitoring, reporting & remediation:
   - Redwood will publish a per-run completion timestamp to the customer's S3/event sink and emit an alert when in-progress jobs exceed 5 hours.
   - Credit formula: if monthly P95 > 6 hours for the agreed nightly job, Redwood will provide a service credit equal to 5% of that month’s invoice for each additional 6-hour violation bracket, capped at 30% for the month. Exceptions for force majeure/maintenance are excluded when notified per the maintenance window policy.

5) Operational runbook (high level):
   - Detect: alert at 5 hours in-progress; collect trace id and run_id.
   - Contain: if the issue is due to backpressure, automatically escalate to Redwood Dedicated pool autoscale policy; if persistent, trigger fallback to lower-cost model variant (per agreed fallbacks).
   - Recover: re-run job segments using idempotency_token; reconcile downstream offsets.

Please flag any legal/commercial issues; Ben, any redlines on credits? Rachel — please confirm the feasibility of the 6-hour P95 target under a 1.2M document night on N4 GPU pool profile.

Laura
VP, Commercial Contracts

From: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 12 Nov 2027 11:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Proposed SLA carveout for nightly async job finalization

Laura — reviewed. Operational notes from Product/Infra:
- Feasibility: 6-hour P95 is reasonable provided the job follows the preconditions. On our N4 pool w/ recommended chunk sizes (8k docs/RPC) and a small prefix cache for repeated segments, we've seen similar customers hit ~4.2h P95. We'll need a short onboarding window to validate real-world characteristics (two trial nights).
- Idempotency: we support a per-item idempotency_token; caution that if customers submit different tokens for retried items, dedupe cannot be guaranteed. We'll include a lightweight client-side helper lib to sign tokens and ensure consistent run_id propagation.
- Retries: server-side automatic retries will not re-enqueue long-running downstream tasks; those are marked FAILED and surfaced to the re-run toolkit. We will document the exact failure codes and recommended mitigation mapping.

Operational asks for the customer SRE: ensure a single job-run_id and provide a small sample of the largest documents (top 1% by size) — those can skew P95.

I'll attach a short tech spec and the sample batch adapter config.

Rachel Kim
Principal Product Manager, Redwood Inference
--
Attachment: batch-retry-idempotency-spec.md (stub)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@borealis-analytics.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Procurement <procurement@borealis-analytics.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Nov 2027 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Nightly async job SLA — completion window request

Tom — thanks for the patience. Attached are: 1) a clean service-level amendment PDF (redwood-batch-sla-carveout-20271115.pdf) with the clause Laura drafted, 2) a short technical runbook and the adapter config (batch-retry-idempotency-spec.md), and 3) a draft contract amendment for your procurement team to review.

High-level summary of the ask and Redwood's offer:
- SLO: 6-hour finalization at P95 (monthly rolling) for the nightly-batch job given the stated preconditions.
- Idempotency & retries: per-document idempotency_token required for safe partial replays; server-side retries for transient RPC failures (3 attempts). Duplicate detection window 72 hours.
- Monitoring/remediation: alert at 5 hours; service credit formula (5% per 6-hour bracket over threshold, capped at 30%/month) as drafted.
- Next steps: if this is acceptable, procurement can sign the amendment and we will schedule two onboarding validation nights (non-production first) to observe P95 characteristics and finalize the production cutover.

Would you like our SRE partner to do a joint runbook session with your SREs this week?

Best,
Aditya Rao
Sr. Account Executive — Redwood Inference
--
Attachments: redwood-batch-sla-carveout-20271115.pdf; draft-contract-amendment.docx; batch-retry-idempotency-spec.md

- Retries: server-side automatic retries will not re-enqueue long-running downstream tasks; those are marked FAILED and surfaced to the re-run toolkit. We will document the exact failure codes and recommended mitigation mapping.

Operational asks for the customer SRE: ensure a single job-run_id and provide a small sample of the largest documents (top 1% by size) — those can skew P95.

I'll attach a short tech spec and the sample batch adapter config.

Rachel Kim
Principal Product Manager, Redwood Inference
--
Attachment: batch-retry-idempotency-spec.md (stub)


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@borealis-analytics.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Procurement <procurement@borealis-analytics.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Nov 2027 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Nightly async job SLA — completion window request

Tom — thanks for the patience. Attached are: 1) a clean service-level amendment PDF (redwood-batch-sla-carveout-20271115.pdf) with the clause Laura drafted, 2) a short technical runbook and the adapter config (batch-retry-idempotency-spec.md), and 3) a draft contract amendment for your procurement team to review.

High-level summary of the ask and Redwood's offer:
- SLO: 6-hour finalization at P95 (monthly rolling) for the nightly-batch job given the stated preconditions.
- Idempotency & retries: per-document idempotency_token required for safe partial replays; server-side retries for transient RPC failures (3 attempts). Duplicate detection window 72 hours.
- Monitoring/remediation: alert at 5 hours; service credit formula (5% per 6-hour bracket over threshold, capped at 30%/month) as drafted.
- Next steps: if this is acceptable, procurement can sign the amendment and we will schedule two onboarding validation nights (non-production first) to observe P95 characteristics and finalize the production cutover.

Would you like our SRE partner to do a joint runbook session with your SREs this week?

Best,
Aditya Rao
Sr. Account Executive — Redwood Inference
--
Attachments: redwood-batch-sla-carveout-20271115.pdf; draft-contract-amendment.docx; batch-retry-idempotency-spec.md
text:
From: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@borealis-analytics.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: SRE Team <sre@borealis-analytics.com>, Procurement <procurement@borealis-analytics.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Nov 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Nightly async job SLA — completion window request

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for yesterday's call. As we discussed, Borealis runs a suite of nightly batch inference jobs (approx. 1.2M documents / night) that feed our downstream analytics pipelines. Our compliance and downstream SLAs require us to have these jobs finalized within a 6-hour window after job start; repeated late completions are a showstopper.

We understand Redwood's standard async SLA is oriented around best-effort completion with monitoring and credits for uptime, but we need a documented carveout/exception that: 1) guarantees job finalization for our \"nightly\" workload within 6 hours at P95 under normal utilization, 2) defines retry behavior and idempotency semantics so we can safely re-run parts of the job without duplication, and 3) clarifies monitoring/alerting and the credit remediation formula if the carveout is violated.

Can you send a proposed service-level amendment and a short operational runbook for our SREs (idempotency tokens, safe retry windows, and recommended client backoff behavior)? Procurement wants the clause to be explicit about credits (not just \"commercially reasonable\").

Appreciate any examples of how Redwood has handled similar carveouts for other enterprise customers.

Best,
Tom
Tom Ellis
Head of Data Platform — Borealis Analytics
--
Attachment: nightly-job-metrics.csv (stub, 1.2M rows)


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@borealis-analytics.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 10 Nov 2027 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Nightly async job SLA — completion window request

Hi Tom — thanks for the clear ask and the metrics sample. I'm looping in Laura (Commercial Contracts) and Rachel (Product — SLOs) to draft an actionable carveout.

Quick clarifying q's for the SRE runbook:
- Job start signal: do you always kick these jobs from your side (single scheduled orchestrator) or do you rely on many independent triggers?
- Do you have an existing idempotency token per document or per job-run?

We'll assemble: a proposed amendment (SLA language + credit formula), a short runbook for safe retries and idempotency guidance, and a monitoring/alerting checklist. Expect a first draft by Monday.

Cheers,
Aditya Rao
Sr. Account Executive — Redwood Inference
--
Attachment: none


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 11 Nov 2027 08:30:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Nightly async job SLA — carveout language (internal draft)

Folks — drafting language and commercial remedy. Draft below for review; I'll clean up into a tracked Word doc for procurement.

----- Forwarded message -----
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 11 Nov 2027 08:28:00 -0800
Subject: Proposed SLA carveout for nightly async job finalization

Proposed clause (summary):
1) Service-level objective: For the scheduled \"nightly-batch\" job (document count ~1.2M), Redwood will target job finalization within six (6) hours from job start at a P95 level, measured over calendar-month rolling windows, provided the customer adheres to the configuration and preconditions in Section 2.

2) Preconditions and operational controls: The following must be true for the target to apply:
   - Customer triggers the job via a single orchestrator endpoint and includes a consistent run_id in the request payload.
   - Customer attaches per-document idempotency_token (UUID) for any re-submitted item; for partial replays, the same idempotency_token must be used.
   - Requests use the documented chunking (<= 10k documents per RPC) and recommended concurrency profile; Redwood will provide a batch adapter config.

3) Retry & idempotency behavior:
   - Redwood will attempt server-side retries for transient infra errors up to 3 attempts with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s) for RPC-level failures.
   - Customer-driven retries are supported; Redwood guarantees idempotent processing provided idempotency_token rules above are followed. Duplicate detection window: 72 hours after initial processing.

4) Monitoring, reporting & remediation:
   - Redwood will publish a per-run completion timestamp to the customer's S3/event sink and emit an alert when in-progress jobs exceed 5 hours.
   - Credit formula: if monthly P95 > 6 hours for the agreed nightly job, Redwood will provide a service credit equal to 5% of that month’s invoice for each additional 6-hour violation bracket, capped at 30% for the month. Exceptions for force majeure/maintenance are excluded when notified per the maintenance window policy.

5) Operational runbook (high level):
   - Detect: alert at 5 hours in-progress; collect trace id and run_id.
   - Contain: if the issue is due to backpressure, automatically escalate to Redwood Dedicated pool autoscale policy; if persistent, trigger fallback to lower-cost model variant (per agreed fallbacks).
   - Recover: re-run job segments using idempotency_token; reconcile downstream offsets.

Please flag any legal/commercial issues; Ben, any redlines on credits? Rachel — please confirm the feasibility of the 6-hour P95 target under a 1.2M document night on N4 GPU pool profile.

Laura
VP, Commercial Contracts


From: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 12 Nov 2027 11:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Proposed SLA carveout for nightly async job finalization

Laura — reviewed. Operational notes from Product/Infra:
- Feasibility: 6-hour P95 is reasonable provided the job follows the preconditions. On our N4 pool w/ recommended chunk sizes (8k docs/RPC) and a small prefix cache for repeated segments, we've seen similar customers hit ~4.2h P95. We'll need a short onboarding window to validate real-world characteristics (two trial nights).
- Idempotency: we support a per-item idempotency_token; caution that if customers submit different tokens for retried items, dedupe cannot be guaranteed. We'll include a lightweight client-side helper lib to sign tokens and ensure consistent run_id propagation.
- Retries: server-side automatic retries will not re-enqueue long-running downstream tasks; those are marked FAILED and surfaced to the re-run toolkit. We will document the exact failure codes and recommended mitigation mapping.

Operational asks for the customer SRE: ensure a single job-run_id and provide a small sample of the largest documents (top 1% by size) — those can skew P95.

I'll attach a short tech spec and the sample batch adapter config.

Rachel Kim
Principal Product Manager, Redwood Inference
--

Tom — thanks for the patience. Attached are: 1) a clean service-level amendment PDF (redwood-batch-sla-carveout-20271115.pdf) with the clause Laura drafted, 2) a short technical runbook and the adapter config (batch-retry-idempotency-spec.md), and 3) a draft contract amendment for your procurement team to review.

- Idempotency & retries: per-document idempotency_token required for safe partial replays; server-side retries for transient RPC failures (3 attempts). Duplicate detection window 72 hours.
- Monitoring/remediation: alert at 5 hours; service credit formula (5% per 6-hour bracket over threshold, capped at 30%/month) as drafted.

source.subject: Weekly GPU orchestration: pre-close sizing request for Acme Robotics
messages:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Julie Hart <julie.hart@acme-robotics.com>
Date: Fri, 09 Oct 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Weekly GPU orchestration: pre-close sizing request for Acme Robotics

Team —

Bringing the weekly fleet planning item over for the Acme pipeline that Julie flagged. They’ve moved their pilot to a POC costing call and finance asked for a committed slot proposal backed by a sizing note; procurement wants an indicative monthly reserve commitment range so they can budget for Q4.

Quick summary of asks from Acme (see attached forecast sheet):
- Sept–Nov pilot burn: mostly p99 short jobs, peak concurrency 22 inference workers; model base is a 13B quantized variant.
- Expected Q4 initial commit window: 4–8 dedicated slots (A100-equivalent) for steady throughput + burst for spikes.
- SOC2 evidence + audit log cadence requested by procurement.

What I need from you for tomorrow’s sales-finops-ops sync (Mon 10/11):
1) An ops-ready committed slot proposal: recommended slot size (vGPU/A100-equivalent), conservative headroom factor, and fallback (burst) plan.
2) Finance-facing unit economics: expected $/slot-hour and projected monthly token spend for the commit ranges in the sheet.
3) Any blockers for private-reserved deployment (networking/KMS) that would push them to Dedicated rather than Private.

I attached Acme_pipeline_forecast_v2.xlsx with their weekly concurrency numbers and SOC2 checklist stub. Please add notes inline; I’ll consolidate into the pre-close packet.

Thanks —
Aditya
Aditya Rao
Head of Strategic Accounts, Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0172

Attachments: Acme_pipeline_forecast_v2.xlsx (spreadsheet), soc2_evidence_request.pdf (doc)
From: Julie Hart <julie.hart@acme-robotics.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Acme Robotics Procurement <procure@acme-robotics.com>
Date: Fri, 09 Oct 2027 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Weekly GPU orchestration: pre-close sizing request for Acme Robotics

Aditya / team —

Thanks for the quick turnaround. A few clarifications from our side while you size:
- The 22 concurrent workers are a peak engineering-run figure; expected steady concurrency is 10–12 for most weekdays.
- We plan to roll the 13B model across ~6 internal services (web inference, batch inference, rerank) — latency SLOs vary: web inference needs <150ms p95; batch rerank is tolerant.
- Procurement asks: provide two commitment tiers (Conservative: 4 slots; Growth: 8 slots) and the monthly cancellation window and burst billing approach.

We can be flexible on a two-quarter upfront commit if you provide a predictable pricing band and clear burst policy. Also, we’ll need SOC2 evidence by the contract signature date (we can accept a statement of intent + timeline if final PDF isn’t ready).

Julie
Julie Hart
Director, ML Ops — Acme Robotics
julie.hart@acme-robotics.com
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Julie Hart <julie.hart@acme-robotics.com>
Date: Sat, 10 Oct 2027 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Weekly GPU orchestration: pre-close sizing request for Acme Robotics

Folks —

I pulled the attached forecast and ran a conservative sizing pass. High-level recommendation:
- Committed slot baseline: 4 A100-equivalent slots (each slot ~1x A100 40GB throughput profile). This covers steady concurrency (10–12) with a 1.5x headroom to absorb config variability.
- Growth tier: 8 slots if they commit to the two-quarter upfront; reduces $/slot-hour by ~18% in our model.
- Burst strategy: enable burst credits up to +50% (short-term) with per-token overage billing. Ops can implement automated scaling to move >90s requests to batch pool when web p95 spikes are detected.
- SLA/Fallback: recommend a soft canary for the first two weeks (pin model versions, enable KV prefix caching) and an automatic fallback to quantized-8bit variant if latency regressions >15% are observed.

Finance note for Ben: I estimated monthly compute spend at current pilot qps for the 4-slot baseline at ~ $72k/month (compute only) with the Growth tier at ~$118k/month before discounts. I left room for token-cost variance in the sheet.

Operational blockers: the customer asked for KMS integration for customer-managed keys — infra may need a short checklist on network peering and KMS proof-of-concept steps. Marissa, can you confirm network lead time for a VPC peering + KMS onboarding?

Laura
Laura Bennett
Head of Customer Success, Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.ai

> On Fri, Oct 9, 2027 at 09:12 AM Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai> wrote:
> > Team —
> > Bringing the weekly fleet planning item over for the Acme pipeline that Julie flagged...
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Julie Hart <julie.hart@acme-robotics.com>
Date: Mon, 11 Oct 2027 17:55:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Weekly GPU orchestration: pre-close sizing request for Acme Robotics

Thanks — solid notes. A few additions from FinOps:
- Pricing bands: conservative 4-slot commit = $68k/month compute estimate after standard commercial discount (I trimmed Laura’s buffer slightly), growth 8-slot = $102k/month. Final numbers will depend on token volume reconciliation but these are what I’ll present to procurement.
- Cancellation: standard 30-day notice for monthly reserved slots; for the two-quarter prepaid we can offer prorated credits if they convert to a different commitment on >=60 days notice.
- Burst billing: overage billed at on-demand per-token rate with a cap defined in the SLA (we typically cap at 2x on-demand for emergency bursts).

Marissa — if ops can confirm VPC peering + KMS onboarding in <=10 business days we can put the two-tier proposal in the pre-close packet and route to procurement Tuesday. Julie — can you confirm whether Acme prefers prepaid-2Q or monthly reserved to guide discount band?

I’ll assemble the deck for procurement with the two commitment tiers, breakouts, and an ops checklist.

Ben Carter
Director, Finance — Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

Attachments referenced: Acme_pipeline_forecast_v2.xlsx (updated with cost bands)

Subject: Re: Weekly GPU orchestration: pre-close sizing request for Acme Robotics

Folks —

I pulled the attached forecast and ran a conservative sizing pass. High-level recommendation:
- Committed slot baseline: 4 A100-equivalent slots (each slot ~1x A100 40GB throughput profile). This covers steady concurrency (10–12) with a 1.5x headroom to absorb config variability.
- Growth tier: 8 slots if they commit to the two-quarter upfront; reduces $/slot-hour by ~18% in our model.
- Burst strategy: enable burst credits up to +50% (short-term) with per-token overage billing. Ops can implement automated scaling to move >90s requests to batch pool when web p95 spikes are detected.
- SLA/Fallback: recommend a soft canary for the first two weeks (pin model versions, enable KV prefix caching) and an automatic fallback to quantized-8bit variant if latency regressions >15% are observed.

Finance note for Ben: I estimated monthly compute spend at current pilot qps for the 4-slot baseline at ~ $72k/month (compute only) with the Growth tier at ~$118k/month before discounts. I left room for token-cost variance in the sheet.

Operational blockers: the customer asked for KMS integration for customer-managed keys — infra may need a short checklist on network peering and KMS proof-of-concept steps. Marissa, can you confirm network lead time for a VPC peering + KMS onboarding?

Laura
Laura Bennett
Head of Customer Success, Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.ai

> On Fri, Oct 9, 2027 at 09:12 AM Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai> wrote:
> > Team —
> > Bringing the weekly fleet planning item over for the Acme pipeline that Julie flagged...


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Julie Hart <julie.hart@acme-robotics.com>
Date: Mon, 11 Oct 2027 17:55:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Weekly GPU orchestration: pre-close sizing request for Acme Robotics

Thanks — solid notes. A few additions from FinOps:
- Pricing bands: conservative 4-slot commit = $68k/month compute estimate after standard commercial discount (I trimmed Laura’s buffer slightly), growth 8-slot = $102k/month. Final numbers will depend on token volume reconciliation but these are what I’ll present to procurement.
- Cancellation: standard 30-day notice for monthly reserved slots; for the two-quarter prepaid we can offer prorated credits if they convert to a different commitment on >=60 days notice.
- Burst billing: overage billed at on-demand per-token rate with a cap defined in the SLA (we typically cap at 2x on-demand for emergency bursts).

Marissa — if ops can confirm VPC peering + KMS onboarding in <=10 business days we can put the two-tier proposal in the pre-close packet and route to procurement Tuesday. Julie — can you confirm whether Acme prefers prepaid-2Q or monthly reserved to guide discount band?

I’ll assemble the deck for procurement with the two commitment tiers, breakouts, and an ops checklist.

Ben Carter
Director, Finance — Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

Attachments referenced: Acme_pipeline_forecast_v2.xlsx (updated with cost bands)

source.subject: Inference network corridor and SLO alignment — post-demo follow-up
messages:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.inference>
To: Elena Park <elena.park@finapps.com>, Rajesh Kumar <rajesh.k@example-finapps.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 4 Oct 2027 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Inference network corridor and SLO alignment — post-demo follow-up

Hi Elena / Rajesh —

Great to meet you both on the demo earlier. As promised, this note captures the run-of-show items and the minimal network/perf evidence we need to qualify the private/VPC deployment for your streaming inference workload. High level summary and asks below.

Quick summary (goal):
- Target 95th pct tail latency <= 120ms for small prompt (up to 512 tokens) in steady-state streaming.
- Sustained throughput target: 400 concurrent streaming sessions with ~30 tokens/s average per session.
- Cold-start concurrency: support 75 cold starts/minute during burst windows without violating latency SLOs.

Immediate asks from FinApps (what we need to validate):
1) Network path diagram (ingress/egress NATs, any transit providers, VPN/peering endpoints) — prefer a simple CIDR + ASN list and egress IPs.
2) Baseline egress bandwidth and per-flow RTT measurements from your app tier to our proposed VPC endpoint (detailed ip/port below) during a 2-hour window.
3) Firewall and proxy behavior for long-lived streaming TCP connections (idle timeouts, keepalive policies).

Attached: a suggested network test plan and an SLO alignment matrix (redwood-inference-network-test-plan.xlsx, slo-alignment-matrix.pdf). The test plan includes iperf3 commands, REQ/RESP size profiles, and a lightweight synthetic streaming harness we can run from your staging app nodes.

Suggested timeline / next steps:
- You provide network diagram + egress CIDRs by EOD Wed (2027-10-06).
- We run a short 2-hour baseline test Thurs 2027-10-07 morning (08:00–10:00 PT) — Ben will coordinate.
- Follow-up sync (30m) to review results Thurs 2027-10-07 11:00 PT.

Ben and Karthik (cc'd) will own the measurement orchestration and will ask for ephemeral creds and an agent install on one staging host. If you have a preferred testing window other than the one proposed, please suggest.

Thanks — looking forward to validating the path and getting you onto a Private deployment prototype.

Cheers,
Aditya Rao
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.inference
+1 (415) 555-0183

Attachment stubs: redwood-inference-network-test-plan.xlsx (spreadsheet), slo-alignment-matrix.pdf (pdf)

From: Elena Park <elena.park@finapps.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.inference>, Rajesh Kumar <rajesh.k@example-finapps.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 4 Oct 2027 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Inference network corridor and SLO alignment — post-demo follow-up

Thanks Aditya —

Appreciate the clear checklist. Quick answers inline:
- We attached a high-level path diagram to our internal ticket (I pasted the Drive link below). Our egress IPs live behind an outbound NAT at 54.83.12.0/24; we use a transit provider (Tier2Transit) with ASN 64512 for cross-region traffic.
- We can run the baseline tests from our staging cluster (stg-app-*) but we have an HA proxy in front of the app nodes that will terminate long idle connections after 5 minutes; we can temporarily bump keepalives for the test.
- Morning Thu (10/7) is OK for a 08:00–10:00 PT window; Rajesh will coordinate a short maintenance window so we can adjust proxy timeouts.

Forwarding our internal note:

On Mon, Oct 4, 2027 at 14:05, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.inference> wrote:
> Great to meet you both on the demo earlier. As promised, this note captures the run-of-show items and the minimal network/perf evidence we need to qualify the private/VPC deployment for your streaming inference workload. High level summary and asks below.
> 
> [snip — test plan and SLOs attached]

Thanks,
Elena
Network Engineering Manager | FinApps
elena.park@finapps.com

Drive link (internal): https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aB-network-tests-finapps

Attachment stubs: finapps-egress-cidr-and-path.png (png)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.inference>, Elena Park <elena.park@finapps.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.inference>
Date: Thu, 7 Oct 2027 07:35:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Inference network corridor and SLO alignment — post-demo follow-up

Morning all —

Per plan: Ben here. We will run the baseline from our side and also ask you to run the staged client harness so we capture both directions. Quick measurement plan (ops checklist):
- Redwood agent (temporary) will be SSH-installed on stg-app-1 (we'll provide a tiny install script). No access to secrets, only networking probes and the streaming harness.
- iperf3: 5-minute runs at 10 parallel streams to establish bandwidth.
- RTT and per-packet jitter: pings and tcptraceroute from stg-app-* to the proposed VPC endpoint (34.210.77.55:443) at 1s cadence for 10 minutes pre-test, then every 10s during load.
- Streaming harness: simulate 400 concurrent sessions for 15 minutes with token emission profile matching the demo (30 tokens/s avg, 200–600 byte token payloads).
- Logs we'll collect: per-connection accept timestamp, per-message latency, kernel-level retransmits, and egress bytes by source IP.

Can you confirm the staging host fqdn(s) and if we should use a bastion IP? Also: please confirm if the proxy keepalive will be raised to >10 minutes during the test. If you can paste the NAT/egress IP list here we'll update the firewall allowlist.

I'll upload the raw measurement template to the Drive link and post results into the HubSpot deal record after the run.

-Ben
Ben Carter
Platform Performance Lead | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.inference

Attachment stubs: measurement-template.csv (csv)
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/FIN-2027-0732

From: Rajesh Kumar <rajesh.k@example-finapps.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.inference>
Cc: Elena Park <elena.park@finapps.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.inference>
Date: Thu, 7 Oct 2027 18:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Inference network corridor and SLO alignment — post-demo follow-up

Thanks Ben —

Staging hosts: stg-app-1.finapps.internal (10.10.11.21), stg-app-2.finapps.internal (10.10.11.22). Use our bastion at 54.83.12.7 for SSH into staging.
We will raise the HAProxy keepalive to 12 minutes for the maintenance window. Egress NAT block: 54.83.12.0/24 (pat to 54.83.12.7).

I'll add the test window to our calendar and will ping you when the bastion creds are live (short-lived key). Looking forward to the run — if the initial results look good we'll propose an A/B rollout plan for private deployment next week.

-Rajesh
Senior SRE | FinApps
rajesh.k@example-finapps.com

On Thu, Oct 7, 2027 at 07:35, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference> wrote:
> Morning all —
> 
> Per plan: Ben here. We will run the baseline from our side and also ask you to run the staged client harness so we capture both directions. Quick measurement plan (ops checklist):
> [snip]
> 
> -Ben

source.subject: StellarNet trial — scheduling demo and IT gating
messages:
From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>
To: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 13 Sep 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: New inbound trial — StellarNet (self-serve sign up)

Hi Grace / Ben —

Heads-up: StellarNet just converted from the self-serve trial and requested a walkthrough/demo. Lead contact is Caroline Thomas (caroline.thomas@stellarnet.com). They noted interest in the low-latency streaming use case and asked for an IT/security attendee to validate network/retention implications.

Key lead details (from signup):
- Company: StellarNet (US, finance infra)
- Contact: Caroline Thomas, Director of Product
- Trial plan: 14-day eval (token budget attached in HubSpot)

As discussed in SDR playbook, can we land a 45-min live demo next week with SE + AE + IT? I flagged this as high priority because they mentioned SOC/compliance gating. I attached the signup export and the customer's brief security checklist they uploaded.

Attachments: [StellarNet_signup_export.csv], [StellarNet_Security_Checklist.pdf] (forwarded by lead)

Next step: suggest 3 slots and I can send an intro "calendar invite with options". Grace — can you propose times in the thread? Thanks!

— Jasmine
Jasmine Liu | SDR, Redwood Inference
jasmine.liu@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0192

From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
To: Caroline Thomas <caroline.thomas@stellarnet.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 13 Sep 2027 11:00:00 -0700
Subject: Re: New inbound trial — StellarNet (self-serve sign up) — demo options

Hi Caroline —

Welcome and thanks for signing up — excited to get StellarNet onboarded. I'm the AE assigned and Ben (cc) will cover the technical walkthrough. Would love to schedule a 45-minute demo next week that includes your IT/security lead so we can review any network or data controls up front.

Proposed slots (all PT):
- Tue 9/14 — 10:00–10:45am PT (1:00–1:45pm ET)

source.subject: Graybridge: sudden p99 latency spike & errors — proposed KV rebalance
messages:
From: Eli Turner <eli.turner@graybridge.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: Graybridge SRE <sre@graybridge.com>
Date: Sun, 12 Sep 2027 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Re: API latency spike affecting ingestion pipeline

Hi Aisha —

We started seeing a sharp jump in p99 latency and a correlated rise in 500 responses for the embeddings route around 08:30 PDT. Throughput dropped by ~30% for a 15m window and then oscillated. Our job runs that depend on timely embeddings are backlogged and a downstream ingestion task failed.

Attached is a snapshot from our Grafana (metric_snapshot-20270912.png) and a short log excerpt. We rolled no code on our side in the window. Can you confirm this is on Redwood's side and give an ETA for mitigation? We will escalate internally if this continues.

Thanks,
Eli
Eli Turner
SRE Lead, Graybridge Solutions

Attachments: metric_snapshot-20270912.png (PNG, 86KB)
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
To: ops-incident@redwood.com, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Eli Turner <eli.turner@graybridge.com>
Date: Sun, 12 Sep 2027 09:28:44 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Graybridge: API latency spike affecting ingestion pipeline — urgent triage

Forwarding Graybridge SRE report — they have p99 spikes + 500s on embeddings. Eli says no changes on their side.

Ask: can infra/ops confirm whether we have a KV-store eviction pattern or hot-prefix activity that would cause HOL-style slowdown? If possible, propose a short mitigation (10-30m) we can apply that reduces downstream errors while we run a deeper fix. Please include timing and any customer-facing messaging. I have looped in Marissa for exec awareness.

Aisha
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
--- Forwarded message attached below ---
From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>, ops-incident@redwood.com
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Eli Turner <eli.turner@graybridge.com>
Date: Sun, 12 Sep 2027 09:43:11 -0700
Subject: Re: Graybridge: sudden p99 latency spike & errors — initial triage

Aisha / Eli —

Initial findings from a 09:30 check: multiple tenants were hitting a narrow set of KV prefixes due to a customer-side traffic pattern combined with our recent key-hash change (rolled 09/11). We are seeing rapid TTL churn and eviction storms on a subset of shards which is producing head-of-line queuing for downstream tokenization and batching paths. Symptoms match HOL blocking caused by cache churn rather than pure CPU saturation.

Immediate mitigations we can take (recommended order):
1) Apply an emergency KV rebalance for affected shards (non-disruptive). This spreads hot prefixes across more hosts and reduces eviction contention. Estimated 20m to complete and validate.
2) Temporarily relax prefix hashing affinity for Graybridge's tenant (route their traffic to a dedicated set of shards) — ~10m to flip routing, requires a short config push and circuit check.
3) Reduce batching window for embeddings for the next 30m to avoid large batch stall amplification (config-only, ~5m).

I attached a short runbook and a one-page rebalance plan. If we run 1+2 together we expect p99 to fall within their SLA within ~30 minutes. Risk: rebalance increases behind-the-scenes KV moveload; we will throttle the migration rate.

I will start the rebalance at T+10 assuming ops approves; marking this P1.

Jonas
Principal SRE, Redwood Inference

Attachments: kv_short_runbook.md (Markdown, 6KB), kv_rebalance_plan.pdf (PDF, 38KB)
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Eli Turner <eli.turner@graybridge.com>
Date: Sun, 12 Sep 2027 10:02:05 -0700
Subject: Re: Graybridge: sudden p99 latency spike & errors — exec note

Team —

Thanks for the quick triage. Please proceed with Jonas's recommendations. Aisha, keep Eli in the loop with estimated timing and a short one-line status every 15m. If we cannot return to SLA within 1 hour, I want a suggested credit calculation and an escalation brief for the CRO.

We should also call out the configuration change that correlated with this (09/11 key-hash roll) in the post-mortem and propose a global guardrail to prevent small hash changes from causing shard hotness. Jonas — please snapshot compaction and eviction rates before and after the rebalance for the postmortem.

Marissa Cole
Director, Customer Success | Redwood Inference
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
To: Eli Turner <eli.turner@graybridge.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 12 Sep 2027 10:18:52 -0700
Subject: Re: Graybridge: mitigation plan & timing

Eli —

Thanks for flagging this — quick update from our side:
- Jonas is starting an emergency KV rebalance now; we expect the operation to finish and validate within 20–30m.
- We will route your tenant to a temporary dedicated shard set during the window; that flip is in progress.
- We reduced the embeddings batching window for the next 30m to limit stall amplification.

We will send a short status every 15m. If you see any additional 500s, please forward logs and we will add them to the investigation. Sorry for the disruption — we are treating this as a P1 and will follow up with a post-mortem and remediation plan.

Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

---
On Mon, 13 Sep 2027 01:05:00 +0000 Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com> wrote:
> Update: rebalance completed at 01:05 UTC. p99 reduced from ~2.1s to ~450ms in the affected window; 500s dropped to baseline. Monitoring for 30m.
>
> Jonas

title: Graybridge: sudden p99 latency spike & errors — proposed KV rebalance
preview:
From: Eli Turner <eli.turner@graybridge.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: Graybridge SRE <sre@graybridge.com>
Date: Sun, 12 Sep 2027 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Re: API latency spike affecting ingestion pipeline

Hi Aisha —

We started seeing a sharp jump in p99 latency and a correlated rise in 500 responses for the embeddings route around 08:30 PDT. Throughput dropped by ~30% for a 15m window and then oscillated. Our job runs that depend on timely embeddings are backlogged and a downstream ingestion task failed.

Attached is a snapshot from our Grafana (metric_snapshot-20270912.png) and a short log excerpt. We rolled no code on our side in the window. Can you confirm this is on Redwood's side and give an ETA for mitigation? We will escalate internally if this continues.

Thanks,
Eli
Eli Turner
SRE Lead, Graybridge Solutions

Attachments: metric_snapshot-20270912.png (PNG, 86KB)


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
To: ops-incident@redwood.com, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Eli Turner <eli.turner@graybridge.com>
Date: Sun, 12 Sep 2027 09:28:44 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Graybridge: API latency spike affecting ingestion pipeline — urgent triage

Forwarding Graybridge SRE report — they have p99 spikes + 500s on embeddings. Eli says no changes on their side.

Ask: can infra/ops confirm whether we have a KV-store eviction pattern or hot-prefix activity that would cause HOL-style slowdown? If possible, propose a short mitigation (10-30m) we can apply that reduces downstream errors while we run a deeper fix. Please include timing and any customer-facing messaging. I have looped in Marissa for exec awareness.

Aisha
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
--- Forwarded message attached below ---


From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>, ops-incident@redwood.com
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Eli Turner <eli.turner@graybridge.com>
Date: Sun, 12 Sep 2027 09:43:11 -0700
Subject: Re: Graybridge: sudden p99 latency spike & errors — initial triage

Aisha / Eli —

Initial findings from a 09:30 check: multiple tenants were hitting a narrow set of KV prefixes due to a customer-side traffic pattern combined with our recent key-hash change (rolled 09/11). We are seeing rapid TTL churn and eviction storms on a subset of shards which is producing head-of-line queuing for downstream tokenization and batching paths. Symptoms match HOL blocking caused by cache churn rather than pure CPU saturation.

Immediate mitigations we can take (recommended order):
1) Apply an emergency KV rebalance for affected shards (non-disruptive). This spreads hot prefixes across more hosts and reduces eviction contention. Estimated 20m to complete and validate.
2) Temporarily relax prefix hashing affinity for Graybridge's tenant (route their traffic to a dedicated set of shards) — ~10m to flip routing, requires a short config push and circuit check.
3) Reduce batching window for embeddings for the next 30m to avoid large batch stall amplification (config-only, ~5m).

I attached a short runbook and a one-page rebalance plan. If we run 1+2 together we expect p99 to fall within their SLA within ~30 minutes. Risk: rebalance increases behind-the-scenes KV moveload; we will throttle the migration rate.

I will start the rebalance at T+10 assuming ops approves; marking this P1.

Jonas
Principal SRE, Redwood Inference

Attachments: kv_short_runbook.md (Markdown, 6KB), kv_rebalance_plan.pdf (PDF, 38KB)


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Eli Turner <eli.turner@graybridge.com>
Date: Sun, 12 Sep 2027 10:02:05 -0700
Subject: Re: Graybridge: sudden p99 latency spike & errors — exec note

Team —

Thanks for the quick triage. Please proceed with Jonas's recommendations. Aisha, keep Eli in the loop with estimated timing and a short one-line status every 15m. If we cannot return to SLA within 1 hour, I want a suggested credit calculation and an escalation brief for the CRO.

We should also call out the configuration change that correlated with this (09/11 key-hash roll) in the post-mortem and propose a global guardrail to prevent small hash changes from causing shard hotness. Jonas — please snapshot compaction and eviction rates before and after the rebalance for the postmortem.

Marissa Cole
Director, Customer Success | Redwood Inference


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
To: Eli Turner <eli.turner@graybridge.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 12 Sep 2027 10:18:52 -0700
Subject: Re: Graybridge: mitigation plan & timing

Eli —

Thanks for flagging this — quick update from our side:
- Jonas is starting an emergency KV rebalance now; we expect the operation to finish and validate within 20–30m.
- We will route your tenant to a temporary dedicated shard set during the window; that flip is in progress.
- We reduced the embeddings batching window for the next 30m to limit stall amplification.

We will send a short status every 15m. If you see any additional 500s, please forward logs and we will add them to the investigation. Sorry for the disruption — we are treating this as a P1 and will follow up with a post-mortem and remediation plan.

Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

---
On Mon, 13 Sep 2027 01:05:00 +0000 Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com> wrote:
> Update: rebalance completed at 01:05 UTC. p99 reduced from ~2.1s to ~450ms in the affected window; 500s dropped to baseline. Monitoring for 30m.
>
> Jonas
