source.subject: Request: Short-term credits while PO clears (HelioS Medical)
messages:
From: Mike Sanders <mike.sanders@heliosmed.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Purchasing Team <purchasing@heliosmed.com>, Helen Park <h.park@heliosmed.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Demo environment hold — request for short-term credits

Hi Alex,

We kicked off our internal risk review yesterday and finance has put a temporary hold on the PO while they validate some vendor paperwork. Procurement says the approval should clear within 10 business days, but that timeline is slipping into next week. Our product team is mid-run with the Redwood sandbox and we're concerned about losing momentum — several stakeholders rely on the demo traffic for next Tuesday's steering meeting.

Could Redwood provide temporary credits to keep the environment live until the PO clears? We're asking for about 3,000 token credits for up to 10 days. I've attached the draft PO and a short usage export so you can see our run rate. We can sign a simple acknowledgement that the credits will be reconciled against the PO when it's processed.

Attachments: helios_po_draft.pdf (PO draft), trial_usage_report_helios.csv (usage snapshot)

Thanks,
Mike
Mike Sanders
Sr. Product Manager, HelioS Medical
MS: +1 415-555-0193

On Fri, Jun 19, 2027 at 09:12 AM Mike Sanders <mike.sanders@heliosmed.com> wrote:
> Alex — attaching the PO draft. Procurement prefers email threads for documentation.

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mike Sanders <mike.sanders@heliosmed.com>
Cc: Purchasing Team <purchasing@heliosmed.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Jun 2027 12:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Demo environment hold — request for short-term credits

Hi Mike — thanks for the context and the attachments. I see the PO draft and the usage snapshot. Sorry to hear finance flagged the vendor paperwork; that can definitely stall things.

I can move this forward from our side — two asks: 1) I’ll escalate internally for provisional credits, and 2) could you confirm the exact date you expect the PO to clear? If procurement can give us a target date, we can set an expiration on any provisional credits to match.

Quick timeline from me: I’ll loop in Ben (finance channel approvals) and Lauren (CS) to approve a short-term runway. If approved I’ll provision the credits and send you activation steps + a brief acknowledgment form we can add to the PO package.

I’ve cc'd Purchasing here so your procurement thread remains complete.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com | +1 646-555-0122

On Fri, Jun 19, 2027 at 09:12 AM Mike Sanders <mike.sanders@heliosmed.com> wrote:
> Appreciate the quick help — aiming for next Friday (Jun 25) but finance said worst-case into the following week.

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Jun 2027 13:05:00 -0700
Subject: Approval request: provisional credit for HelioS Medical (HM-2027-042)

Ben / Lauren — quick internal ask. HelioS Medical (deal HM-2027-042) is stalled in procurement; client asked for ~3,000 token credits (10-day window) to keep their demo/live sandbox available for a steering review next week. They provided a PO draft and a usage snapshot (attached).

Proposal: provision a conditional credit of 2,000 tokens immediately (expires 10 days from provisioning) with the option to top up another 1,000 tokens if PO progress is confirmed. Condition: credits are reconciled against the final PO and invalidated if a PO is not completed within 30 days.

Points for approval:
- Amount: 2,000 provisional tokens now, +1,000 contingency
- Expiry: 10 days from grant
- Accounting: flag to deals/finance as a provisional promotion; recover or reconcile to PO once processed
- Legal: we’ll use a two-line acknowledgement (I can draft) and attach to the PO record

Attachments: trial_usage_report_helios.csv, helios_po_draft.pdf

Can you weigh in? If ok I’ll prepare the approval form and provision. This is time-sensitive — client meeting next Tues.

Thanks,
Alex

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, 20 Jun 2027 10:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Approval request: provisional credit for HelioS Medical (HM-2027-042)

Alex — I’m fine with provisioning short-term runway to avoid losing product momentum. A couple of constraints from finance:
1) Limit to 2,000 tokens immediately.
2) Mark as "provisional promotion" on the deal record and include the PO draft in the reconciliation notes.
3) Any top-up (+1,000 tokens) requires either a purchase order number or explicit finance confirmation.

I’ll sign off on the 2,000 tokens now. Can you ensure the acknowledgement form includes the reconciliation clause? Also flag the credit in Chargeback so it’s tracked against HM-2027-042.

Ben
Ben Carter
Director, Revenue Operations | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com

On Fri, Jun 19, 2027 at 01:05 PM Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Attaching usage and PO draft — let me know if you need more details.

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, 20 Jun 2027 15:00:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Approval request: provisional credit for HelioS Medical (HM-2027-042)

Thanks Ben — and Alex, agree with the approach. Two operational notes from CS:
- Please set the credits to a hard expiry (system-set) so they cannot be re-allocated beyond the 10-day window.
- Include usage throttles alert so we can notify HelioS if they spike past expected run rate.

Marissa — could you do a quick legal sanity check on the acknowledgement wording? I'll draft the form and share.

Lauren Bishop
Senior Customer Success Manager | Redwood Inference
lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com

On Sat, Jun 20, 2027 at 10:22 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> I signed off on the 2,000 tokens, Alex — please flag appropriately.

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mike Sanders <mike.sanders@heliosmed.com>, Purchasing Team <purchasing@heliosmed.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 23 Jun 2027 17:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Demo environment hold — request for short-term credits

Mike — quick update and next steps. Per internal approval we’ve provisioned a one-time provisional credit of 2,000 tokens to HelioS Medical. Details: 
- Amount: 2,000 tokens
- Validity: 10 days from provisioning (will auto-expire 2027-07-03)
- Condition: Credits are provisional and will be reconciled against the final PO (HM-2027-042). If PO is not completed within 30 days credits will be deducted per our reconciliation policy.

Action items for HelioS:
- Please confirm the expected PO clearance date (you mentioned Jun 25 as probable)
- Sign the attached two-line acknowledgement and send back to purchasing@heliosmed.com for their records

Attachments: credit_approval_form.docx (please sign and return), activation_instructions.txt
Related link: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/847-HM-2027-042

We’ve also configured a usage alert at +40% above the current run rate so Ben/CS will get a heads up if traffic spikes. If procurement confirms the PO early we can top up the remaining 1,000 tokens as discussed — finance will need the PO number to unlock that.

Hope this helps keep your demo running for the steering meeting. Let me know if you want me to join the procurement thread to expedite.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com | +1 646-555-0122

On Sat, Jun 20, 2027 at 03:00 PM Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Alex — I’ll send the acknowledgement draft to Marissa for quick review; should be short.

From: Mike Sanders <mike.sanders@heliosmed.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Purchasing Team <purchasing@heliosmed.com>, Helen Park <h.park@heliosmed.com>
Date: Thu, 24 Jun 2027 08:09:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Demo environment hold — request for short-term credits

Alex — thank you. Confirming receipt and the team is very grateful. We’ll sign the acknowledgement and aim to get the PO number to you no later than Jun 30. If anything moves earlier I’ll ping you directly.

—Mike

On Wed, Jun 23, 2027 at 05:45 PM Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Provisioned 2,000 tokens — please return the signed acknowledgement when you have it.

messages:
From: Evan Liu <evan.liu@luma.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Menon <priya.m@luma.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2027 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: RFP follow-up: TAM coverage, escalation expectations

Hi Aditya,

Thanks for the quick intro call last week. As part of our procurement packet we're consolidating answers for the support & TAM section of the RFP. Can you provide a short, itemized response for Luma that covers the following:?

- Dedicated TAM coverage model (hours / SLA for email, phone, P1/P2)
- On-call / after-hours escalation path and guaranteed response windows
- Roster: primary/on-call shifts and names (we can accept role placeholders for now)
- Incident communications workflow (who we hear from at what cadence and what channels)
- Evidence: SOC 2 report availability, audit logs, and retention policy summary

We have a target to submit the RFP by EOD Tuesday (6/22). If you can send a consolidated document or a quick email summary, that would be ideal.

Best,
Evan
Evan Liu
Head of SRE, Luma Financial

--
Attachment: Luma_RFP_Template_v3.docx (referenced, not sent yet)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2027 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: RFP follow-up: TAM coverage, escalation expectations

Team — forwarding Luma's questions. They want a concise, bulletized answer they can paste into the procurement packet. Deadline EOD Tue 6/22.

Neha: can you draft the TAM coverage (hours, SLA windows, P1/P2 response times)?
Marissa: can you assemble the roster CSV (role placeholders ok) and the escalation diagram?
Ben: flag anything commercial (reserved hours / retainer language) we should call out so procurement doesn't misinterpret.

I'll own the consolidated PDF and the Drive link. Please add answers in-line on this thread so I can collate.

Thanks — Aditya

On Mon, Jun 14, 2027 at 10:12 AM Evan Liu <evan.liu@luma.com> wrote:
> [See above]

From: Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 09:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: RFP follow-up: TAM coverage, escalation expectations

AD: Draft answers below — keeping these short so procurement can paste directly. Marissa, I'll reference the roster file for names. Ben, I flagged commercial notes with [COMM] tags.

1) TAM coverage model:
- Primary TAM for Luma (Dedicated): 40 hours/week standard engagement + 8 hours/week strategic planning (retainer).
- Email/Portal response: P1 (sev/production down) — initial ack within 30 minutes, phone/video response within 2 hours; P2 (major impact) — ack within 2 hours, response within 6 hours.
- Business hours: 07:00–19:00 PT (Mon–Fri). After-hours coverage is on-call rotation for P1 only.
- [COMM] Retainer covers up to 4 on-call P1 responses per month; additional P1 incidents billed at T&M unless a SLA addendum is signed.

2) On-call & escalation path (summary):
- Level 0: Luma first responder (customer ops) -> Level 1: Redwood TAM (primary) -> Level 2: Redwood Platform Infra & Eng on-call -> Level 3: Engineering escalation (swarm) / Executive paging if >4 hours impact.
- Escalation windows and primary contacts are included in the roster CSV (see attachment).

3) Roster (placeholder roles allowed): attached CSV contains: role, name (placeholder), on-call phone, escalation order, timezone.

4) Incident communications workflow:
- For P1 incidents: phone alert to TAM within 15 minutes of detection, 15-min status updates for first hour, then 30-min cadence until resolution. Postmortem delivered within 72 hours; RCA and action plan within 10 business days.
- For P2: status email every 2 hours during business hours; summary and RCA within 7 business days.

5) Evidence & retention:
- SOC 2 Type II report: available under NDA; we can provide the report and bridge letter.
- Audit logs: retention 365 days by default; can be extended via contract.
- Data retention / export: customers may request full exports; we maintain sandboxed retention controls per account.

Let me know if you'd like me to tighten timing language or remove the commercial callouts before final.

-Neha

--
Attachment referenced: redwood-support-roster-2027.csv

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 16 Jun 2027 08:47:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: RFP follow-up: TAM coverage, escalation expectations

Team — I created two artifacts: 1) tam-escalation-diagram.png (simple flowchart for procurement), 2) Luma-RFP-TAM-Answers.pdf which integrates Neha's bullets and the roster CSV.

Quick notes:
- I normalized time windows to PT as requested; included explicit phone escalation numbers (placeholder roles) so Luma has something to ingest.
- For SOC 2 we added the standard NDA line and an option to provide a redacted report directly to security.

I'll attach the artifacts to the draft Drive doc; Aditya, ping me if you want stronger SLA commitments called out — I erred conservative to avoid contractual exposure.

-Marissa

Attachments: tam-escalation-diagram.png, Luma-RFP-TAM-Answers.pdf

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Evan Liu <evan.liu@luma.com>, Priya Menon <priya.m@luma.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 21 Jun 2027 08:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Customer RFP: Technical Account Manager coverage, escalation flow, and incident comms

Hi Evan / Priya — please find our consolidated responses attached and a short summary below (the PDF and roster CSV are linked in Drive). Happy to hop on a 30-min call if you want to walk through any line items.

Summary highlights:
- Dedicated TAM model: 40h/week primary, on-call rotation for P1 after-hours; P1 ack within 30 minutes, phone response within 2 hours.
- Incident comms: 15-min updates for the first hour on P1; postmortem within 72 hours, RCA & action plan in 10 business days.
- Evidence: SOC 2 Type II available under NDA; audit logs retained 365 days by default.
- Commercial note: retainer covers up to 4 after-hours P1s/mo; additional incidents billed at T&M unless an SLA addendum is executed.

Drive links: https://drive.redwood.com/rdw/luma-rfp-answers-2027
DocuSign: DOCUSIGN-REQ-44921 (if procurement requires signatures)

Thanks — looking forward to your feedback; we can tighten SLA language if needed for contracting.

-Aditya

Attachments: Luma-RFP-TAM-Answers.pdf, redwood-support-roster-2027.csv

From: Evan Liu <evan.liu@luma.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Menon <priya.m@luma.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 21 Jun 2027 09:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Customer RFP: Technical Account Manager coverage, escalation flow, and incident comms

Aditya / team — this is great, thank you. The consolidated PDF and roster give procurement what they need. A few quick clarifications we might ask procurement to accept:

- Confirming: postmortem within 72 hours is fine; RCA in 10 business days accepted.
- Could you add a short sentence that on-call rotations will include regional overlap (PT and ET) for primary coverage?
- We'll sign the NDA for SOC 2 and follow via DocuSign if required.

I'll flag internally that we may request a slightly expanded log retention window for one regulated workload — will follow up if that becomes a requirement.

Appreciate the quick turnaround.

Best,
Evan

--
Evan Liu
Head of SRE, Luma Financial

AD: Draft answers below — keeping these short so procurement can paste directly. Marissa, I'll reference the roster file for names. Ben, I flagged commercial notes with [COMM] tags.

1) TAM coverage model:
- Primary TAM for Luma (Dedicated): 40 hours/week standard engagement + 8 hours/week strategic planning (retainer).
- Email/Portal response: P1 (sev/production down) — initial ack within 30 minutes, phone/video response within 2 hours; P2 (major impact) — ack within 2 hours, response within 6 hours.
- Business hours: 07:00–19:00 PT (Mon–Fri). After-hours coverage is on-call rotation for P1 only.
- [COMM] Retainer covers up to 4 on-call P1 responses per month; additional P1 incidents billed at T&M unless a SLA addendum is signed.

2) On-call & escalation path (summary):
- Level 0: Luma first responder (customer ops) -> Level 1: Redwood TAM (primary) -> Level 2: Redwood Platform Infra & Eng on-call -> Level 3: Engineering escalation (swarm) / Executive paging if >4 hours impact.
- Escalation windows and primary contacts are included in the roster CSV (see attachment).

3) Roster (placeholder roles allowed): attached CSV contains: role, name (placeholder), on-call phone, escalation order, timezone.

4) Incident communications workflow:
- For P1 incidents: phone alert to TAM within 15 minutes of detection, 15-min status updates for first hour, then 30-min cadence until resolution. Postmortem delivered within 72 hours; RCA and action plan within 10 business days.
- For P2: status email every 2 hours during business hours; summary and RCA within 7 business days.

5) Evidence & retention:
- SOC 2 Type II report: available under NDA; we can provide the report and bridge letter.
- Audit logs: retention 365 days by default; can be extended via contract.
- Data retention / export: customers may request full exports; we maintain sandboxed retention controls per account.

Let me know if you'd like me to tighten timing language or remove the commercial callouts before final.

-Neha

source.subject: Partner cost-sharing for vector-to-S3 eviction policy
messages:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@pinecone.io>, Marco Rossi <marco@weaviate.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 05 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Partner cost-sharing for vector-to-S3 eviction policy

Hi Elena / Marco —

Following up from last week's partner sync: we need to land on an operational and commercial model for how partner storage and cold spillover costs are allocated when customer traffic causes vector DB eviction to S3 (or S3 read-through) under hybrid index setups. This is especially important for customers in the Acme Retail pilot where we've seen frequent cold churn spikes during batch reindex windows.

Attached is a draft cost-split spreadsheet (cost-split-draft.xlsx) and a sketch of the spillover flow (diagram_spillover.drawio). High-level ask: can each partner review the proposed allocation logic and confirm whether you can: 1) emit the usage telemetry we need (eviction counts, bytes spilled, egress calls), and 2) accept a partner-facing invoice or credit flow based on the chosen model?

We proposed two models in the spreadsheet:
- Split by bytes spilled (simple)
- Split by query-weighted usage (favours partner if they serve more reads after cold warm-up)

If possible, please review by EOD Wednesday (2027-06-09). If there are immediate no-go items (e.g., egress billing constraints), flag them here and we'll schedule a short tech+commercial sync.

Thanks — appreciate a quick read.

Alex Martinez
Product Partnerships, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0112

---
Attachment: cost-split-draft.xlsx (spreadsheet, 14KB)
Attachment: diagram_spillover.drawio (diagram, 8KB)

From: Elena Morales <elena@pinecone.io>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 05 Jun 2027 10:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Partner cost-sharing for vector-to-S3 eviction policy

Alex —

Thanks for the clear note and attachments. Pinecone can provide eviction and spill telemetry (evictions/sec, bytes_spilled/hour, per-index counters) but two quick concerns from our side:

1) Egress semantics: in many cloud setups egress from Pinecone to the customer's S3 (or between regions) can be billed at a different rate depending on the customer's cloud account. We need to define which account incurs egress and whether Redwood will aggregate and bill back.

2) Warm-up/anti-eviction incentives: if we surface a cost to partners purely by bytes spilled, that creates a tension where partners will try to keep fewer segments in memory to avoid share payments. We think a hybrid approach that includes a small warm-up credit (partner covers X% of first-byte egress, Redwood credits for prefetch requests) could align behaviour.

I've looped Marco from Weaviate to see if the same telemetry is practical on their side (cc'd). Also, can you share a sample 24-hour traffic slice for Acme Retail so we can model real churn?

Happy to jump on a 30m sync next week — propose Tue 6/8 11:00 PDT.

Best,
Elena Morales
Strategic Partnerships, Pinecone
elena@pinecone.io

---
(Quoted message)
On Fri, Jun 5, 2027 at 9:12 AM Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Elena / Marco —
> 
> Following up from last week's partner sync: we need to land on an operational and commercial model for how partner storage and cold spillover costs are allocated when customer traffic causes vector DB eviction to S3...

From: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@pinecone.io>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 06 Jun 2027 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Partner cost-sharing for vector-to-S3 eviction policy — technical notes

Elena / Alex —

Quick engineering notes after skimming the draft:

- Eviction triggers: we typically see two dominant causes for spillover: large batch reindexing (bulk writes) and temporal access spikes on long-tail documents. Our metric of choice to attribute cost is "eviction-weighted-queries", which multiplies evicted_bytes by the number of subsequent queries that required rehydration within a 12-hour window.

- Cache TTLs & prefetch: if partners can expose an "estimated warm cache size" and allow Redwood to request proactive prefetches for hot keys, we can avoid a large class of rehydration reads. Prefetches should be flagged so they don't get double-counted as normal queries.

- Accounting parity: for fairness we need both sides to surface a) per-index eviction_count, b) per-index bytes_spilled, and c) rehydrate_reads_for_evicted_keys. Pinecone's approach to counts looks compatible based on Elena's note; Weaviate may need an SDK change.

- SLO impact: any commercial split must be paired with SLA language that ties predictable short-term latency degradation (during massive rehydration) to a remediation path (credits or temporary capacity uplift).

I attached a short note with heuristics (eviction-heuristics.md). Happy to present these metrics in the sync.

Soojin Lee
Engineering Manager, Indexing & Runtime, Redwood Inference
soojin.lee@redwood.com

---
Attachment: eviction-heuristics.md (text, 6KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Elena Morales <elena@pinecone.io>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 08 Jun 2027 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Partner cost-sharing for vector-to-S3 eviction policy — commercial proposal

Alex / Elena —

Commercially, for pilots (Acme Retail + two mid-market customers) I propose a floor/split approach to reduce churn on refresh:

- Floor: Redwood covers the first $0.10 per 10GB/day of spill (to absorb noise).
- Split: Remaining costs split 60/40 (Redwood/Partner) when rehydrate_reads_for_evicted_keys > 500 per index per day.
- Credit window: if the partner cannot meet the telemetry requirements, the partner agrees to a larger split (70/30) or provide credits to cover missing visibility.

Attached is a light term sheet capturing these numbers (commercial-term-sheet_v3.pdf). Key ask for partners: can you commit to surfacing the 3 metrics Soojin called out and accept an aggregated monthly billing reconciliation?

If Pinecone prefers an API-based billing callback instead of invoices, we can accept that — just need one of the flows.

Ben Carter
Partnerships Lead, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

---
Attachment: commercial-term-sheet_v3.pdf (pdf, 32KB)

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@pinecone.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 09 Jun 2027 15:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Partner cost-sharing for vector-to-S3 eviction policy — next steps

Elena —

Thanks for the rapid responses and helpful detail. Agreed next steps:

1) Engineering sync (30m) to align telemetry fields and prefetch semantics — Tue 6/15 11:00 PDT (calendar invite to follow).
2) Redwood legal to fold the floor/split language from Ben's term sheet into the Acme Retail addendum and circulate a DocuSign draft by 6/18 (link below).
3) Pinecone to confirm telemetry availability and whether you'd prefer invoice vs API callback by EOD 6/11.

I'm updating HubSpot (Deal D-98432) with the proposed commercial model and will attach Ben's term sheet. If Pinecone wants to run a quick cost model against a 24-hour trace we can provide one — Ben, can you share the Acme trace anonymized?

Thanks all — looking forward to locking this down.

Alex Martinez
Product Partnerships, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.com

---
Related links: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/98432
DocuSign draft: https://www.docusign.com/embedded/abc123
(Forwarded for visibility)
On Mon, Jun 8, 2027 at 11:05 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Alex / Elena —
> 
> Commercially, for pilots (Acme Retail + two mid-market customers) I propose a floor/split approach to reduce churn on refresh:...

Subject: Partner cost-sharing for vector-to-S3 eviction policy

Hi Elena / Marco —

Following up from last week's partner sync: we need to land on an operational and commercial model for how partner storage and cold spillover costs are allocated when customer traffic causes vector DB eviction to S3 (or S3 read-through) under hybrid index setups. This is especially important for customers in the Acme Retail pilot where we've seen frequent cold churn spikes during batch reindex windows.

Attached is a draft cost-split spreadsheet (cost-split-draft.xlsx) and a sketch of the spillover flow (diagram_spillover.drawio). High-level ask: can each partner review the proposed allocation logic and confirm whether you can: 1) emit the usage telemetry we need (eviction counts, bytes spilled, egress calls), and 2) accept a partner-facing invoice or credit flow based on the chosen model?

We proposed two models in the spreadsheet:
- Split by bytes spilled (simple)
- Split by query-weighted usage (favours partner if they serve more reads after cold warm-up)

If possible, please review by EOD Wednesday (2027-06-09). If there are immediate no-go items (e.g., egress billing constraints), flag them here and we'll schedule a short tech+commercial sync.

Thanks — appreciate a quick read.

Alex Martinez
Product Partnerships, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0112

---
Attachment: cost-split-draft.xlsx (spreadsheet, 14KB)
Attachment: diagram_spillover.drawio (diagram, 8KB)

From: Elena Morales <elena@pinecone.io>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 05 Jun 2027 10:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Partner cost-sharing for vector-to-S3 eviction policy

Alex —

Thanks for the clear note and attachments. Pinecone can provide eviction and spill telemetry (evictions/sec, bytes_spilled/hour, per-index counters) but two quick concerns from our side:

1) Egress semantics: in many cloud setups egress from Pinecone to the customer's S3 (or between regions) can be billed at a different rate depending on the customer's cloud account. We need to define which account incurs egress and whether Redwood will aggregate and bill back.

2) Warm-up/anti-eviction incentives: if we surface a cost to partners purely by bytes spilled, that creates a tension where partners will try to keep fewer segments in memory to avoid share payments. We think a hybrid approach that includes a small warm-up credit (partner covers X% of first-byte egress, Redwood credits for prefetch requests) could align behaviour.

I've looped Marco from Weaviate to see if the same telemetry is practical on their side (cc'd). Also, can you share a sample 24-hour traffic slice for Acme Retail so we can model real churn?

Happy to jump on a 30m sync next week — propose Tue 6/8 11:00 PDT.

Best,
Elena Morales
Strategic Partnerships, Pinecone
elena@pinecone.io

---
(Quoted message)
On Fri, Jun 5, 2027 at 9:12 AM Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Elena / Marco —
> 
> Following up from last week's partner sync: we need to land on an operational and commercial model for how partner storage and cold spillover costs are allocated when customer traffic causes vector DB eviction to S3...


From: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@pinecone.io>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 06 Jun 2027 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Partner cost-sharing for vector-to-S3 eviction policy — technical notes

Elena / Alex —

Quick engineering notes after skimming the draft:

- Eviction triggers: we typically see two dominant causes for spillover: large batch reindexing (bulk writes) and temporal access spikes on long-tail documents. Our metric of choice to attribute cost is "eviction-weighted-queries", which multiplies evicted_bytes by the number of subsequent queries that required rehydration within a 12-hour window.

- Cache TTLs & prefetch: if partners can expose an "estimated warm cache size" and allow Redwood to request proactive prefetches for hot keys, we can avoid a large class of rehydration reads. Prefetches should be flagged so they don't get double-counted as normal queries.

- Accounting parity: for fairness we need both sides to surface a) per-index eviction_count, b) per-index bytes_spilled, and c) rehydrate_reads_for_evicted_keys. Pinecone's approach to counts looks compatible based on Elena's note; Weaviate may need an SDK change.

- SLO impact: any commercial split must be paired with SLA language that ties predictable short-term latency degradation (during massive rehydration) to a remediation path (credits or temporary capacity uplift).

I attached a short note with heuristics (eviction-heuristics.md). Happy to present these metrics in the sync.

Soojin Lee
Engineering Manager, Indexing & Runtime, Redwood Inference
soojin.lee@redwood.com

---
Attachment: eviction-heuristics.md (text, 6KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Elena Morales <elena@pinecone.io>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 08 Jun 2027 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Partner cost-sharing for vector-to-S3 eviction policy — commercial proposal

Alex / Elena —

Commercially, for pilots (Acme Retail + two mid-market customers) I propose a floor/split approach to reduce churn on refresh:

- Floor: Redwood covers the first $0.10 per 10GB/day of spill (to absorb noise).
- Split: Remaining costs split 60/40 (Redwood/Partner) when rehydrate_reads_for_evicted_keys > 500 per index per day.
- Credit window: if the partner cannot meet the telemetry requirements, the partner agrees to a larger split (70/30) or provide credits to cover missing visibility.

Attached is a light term sheet capturing these numbers (commercial-term-sheet_v3.pdf). Key ask for partners: can you commit to surfacing the 3 metrics Soojin called out and accept an aggregated monthly billing reconciliation?

If Pinecone prefers an API-based billing callback instead of invoices, we can accept that — just need one of the flows.

Ben Carter
Partnerships Lead, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

---
Attachment: commercial-term-sheet_v3.pdf (pdf, 32KB)

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@pinecone.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 09 Jun 2027 15:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Partner cost-sharing for vector-to-S3 eviction policy — next steps

Elena —

Thanks for the rapid responses and helpful detail. Agreed next steps:

1) Engineering sync (30m) to align telemetry fields and prefetch semantics — Tue 6/15 11:00 PDT (calendar invite to follow).
2) Redwood legal to fold the floor/split language from Ben's term sheet into the Acme Retail addendum and circulate a DocuSign draft by 6/18 (link below).
3) Pinecone to confirm telemetry availability and whether you'd prefer invoice vs API callback by EOD 6/11.

I'm updating HubSpot (Deal D-98432) with the proposed commercial model and will attach Ben's term sheet. If Pinecone wants to run a quick cost model against a 24-hour trace we can provide one — Ben, can you share the Acme trace anonymized?

Thanks all — looking forward to locking this down.

Alex Martinez
Product Partnerships, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.com

---
Related links: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/98432
DocuSign draft: https://www.docusign.com/embedded/abc123
(Forwarded for visibility)

> 
> Commercially, for pilots (Acme Retail + two mid-market customers) I propose a floor/split approach to reduce churn on refresh:...

messages:
From: Samir Patel <samir@fastcart.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Rao <priya@fastcart.ai>
Date: Tue, 8 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: FastCart signup — quick demo?

Hey Alex,

We just signed up for the self-serve tier and started poking at the API. FastCart is an ecommerce search + discovery startup — primary use case is product search relevance + a conversational shopping assistant on product pages.

We have a few concrete asks before we schedule a demo:
- Expected sandbox volume: ~500k embeddings/month (initial).
- Generation: approx 200k tokens/day during peak tests; QPS ramp to ~20 req/s in pilot.
- Timeline: we'd like a short pilot in the next 6–8 weeks and evaluate Dedicated/Private options later.
- Security: SOC 2 evidence + SAML SSO for console users.

Can you share an engineer-led demo and a quick checklist of what you need from us (test data, sample prompts, network access)? I'm in PST — available Tue/Wed afternoons.

Thanks,
Samir
Samir Patel
Head of ML, FastCart
samir@fastcart.ai

---
Attachments: none

From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir@fastcart.ai>
Cc: Priya Rao <priya@fastcart.ai>
Date: Tue, 8 Jun 2027 10:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: FastCart signup — quick demo?

Hi Samir — great to meet you (virtually) and congrats on getting started with the self-serve flow. Happy to run an engineer-led demo and get the pilot scoped.

Quick answers up-front and a few qualification items so we can tailor the session:
- We can cover product-search tuning, embeddings lifecycle (indexing, re-embedding cadence), and a live gen demo for recommendations.
- Hosted API should be fine for the pilot; we can discuss Dedicated/Private during rollout.

A few follow-ups that help our demo engineer prepare:
1) Which embedding model profile are you planning to use (semantic search vs. semantic + metadata)?
2) Do you already have a vector index provider (FAISS/Elastic/vector DB) or will you evaluate Redwood embeddings + on-platform indexing?
3) Tokenization detail: are the 200k tokens/day mostly generation (assistant responses) or includes upstream user input?
4) Any regulatory constraints on data residency?
5) For SSO: you mentioned SAML — can you share your IdP metadata or confirm Okta/Azure AD?

I've attached a short demo guide (redwood-demo-guide.pdf) that lists common artifacts we ask for (sample product catalog CSV, sample query set, small telemetry export). If you can drop the security questionnaire (fastcart-security-reqs.pdf) into the thread ahead of the call that helps speed approvals.

Proposed times: Tue 6/10 2:00–2:30pm PT or Wed 6/11 3:30–4:00pm PT — here's my booking link: https://cal.redwood.com/alex/30min?slot=20270610T1400

If those don't work, share a couple times and we'll adapt. I'll loop in our solutions engineer to join.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Enterprise SDR, Redwood Inference
alex@redwood.com

Attachments: redwood-demo-guide.pdf

From: Samir Patel <samir@fastcart.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Rao <priya@fastcart.ai>
Date: Tue, 8 Jun 2027 16:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: FastCart signup — quick demo?

Thanks Alex — answers inline below and attaching our security checklist.

On Tue, Jun 8, 2027 at 10:15 AM Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com> wrote:
> A few follow-ups that help our demo engineer prepare:
> 1) Which embedding model profile are you planning to use (semantic search vs. semantic + metadata)?

We'd like semantic + metadata (we include category, brand, price buckets in vector metadata).

> 2) Do you already have a vector index provider (FAISS/Elastic/vector DB) or will you evaluate Redwood embeddings + on-platform indexing?

We currently store vectors in a managed Elastic cluster but are open to testing Redwood's managed index in the pilot if it simplifies ops.

> 3) Tokenization detail: are the 200k tokens/day mostly generation (assistant responses) or includes upstream user input?

Rough split: ~60% model output (recommendation text + short prompts), 40% user input. Peak QPS estimates above assume batched inference for search + an async path for chat.

> 4) Any regulatory constraints on data residency?

All production customer PII must remain in us-east for now; sandbox ok elsewhere.

> 5) For SSO: you mentioned SAML — can you share your IdP metadata or confirm Okta/Azure AD?

We use Okta. I'll share the IdP XML in the next email.

Timeline: aiming to run a 6-week pilot starting mid-July, deliverables: basic relevance improvement, embedding refresh cadence, and a short cost/latency report. If pilot looks good, we'll discuss Dedicated capacity for sustained traffic in Q4.

Attaching: fastcart-security-reqs.pdf (quick checklist + SOC2 but we need your SOC 2 cert and data retention policies).

Available times: Tue 6/10 2:00pm PT works for us. Please book and confirm the join (we'd like an engineer who can talk latency/quantization tradeoffs).

Cheers,
Samir

Attachments: fastcart-security-reqs.pdf

From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 9 Jun 2027 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: FastCart signup — demo & qualification

Ben / Monica — forwarding a new inbound. FastCart (Samir) is a commerce search/playback use case:
- ~500k embeddings/month initial, 200k tokens/day (60% gen), peak ~20 QPS
- Okta SAML for SSO, SOC2 evidence requested, production data residency us-east
- Pilot target mid-July for ~6 weeks; wants engineer-led demo Tue 6/10 2:00pm PT (booked link below)

Can you confirm:
1) Which engineer is available for the 6/10 slot and can speak to quantization + latency profiles?
2) Recommended model family for mixed search + short-assistant responses (we should propose a hybrid? e.g., embedding model X + generative model Y)
3) Any internal checklist we should complete before the call (test dataset upload, sandbox token creation, pre-configure SAML SSO demo)?

I'm including our demo guide and their security checklist in the thread. I'll confirm the calendar once we lock the SRE/SE.

Thanks — Alex

source.subject: Next steps: embedding pipeline decision log & function-call/streaming approach
messages:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Moreno <alex.moreno@novaly.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 07 Jun 2027 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Discovery — embeddings + rerank + streaming funcalls (post-call recap)

Alex — thanks again for taking the call today. Quick recap while it's fresh and a proposed set of decisions so we can lock an implementation path. TL;DR: you want a deterministic rerank/embedding pipeline for patient record retrieval, predictable latency for 95th pct, and a lightweight function-calling surface for structured output to downstream EHR processors.

Key pain points you mentioned:
- Search recall variability across long clinical notes (embeddings drift across updates).
- Token-level cost spikes during peak triage windows (evening PST).
- Need for a single, auditable structured output schema for downstream ingestion into your CDW.

Goals we aligned on:

source.subject: Sierra Analytics — sizing ask: committed TPS, latency headroom, and fallback
messages:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
To: Melissa Cheng <melissa@hypersight.ai>
Date: Sat, 05 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: initial sizing numbers for Dedicated commit

Hi Melissa — good to connect last week. Quick follow-up: to build a committed Dedicated proposal and committed capacity pricing for Sierra, can you confirm the following items and share any attachment you have for traffic profiles?

1) Expected 95th-percentile sustained tokens/sec during peak windows (per-organization) and typical burst multiplier (e.g., 3x short bursts).
2) Average request generation length (tokens) and max expected generation length.
3) Percent of requests that are chat (multi-turn) vs single-prompt inference.
4) Required latency SLOs (p95 and p99) for interactive flows; batch/async tolerances if any.
5) Any regulatory/regional restrictions for data residency.

If you can attach a CSV or a simple spreadsheet that shows hourly TPS/tokens for a week, that would be ideal — we use that to simulate batching/caching effects in our latency model.

I’ve copied Ben (capacity), Sergio (runtime optimizations), and Lauren (commercial) so we can run a quick runbook after we get the numbers.

Thanks — Aisha

--
Aisha Rahman
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
[aisha@redwoodinference.com]
[Attachment: request-checklist.pdf (stub)]

From: Melissa Cheng <melissa@hypersight.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@sierranet.com
Date: Mon, 07 Jun 2027 08:23:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: initial sizing numbers for Dedicated commit

Hi Aisha — thanks. Attached is the demand profile you asked for. High-level callouts:

- Peak sustained tokens/sec (95th): 45,000 tokens/sec across the tenant during our top 2-hour window (we can provide per-route breakout if helpful).
- Burst behavior: short spikes up to ~140,000 tokens/sec observed (3.1x).
- Avg generation length: 80 tokens; max observed 1,200 tokens (rare).
- Chat vs single-turn: ~68% chat (multi-turn). KV cache hit estimated at ~22% (session affinity).
- Latency requirements: interactive product flows p95 <= 250 ms (including network), p99 <= 450 ms. Background batch jobs can be 2–5s.
- Residency: US only for now.

Attachments: sierra_demand_profile.csv (hourly tokens and route), SLA-reqs.pdf.

Let us know the earliest you can run a model and share a recommended committed slab + fallback.

Best,
Melissa
Head of Product, Sierra Analytics
[Attachment: sierra_demand_profile.csv (CSV, 48KB)]

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio_alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 07 Jun 2027 10:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Sierra demand profile + sizing ask — need throughput/latency model

Forwarding Sierra's demand profile — please see Melissa's note below. Quick ask: can we produce a deal-specific throughput/latency model (tokens/sec estimates and SLO fit) and a recommended Dedicated slab configuration with headroom and fallback policies? Key constraints:

- Model they plan to run: redwood-open-7b-q4 (producer says 7B-family, quantized). We should validate perf on our A100-80GB baseline; if we need other shapes call it out.
- Peak 95th sustained: 45k tokens/sec; bursts up to 140k. Average gen length 80 tokens, chat-heavy.
- Latency SLO target: p95 <= 250ms (end-to-end).

Please include in the output:
- Estimated tokens/sec per GPU (A100-80GB) for their mix and sequence length distribution — show the math.
- Required number of GPUs for committed slab to meet p95 with a safety multiplier (recommendation + rationale).
- Burst/overflow plan (fallback to hosted + model variant quantization, warm KV cache strategy).
- Ops runbook notes: cold-start cache warming, autoscale thresholds, target queue latency.

Ben — can you run the batch of perf numbers and produce the initial Excel model? Sergio — please flag any runtime knobs (KV cache hit ops, quantization adjustments) that would materially reduce GPU needs. Lauren — I'll need a commercial blurb and suggested commitment term and price uplift for headroom.

Deadline: can we have a first-pass by EOD tomorrow PST?

Thanks — Aisha

--
Aisha Rahman
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: latency-model-v2.xlsx (stub)]

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwoodinference.com>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio_alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 08 Jun 2027 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Sierra demand profile + sizing ask — recommended slab and headroom

Team — ran a quick model (attached) and summarized below. Assumptions and rationale first, then recommended committed slab.

Assumptions (baseline):
- Baseline serving config: redwood-open-7b-q4 on A100-80GB nodes, single model instance per GPU with dynamic KV caching.
- Measured median tokens/sec per GPU (our internal microbench for 80-token average, multi-turn mix): ~3,800 tokens/sec sustained per A100-80GB for the observed chat-heavy mix (this includes inference + kv cache overhead).
- For longer sequences (avg 80 -> p95 includes occasional longer tails), effective per-GPU sustained drops to ~3,200 tps when accounting for 10% extra model context processing and network overhead.
- Burst handling: we plan for 2.5x burst absorb capacity via pre-warmed host fallback pools + autoscale. Pure committed slab should be sized for sustained 95th load with a safety multiplier of 1.25x for ops variance.

Simple math (sustained):
- Required sustained GPUs = ceil(45,000 tokens/sec / 3,200 tokens/sec_per_gpu) = ceil(14.06) => 15 GPUs to cover 95th sustained without safety buffer.
- With safety multiplier 1.25 => 15 * 1.25 = 18.75 => recommend reserving 19 A100-80GB GPUs for committed slab.

Burst plan (140k observed):
- Committed slab (19 GPUs) provides ~60.8k tokens/sec sustained (19 * 3,200).
- For bursts to 140k, rely on a) warm hosted fallback pool sized to absorb remaining ~79k tokens/sec; b) autoscale burst nodes (ephemeral) and prioritized queueing for non-critical routes. Expect short p95 degradations during longest tails; recommend SLA language for burst-mode latency tolerance.

Latency fit:
- With 19 GPUs and KV cache warm, we expect p95 model-inference latency inside 160–190 ms; network/egress budget 40–90 ms depending on region/peering. Combined end-to-end p95 estimate ~220–280 ms. To meet strict 250 ms p95 target across all edge cases, we suggest:
  - Keep 19 GPUs committed, and maintain a small warm standby of 3 extra GPUs (hot-warm) capable of absorbing stateful sessions immediately. This pushes committed+standby to 22 GPUs and gives extra headroom for p95.

Ops notes / runbook highlights:
- Warm KV cache policy: pre-warm KV for top 20% of active session IDs (sourced from their session telemetry) during peak windows; this can recover ~12–18% effective tokens/sec improvement.
- Autoscale thresholds: trigger ephemeral scale when queue latency > 60ms for > 30s. Use queue-first throttling for non-interactive routes.
- Model fallback: offer a slightly faster quantized variant (redwood-open-7b-q4-fast) for degraded tail conditions; quality tests show ~2% NLL degradation but 20–25% higher tokens/sec.

Commercial suggestion (Lauren): recommend 12-month committed slab of 19 GPUs with ability to burst to hosted capacity. Price uplift for standby 3 GPUs included in an enhanced SLA tier. Suggest proposal: 19 committed GPUs + 3 hot-standby for guaranteed p95 <=250ms, with defined burst fallback to hosted at defined ingress rates.

Attachments: latency-model-v2.xlsx (attached with per-hour sims).

If you want I can run a more granular per-route simulation (per-route qps and session affinity) tomorrow — that will likely move the GPU requirement by +/-2 GPUs depending on cache hit optimizations.

Ben
Director, Capacity Planning, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: latency-model-v2.xlsx (XLSX, 76KB)]

Cc: procurement@sierranet.com
Date: Mon, 07 Jun 2027 08:23:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: initial sizing numbers for Dedicated commit

Hi Aisha — thanks. Attached is the demand profile you asked for. High-level callouts:

- Peak sustained tokens/sec (95th): 45,000 tokens/sec across the tenant during our top 2-hour window (we can provide per-route breakout if helpful).
- Burst behavior: short spikes up to ~140,000 tokens/sec observed (3.1x).
- Avg generation length: 80 tokens; max observed 1,200 tokens (rare).
- Chat vs single-turn: ~68% chat (multi-turn). KV cache hit estimated at ~22% (session affinity).
- Latency requirements: interactive product flows p95 <= 250 ms (including network), p99 <= 450 ms. Background batch jobs can be 2–5s.
- Residency: US only for now.

Attachments: sierra_demand_profile.csv (hourly tokens and route), SLA-reqs.pdf.

Let us know the earliest you can run a model and share a recommended committed slab + fallback.

Best,
Melissa
Head of Product, Sierra Analytics
[Attachment: sierra_demand_profile.csv (CSV, 48KB)]

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio_alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 07 Jun 2027 10:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Sierra demand profile + sizing ask — need throughput/latency model

Forwarding Sierra's demand profile — please see Melissa's note below. Quick ask: can we produce a deal-specific throughput/latency model (tokens/sec estimates and SLO fit) and a recommended Dedicated slab configuration with headroom and fallback policies? Key constraints:

- Model they plan to run: redwood-open-7b-q4 (producer says 7B-family, quantized). We should validate perf on our A100-80GB baseline; if we need other shapes call it out.
- Peak 95th sustained: 45k tokens/sec; bursts up to 140k. Average gen length 80 tokens, chat-heavy.
- Latency SLO target: p95 <= 250ms (end-to-end).

Please include in the output:
- Estimated tokens/sec per GPU (A100-80GB) for their mix and sequence length distribution — show the math.
- Required number of GPUs for committed slab to meet p95 with a safety multiplier (recommendation + rationale).
- Burst/overflow plan (fallback to hosted + model variant quantization, warm KV cache strategy).
- Ops runbook notes: cold-start cache warming, autoscale thresholds, target queue latency.

Ben — can you run the batch of perf numbers and produce the initial Excel model? Sergio — please flag any runtime knobs (KV cache hit ops, quantization adjustments) that would materially reduce GPU needs. Lauren — I'll need a commercial blurb and suggested commitment term and price uplift for headroom.

Deadline: can we have a first-pass by EOD tomorrow PST?

Thanks — Aisha

--
Aisha Rahman
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: latency-model-v2.xlsx (stub)]

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwoodinference.com>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio_alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 08 Jun 2027 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Sierra demand profile + sizing ask — recommended slab and headroom

Team — ran a quick model (attached) and summarized below. Assumptions and rationale first, then recommended committed slab.

Assumptions (baseline):
- Baseline serving config: redwood-open-7b-q4 on A100-80GB nodes, single model instance per GPU with dynamic KV caching.
- Measured median tokens/sec per GPU (our internal microbench for 80-token average, multi-turn mix): ~3,800 tokens/sec sustained per A100-80GB for the observed chat-heavy mix (this includes inference + kv cache overhead).
- For longer sequences (avg 80 -> p95 includes occasional longer tails), effective per-GPU sustained drops to ~3,200 tps when accounting for 10% extra model context processing and network overhead.
- Burst handling: we plan for 2.5x burst absorb capacity via pre-warmed host fallback pools + autoscale. Pure committed slab should be sized for sustained 95th load with a safety multiplier of 1.25x for ops variance.

Simple math (sustained):
- Required sustained GPUs = ceil(45,000 tokens/sec / 3,200 tokens/sec_per_gpu) = ceil(14.06) => 15 GPUs to cover 95th sustained without safety buffer.
- With safety multiplier 1.25 => 15 * 1.25 = 18.75 => recommend reserving 19 A100-80GB GPUs for committed slab.

Burst plan (140k observed):
- Committed slab (19 GPUs) provides ~60.8k tokens/sec sustained (19 * 3,200).
- For bursts to 140k, rely on a) warm hosted fallback pool sized to absorb remaining ~79k tokens/sec; b) autoscale burst nodes (ephemeral) and prioritized queueing for non-critical routes. Expect short p95 degradations during longest tails; recommend SLA language for burst-mode latency tolerance.

Latency fit:
- With 19 GPUs and KV cache warm, we expect p95 model-inference latency inside 160–190 ms; network/egress budget 40–90 ms depending on region/peering. Combined end-to-end p95 estimate ~220–280 ms. To meet strict 250 ms p95 target across all edge cases, we suggest:
  - Keep 19 GPUs committed, and maintain a small warm standby of 3 extra GPUs (hot-warm) capable of absorbing stateful sessions immediately. This pushes committed+standby to 22 GPUs and gives extra headroom for p95.

Ops notes / runbook highlights:
- Warm KV cache policy: pre-warm KV for top 20% of active session IDs (sourced from their session telemetry) during peak windows; this can recover ~12–18% effective tokens/sec improvement.
- Autoscale thresholds: trigger ephemeral scale when queue latency > 60ms for > 30s. Use queue-first throttling for non-interactive routes.
- Model fallback: offer a slightly faster quantized variant (redwood-open-7b-q4-fast) for degraded tail conditions; quality tests show ~2% NLL degradation but 20–25% higher tokens/sec.

Commercial suggestion (Lauren): recommend 12-month committed slab of 19 GPUs with ability to burst to hosted capacity. Price uplift for standby 3 GPUs included in an enhanced SLA tier. Suggest proposal: 19 committed GPUs + 3 hot-standby for guaranteed p95 <=250ms, with defined burst fallback to hosted at defined ingress rates.

Attachments: latency-model-v2.xlsx (attached with per-hour sims).

If you want I can run a more granular per-route simulation (per-route qps and session affinity) tomorrow — that will likely move the GPU requirement by +/-2 GPUs depending on cache hit optimizations.

Ben
Director, Capacity Planning, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: latency-model-v2.xlsx (XLSX, 76KB)]
text:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
To: Melissa Cheng <melissa@hypersight.ai>
Date: Sat, 05 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: initial sizing numbers for Dedicated commit

Hi Melissa — good to connect last week. Quick follow-up: to build a committed Dedicated proposal and committed capacity pricing for Sierra, can you confirm the following items and share any attachment you have for traffic profiles?

1) Expected 95th-percentile sustained tokens/sec during peak windows (per-organization) and typical burst multiplier (e.g., 3x short bursts).
2) Average request generation length (tokens) and max expected generation length.
3) Percent of requests that are chat (multi-turn) vs single-prompt inference.
4) Required latency SLOs (p95 and p99) for interactive flows; batch/async tolerances if any.
5) Any regulatory/regional restrictions for data residency.

If you can attach a CSV or a simple spreadsheet that shows hourly TPS/tokens for a week, that would be ideal — we use that to simulate batching/caching effects in our latency model.

I’ve copied Ben (capacity), Sergio (runtime optimizations), and Lauren (commercial) so we can run a quick runbook after we get the numbers.

Thanks — Aisha

--
Aisha Rahman
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
[aisha@redwoodinference.com]
[Attachment: request-checklist.pdf (stub)]

source.subject: Webinar replay + prioritized hot list + SDR claim plan
messages:
From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: events-platform <noreply@eventplatform.com>
Date: 2027-06-05 09:05 -0700
Subject: Post-webinar package: attendee export, hotlist, replay link

Team —

Thanks for the quick turnaround on yesterday's "Scaling Inference" webinar. Attached are the three artifacts you asked for: attendee export, our prioritized hot list (MQLs -> PQLs flagged), and the replay packet with slide timestamps and key quote clips. High-level guidance below so SDRs can act today: 

- Hot list: 48 accounts flagged as "high" (column: priority="H"). Focus on accounts marked with a green check in the "on-platform" column (we already have cloud entitlement data).
- Recommended action by priority:
  * H: Outreach + 30-minute discovery (offer demo window)
  * M: Nurture touch, send focused snippet from the replay + content pack
  * L: Add to quarterly nurture list
- Suggested demo SLO: book 40% of H list within 7 business days.
- Routing: regional owners first, then overflow queue (see mapping in the sheet).

Replay link (share with attendees only): https://redwoodinference.com/replay/20270604-enterprise-econ
Drive folder with assets: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-redwood-webinar-20270604

Attachments:
- attendee_export_20270604.csv (email, company, role, event engagement score)
- hotlist_priority_20270604.xlsx (routing, priority, notes)
- replay_packet_20270604.pdf (clips + suggested outreach snippets)

Next steps / asks:
- Aditya: please confirm SDR claim plan for H accounts and how you'll split overflow.
- Ben: take the west-coast H list and start booking.
- Marissa: push replay emails to attendees who registered but didn't watch (use snippets from the packet).

I'll be available for clarifying any lead signals.

Thanks —
Lauren Bishop
Head of Events, Redwood Inference
lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0132

--
Note: external attendee contacts included only for reference in the export; do not add external contacts to internal trackers without consent.

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-06-05 10:12 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-webinar package: attendee export, hotlist, replay link

Thanks Lauren — this is perfect. Confirming plan and adding a few operational details so SDRs have clear acceptance criteria. Quoting your action items below for context.

On 2027-06-05 09:05, Lauren Bishop wrote:
> Team —
> 
> Thanks for the quick turnaround on yesterday's "Scaling Inference" webinar. Attached are the three artifacts you asked for: attendee export, our prioritized hot list (MQLs -> PQLs flagged), and the replay packet with slide timestamps and key quote clips....

My plan: 
- I'll carve the H list into two buckets: West (Ben) and East (me + overflow pool). Please see the routing column in hotlist_priority_20270604.xlsx; it's pre-populated for obvious owners.
- Acceptance criteria for a claim: SDR must leave a logged activity in HubSpot with the template "WEBINAR_H_CLAIM" + one-sentence discovery note. If no activity after 48 hours, auto-escalate to me and Marissa.
- Demo invitation template: short 3-line intro, 1-sentence value prop tied to the clip timecode, and 2 proposed slots. Use DocuSign calendar link for booking when the prospect asks for more details.
- I'll take accounts with existing Cloud entitlements flagged in the sheet (column: cloud_entitled = yes). Ben, please take the west-coast list and start outreach this afternoon.

Ben / Marissa — confirm which accounts you claim (reply-all and list company names) so Lauren can mark them in the hotlist sheet.

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Sales Manager, Enterprise Accounts
aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com
(415) 555-0199

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-06-05 10:35 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-webinar package: attendee export, hotlist, replay link

Claiming the following H accounts (west bucket):
- FinSight Analytics
- DataHarbor Inc.
- Optima Labs
- ClearEdge Systems

I'll start outreach now and log activities with the "WEBINAR_H_CLAIM" tag in HubSpot. If any prospect replies asking for technical specifics, loop in Marissa for the demo or engineer intro.

Ben Carter
SDR, West Region
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-06-05 11:05 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-webinar package: attendee export, hotlist, replay link

Adding schedule availability for demo handoffs. I've created a shared calendar block and a Calendly-style sign-up sheet for booked demos (link in Drive). I'll take the east-coast H accounts flagged with "senior-ml" in the role column.

Quick note: I updated the hotlist sheet and added two comments on FinSight (high executive engagement, ask: customer wanted on-prem quantization details). Lauren, please surface that clip (slide 12, 3:14) in the outbound email.

Forwarded attachment snippet for easy reference:
> attendee_export_20270604.csv — rows 123-129: FinSight Analytics, Sophie Green, VP Data, engagement score 78, cloud_entitled=yes

I'll book first-wave demos for the next 5 business days and share meeting links in the HubSpot activities.

Marissa Cole
Senior Solutions Engineer
marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-06-05 16:22 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-webinar package: attendee export, hotlist, replay link

Perfect — thanks for the quick claims and notes. A few follow-ups: 

- Ben: thanks for the west list. Please add your booked demo times to the shared sheet by EOD tomorrow so we can reconcile capacity.
- Marissa: appreciate the clip callouts — I'll update the outbound template with the exact timestamp and suggested subject lines.
- Aditya: once the 48-hour escalation guard rails kick in, please run the escalation cadence and we'll loop in CS if we see SLA risk.

I'll close the loop on the external replay email (we'll send a targeted snippet to high-engagement attendees tomorrow AM). Lauren out.

—
Lauren Bishop
Head of Events, Redwood Inference
lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0132

source.subject: Throughput assurance & measurement plan for Dedicated commit - Acme AI
source.messages_1:
From: Tom Reyes <tom.reyes@acme.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-30T08:12:00-07:00
Subject: Throughput assurance for Dedicated commit

Alex / Marissa,

Thanks again for the demo last week — Acme is excited about moving from a pilot to a reserved GPU commit. Before we get to signatures the procurement and engineering teams need clear language about the throughput guarantees and how we will measure them. High-level asks from our side:

- A guaranteed sustained tokens/sec figure (and how that maps to requests/sec for a mix of short/long prompts).
- Latency SLOs (median and p95) and whether they’re per-request or per-token.
- The measurement methodology: what harness you run, how long the verification window is, and who provides traffic samples.
- What evidence we get (logs, traces, CSV exports) to validate the run.

We also want to understand how these guarantees affect pricing. Acme is willing to commit to a 12‑month reserved pool but needs a clear banding for discounts tied to verified throughput. Can you send a short playbook we can circulate with procurement + infra?

Thanks,
Tom
Tom Reyes
Head of Platform Engineering, Acme AI
procurement@acme.ai

P.S. attaching a short summary of our expected model mix and a traffic sample link (in the next email) — we can provide a 6‑hour sample of production traffic for replay if that helps.
source.messages_2:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Tom Reyes <tom.reyes@acme.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-30T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Throughput assurance for Dedicated commit

Tom — thanks, really appreciate the clear asks. I’m looping Ben (solutions) and Marissa (sales) so we can cover methodology + commercial terms in one thread. Below is a concise playbook we use for Dedicated throughput assurance. I’m attaching the measurement template (redwood_dedicated_measurement_template.xlsx) for your team to fill in observed traffic characteristics.

Summary (short):
- Guarantee: sustained token-rate guarantee expressed as "sustained tokens/sec per committed GPU pool" for the agreed model profile(s). We translate that into an expected requests/sec range for the customer’s provided model mix during verification.
- Latency targets: p50 and p95 measured as token-emission latency (time from request acceptance to token streaming start) and overall request completion. We publish both.
- Measurement window: two-stage verification (synthetic steady-state + replay):
  1) Synthetic baseline: 30-minute steady-state run at target QPS/tokens with KV cache warmed, continuous batching enabled. Purpose: validate runtime steady-state and kernel choices.
  2) Replay verification: replay of a customer-provided traffic capture (we recommend a 6‑hour sample) at 1x speed and, if requested, a targeted 3x stress pass to validate autoscaling/burst behavior. For acceptance we require the replay 1x pass to meet token-rate and latency SLOs for >= 90% of non-overlapping 10-minute subwindows.
- Evidence bundle: CSV token/time series, per-request latency histogram, node-level CPU/GPU utilization, and a short report with screenshots of Console dashboards. We’ll push the bundle to a shared S3/Drive link.
- Acceptance criteria: sustained tokens/sec >= 95% of committed target across the 30-min synthetic run AND replay 1x meeting p95 latency target for >= 90% of 10-min subwindows. If either fails we run a single remediation pass (configuration tuning: batching, KV cache tuning, quantization profile). A failure after remediation can trigger a priced credit (see commercial section).

Measurement specifics we propose for Acme (please confirm):
- Models: mix = 60% short-chat (<=128 tokens), 30% multi-turn assistant (avg 512 tokens), 10% long-form summarization (avg 2k tokens).
- Target sustained rate: 550k tokens/sec aggregate for the committed GPU pool (maps to approximate 1,200–1,800 requests/sec depending on mix; we’ll produce a per-model RPS mapping in the template).
- Latency SLOs: p50 < 80ms, p95 < 350ms for short-chat; p95 < 700ms for multi-turn; long-form measured on completion time p95 < 2.0s. All measurements to be taken on-client-to-host path from our edge within the region.

Commercial tie-ins:
- Discounting: for a 12-month reserved commit at current sizing (N GPUs), we can offer a tiered discount schedule tied to committed tokens/sec bands. If verification shows we can deliver >= 110% of the committed tokens/sec, we can move Acme one discount tier higher for months 4–12. If verification fails after remediation and the failure is due to runtime limits (not customer traffic shape misconfiguration), Redwood will issue a 1% monthly credit on committed fees for the month of failure. Details in the attached commit_terms_v1.docx.

Next steps I suggest:
1) Acme completes the attached measurement template with observed model mix and provides the 6‑hour traffic sample link.
2) We schedule a 2‑hour verification window (synthetic + replay) — Redwood runs it during a mutually agreed maintenance window.
3) If successful, DocuSign link for the reserved commit and SLA addendum.

Happy to jump on a quick call to walk through the template this afternoon.

— Alex Martinez
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0122

Attachment: redwood_dedicated_measurement_template.xlsx
source.messages_3:
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Tom Reyes <tom.reyes@acme.ai>
Cc: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-31T09:18:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Throughput assurance for Dedicated commit

Tom — following up on Alex's methodology with the commercial framing. We can be flexible on band sizing; below is the pricing ladder we think matches the technical playbook above. This is intentionally compact so procurement has something to circulate: 

Pricing ladder (examples, final terms in Doc):
- Band A (base) — commit size = N GPUs => list price. Baseline guarantee = 450k tokens/sec. Discount = 15% off list.
- Band B (preferred) — commit size = 1.5x N => guarantee = 600k tokens/sec. Discount = 22% off list.
- Band C (enterprise) — commit size >= 2x N => guarantee = 900k tokens/sec. Discount = 30% off list + 4 weeks onboarding credit.

Verification & credits: if the verification run meets >= 110% of the committed guarantee, we will apply an incremental refund (effectively bumping you to the next band pricing) starting month 4; if verification fails due to Redwood platform limits after remediation, we will apply a 1% fee credit for the failure month. If failure is due to customer-provided traffic shape not matching template, we will work with Acme to adapt pricing but credits would not apply.

Contract logistics: we’ve added the verification and credit language to commit_terms_v1.docx (attached). Once Alex confirms the measurement template looks good and we have the traffic sample, I’ll send the DocuSign envelope for signature.

Thanks — Marissa
Marissa Cole
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

Attachment: commit_terms_v1.docx
source.messages_4:
From: Tom Reyes <tom.reyes@acme.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-06-02T15:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Throughput assurance for Dedicated commit

Marissa / Alex — thanks, that layout is helpful. A few quick clarifications before we fill the template and schedule the run: 

1) Measurement models: we want two replay passes during verification — one that represents production shape (1x) and a shorter 3x stress of only the short-chat and multi-turn mix to validate burst behavior. Will Redwood treat the stress pass as informational only (i.e., not count it against acceptance)?

2) Ramp plan: Acme prefers a 4‑week ramp to full committed traffic after sign (week 1: 20%, week 2: 50%, week 3: 80%, week 4: 100%). Will the verification be run against expected full traffic or earlier in the ramp? We’d like verification to be against full (100%) traffic.

3) Evidence exports: please confirm the CSV schema for per-request fields we’ll receive (timestamp, model_id, tokens_in, tokens_out, latency_ms, node_id). Also confirm retention window for these exported artifacts (we need them for our compliance team).

We’ll drop the 6‑hour sample into our link (https://acme.ai/traffic-sample/2027-05-week1) and fill the template by tomorrow. If those 3 clarifications are acceptable we can lock a verification window for June 8th.

On Thu, May 30, 2027 at 10:03 AM Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Tom — thanks, really appreciate the clear asks. I’m looping Ben (solutions) and Marissa (sales) so we can cover methodology + commercial terms in one thread. I’m attaching the measurement template (redwood_dedicated_measurement_template.xlsx) for your team to fill in observed traffic characteristics.
> 
> Summary (short):
> - Guarantee: sustained token-rate guarantee expressed as "sustained tokens/sec per committed GPU pool" for the agreed model profile(s). We translate that into an expected requests/sec range for the customer’s provided model mix during verification.
> - Latency targets: p50 and p95 measured as token-emission latency (time from request acceptance to token streaming start) and overall request completion. We publish both.
> - Measurement window: two-stage verification (synthetic steady-state + replay):
>   1) Synthetic baseline: 30-minute steady-state run at target QPS/tokens with KV cache warmed, continuous batching enabled. Purpose: validate runtime steady-state and kernel choices.
>   2) Replay verification: replay of a customer-provided traffic capture (we recommend a 6‑hour sample) at 1x speed and, if requested, a targeted 3x stress pass to validate autoscaling/burst behavior. For acceptance we require the replay 1x pass to meet token-rate and latency SLOs for >= 90% of non-overlapping 10-minute subwindows.
> - Evidence bundle: CSV token/time series, per-request latency histogram, node-level CPU/GPU utilization, and a short report with screenshots of Console dashboards. We’ll push the bundle to a shared S3/Drive link.
> - Acceptance criteria: sustained tokens/sec >= 95% of committed target across the 30-min synthetic run AND replay 1x meeting p95 latency target for >= 90% of 10-min subwindows. If either fails we run a single remediation pass (configuration tuning: batching, KV cache tuning, quantization profile). A failure after remediation can trigger a priced credit (see commercial section).
> 
> — Alex Martinez
> Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
source.notes: Verification window proposed for June 8th pending template and sample. Acme to fill template and confirm ramp plan. Redwood to confirm CSV schema and evidence retention.

From: Julia Park <julia.park@zenithai.com>

Date: 2027-06-01T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Redwood exec workshop — SF week options, AV & procurement needs

Hi Aisha,

Thanks again for the quick intro call yesterday. Were keen to get a weeklong exec workshop scheduled in San Francisco (Redwood office or nearby venue) in August — target week of Aug 9. High-level: 12-15 onsite execs + ~6 remote participants for daily panels.

Primary asks so we can start procurement:
- Confirm preferred week/dates (we have some internal travel windows)
- Hotel room block recommendations + approximate per-night budget (corporate rate)
- Whether Redwood can issue an invoice/PO to speed travel bookings or we should route through our procurement team
- AV requirements (mics, streaming setup, recording) and whether Redwood has a preferred AV vendor
- Visa/attendance letters for two APAC attendees (need invitation wording)
- Any standard COVID/vaccination or security entry checks?

Attaching our initial workshop requirements spreadsheet. Would appreciate a short run-through of proposed dates and an initial cost estimate so procurement can start a PO. Also please loop in whoever will be our onsite contact.

Thanks — look forward to aligning.

Best,
Julia

Head of Strategic Programs, ZenithAI

Fantastic — wed love to host. Helpful answers inline and next steps below.

Dates: Available windows at our SF office: Aug 9-13 OR Aug 16-20. Were proposing Aug 10-12 for a compact two full-day + half-day wrap (gives your team travel buffers). Please confirm preferred week by EOD Tuesday so we can lock venue and hotel blocks.

Hotel + travel: We can coordinate hotel blocks with our preferred partner (Hilton Embarcadero and Marriott near Market St). Estimated corporate rates: $280-350/night depending on room type. If ZenithAI prefers to run travel through your procurement, please share the procurement contact and PO timeline. If youd like Redwood to book on your behalf, we can accept a PO or a charge-back arrangement.

AV & streaming: Ben (ccd) is our AV lead and will share an AV matrix. We support live stream + recording; recommended vendor for larger rooms is StudioCables (local).

Visa letters/security: We can provide formal invitation/attendance letters for visa. Let us know full legal names and passport expiry dates for the two APAC travelers. No additional COVID checks required at this time.

Onsite host & logistics: Marissa will be the onsite lead for venue coordination and catering. Shell share a travel manifest template and hotel block deadlines.

Attached: draft agenda (v1) for your review. Next steps:
1) Confirm preferred week (Aug 10-12 or Aug 17-19)
2) Share procurement contact/PO timing and any billing address requirements
3) Provide visa names ASAP if needed

Thanks — Ill target a short call Wed afternoon if that works for you to finalize.

Warmly,
Aisha Rahman
Director, Solutions - Redwood Inference

Hi Aisha,

Primary asks so we can start procurement:
- Confirm preferred week/dates (we have some internal travel windows)
- Hotel room block recommendations + approximate per-night budget (corporate rate)
- Whether Redwood can issue an invoice/PO to speed travel bookings or we should route through our procurement team
- AV requirements (mics, streaming setup, recording) and whether Redwood has a preferred AV vendor
- Visa/attendance letters for two APAC attendees (need invitation wording)
- Any standard COVID/vaccination or security entry checks?

Attaching our initial workshop requirements spreadsheet. Would appreciate a short run-through of proposed dates and an initial cost estimate so procurement can start a PO. Also please loop in whoever will be our onsite contact.

Thanks — look forward to aligning.

Best,
Julia
Julia Park
Head of Strategic Programs, ZenithAI
[Attachment: zenithai_requirements.xlsx (Excel, 42KB)]
