Hi Julia,

Fantastic — wed love to host. Helpful answers inline and next steps below.

Dates: Available windows at our SF office: Aug 9-13 OR Aug 16-20. Were proposing Aug 10-12 for a compact two full-day + half-day wrap (gives your team travel buffers). Please confirm preferred week by EOD Tuesday so we can lock venue and hotel blocks.

Hotel + travel: We can coordinate hotel blocks with our preferred partner (Hilton Embarcadero and Marriott near Market St). Estimated corporate rates: $280-350/night depending on room type. If ZenithAI prefers to run travel through your procurement, please share the procurement contact and PO timeline. If youd like Redwood to book on your behalf, we can accept a PO or a charge-back arrangement.

Procurement: Please loop in procurement@zenithai.com (and any internal PO number). Our commercial team will prepare a simple SOW + invoice to attach to PO. Deal reference: Z-2027-0843.

AV & streaming: Ben (ccd) is our AV lead and will share an AV matrix. We support live stream + recording; recommended vendor for larger rooms is StudioCables (local).

Visa letters/security: We can provide formal invitation/attendance letters for visa. Let us know full legal names and passport expiry dates for the two APAC travelers. No additional COVID checks required at this time.

Onsite host & logistics: Marissa will be the onsite lead for venue coordination and catering. Shell share a travel manifest template and hotel block deadlines.

Attached: draft agenda (v1) for your review. Next steps:
1) Confirm preferred week (Aug 10-12 or Aug 17-19)
2) Share procurement contact/PO timing and any billing address requirements
3) Provide visa names ASAP if needed

Thanks — Ill target a short call Wed afternoon if that works for you to finalize.

Warmly,
Aisha Rahman
Director, Solutions - Redwood Inference
[Attachment: draft_agenda_v1.pdf (PDF, 78KB)]

source.subject: SIG questionnaire follow-up: Nova Systems – control mapping & evidence
messages:
From: Elena Torres <procurement@novasystems.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: James Li <james.li@novasystems.com>
Date: Sat, 29 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SIG questionnaire submission + two follow-ups

Hi Aisha,

Thanks again for taking our SIG request — we uploaded the completed questionnaire (attached) but have a couple of open items before we can present to our InfoSec committee next week. Two quick asks: 1) can you confirm where the key rotation procedures and evidence live for customer encryption keys (we're particularly interested in HSM usage and rotation cadence), and 2) please confirm data residency / regional isolation options for Dedicated deployments (we need an EU-only option for a subset of traffic).

I've attached our completed SIG_Questionnaire_NovaSys.xlsx and the Vendor_Risk_Form (for procurement). If helpful, James and I are available Wednesday morning for a 30-minute clarifying call.

Appreciate the quick turnaround — our timeline is tight.

Best,
Elena Torres
Senior Procurement Manager
Nova Systems
procurement@novasystems.com

Attachments: SIG_Questionnaire_NovaSys.xlsx (spreadsheet), Vendor_Risk_Form.pdf (pdf)
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Elena Torres <procurement@novasystems.com>
Cc: James Li <james.li@novasystems.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 29 May 2027 10:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SIG questionnaire submission + two follow-ups

Hi Elena — received, thanks.

Quick ack: we reviewed the uploaded questionnaire and noted the two items you called out. I've looped in Karthik (security & infrastructure) for the HSM/key rotation specifics and Ben for evidence packaging (SOC 2 artefacts, diagrams).

Proposed mutual action plan:
- By EOD Monday (05/31): we will send a control mapping spreadsheet with line-by-line answers populated (I attached our SIG_Control_Mapping_Template.xlsx to this thread).
- By Wednesday (06/02): deliver the requested artefacts (HSM architecture diagram, rotation policy excerpt, and a short statement on regional isolation options for Dedicated).
- If any items need legal/redline review (procurement form), Ben will coordinate with Legal and get back to you by Thursday.

Would you like a 30-minute call Wednesday morning to review the mapping and any exceptions? If so, propose 10:00–10:30 PT.

Thanks — Aisha
Aisha Rahman
Head of Sales, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
aisha_rahman@redwood.com

Attachments: SIG_Control_Mapping_Template.xlsx (xlsx)
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>, Elena Torres <procurement@novasystems.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 31 May 2027 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SIG questionnaire submission + two follow-ups

Elena / Aisha — following up with the technical mapping and answers to the two items you highlighted. I populated the template with concise responses; below are the key excerpts and where to find evidence. Full mapping is in the shared Drive link.

1) Customer key management / HSM usage
- Control summary: Customer data at rest is encrypted using customer-managed keys (CKMS) or Redwood-managed keys depending on plan. For Dedicated/private deployments we support KMS integrations and HSM-backed key storage.
- Rotation policy: Root/intermediate key rotation cadence: annual; Data-encryption key (DEK) rotation: in-place on rekey or compromise; automated key versioning via our KMS connectors.
- Evidence: HSM-arch-diagram.pdf (architecture diagram), rotation-policy-excerpt.txt (policy excerpt), and key-rotation audit sample (redacted) are in the Drive folder: https://drive.redwood.com/shared/NOVA-SIG-2027/artifacts/key-management

2) Regional isolation (Dedicated)
- We support region-specific Dedicated pools (EU-only, US-only). Networking: VPC peering or PrivateLink options; egress controls can be limited to a single cloud region.
- Evidence: deployment-topology-eu.png and our Dedicated SLA excerpt. See Drive: https://drive.redwood.com/shared/NOVA-SIG-2027/artifacts/deployment

Notes and exceptions:
- For Hosted (shared) customers, token caching within the runtime may persist KV prefixes for short periods to optimize latency; caching TTLs are configurable and we can provide a policy statement.
- We need to confirm the legal/contractual language Nova requires for customer-managed KMS—Ben, can you pick this up with Procurement?

Happy to jump on the Wednesday call if you want a short walkthrough of the key-management folder.

-Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Director, Infrastructure Security
karthik_iyer@redwood.com
From: Elena Torres <procurement@novasystems.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: James Li <james.li@novasystems.com>
Date: Mon, 31 May 2027 11:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SIG questionnaire submission + two follow-ups

Karthik — this is helpful, thanks. A couple of very specific follow-ups before our InfoSec review:

- Can you provide an excerpt showing the HSM vendor (Yubico/Thales/CloudHSM) and whether keys are tenant-separated vs multi-tenant within the HSM cluster? Our auditors require tenant separation for customer-managed keys.
- For the KV cache TTL you mentioned: please provide the default TTL value, how it's configured per-customer, and a short statement we can include in the risk form that says whether cached prefixes contain plaintext or only encrypted tokens.

I've attached our Vendor_Risk_Form.pdf with the relevant sections highlighted. If you can drop the two items into the Drive folder by COB today, that would keep us on schedule.

Also, we confirmed internally that Nova will require EU-only Dedicated routing for a pilot set of traffic — can you confirm a test timeline for spinning up an EU-only pool? We expect to start pilot traffic week of 6/14, so needs to be available by 6/10.

Thanks — Elena

Attachments: Vendor_Risk_Form.pdf (pdf)
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Elena Torres <procurement@novasystems.com>, James Li <james.li@novasystems.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 31 May 2027 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SIG questionnaire submission + two follow-ups

Elena — appreciate the quick clarifying notes and the Vendor_Risk_Form. Summary / next steps on our side:

Action items we committed to (recap):
- Karthik: upload HSM vendor & tenancy statement + a short TTL/caching statement to Drive (target: COB today) — artifacts will be in https://drive.redwood.com/shared/NOVA-SIG-2027/artifacts/key-management
- Ben: review procurement/Vendor_Risk_Form and propose contract language for customer-managed KMS (target: 6/3)
- Monica (Ops): confirm EU-only Dedicated pool spin-up timeline and test readiness (target: 6/10); she will also share a brief checklist for network peering and egress controls.
- Aisha: schedule the Wednesday 10:00 PT call and send calendar invite (invite sent).

If anything slips, we'll flag and propose alternate dates. Otherwise, we'll plan to surface the assembled evidence and a 15–20 minute walkthrough during Wednesday's call.

Thanks all — look forward to wrapping this up quickly.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Head of Sales, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
aisha_rahman@redwood.com

Calendar invite: Redwood/Nova SIG clarifying call — Wed 06/02 10:00–10:30 PT

- We support region-specific Dedicated pools (EU-only, US-only). Networking: VPC peering or PrivateLink options; egress controls can be limited to a single cloud region.
- Evidence: deployment-topology-eu.png and our Dedicated SLA excerpt. See Drive: https://drive.redwood.com/shared/NOVA-SIG-2027/artifacts/deployment

Notes and exceptions:
- For Hosted (shared) customers, token caching within the runtime may persist KV prefixes for short periods to optimize latency; caching TTLs are configurable and we can provide a policy statement.
- We need to confirm the legal/contractual language Nova requires for customer-managed KMS—Ben, can you pick this up with Procurement?

Happy to jump on the Wednesday call if you want a short walkthrough of the key-management folder.

-Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Director, Infrastructure Security
karthik_iyer@redwood.com


From: Elena Torres <procurement@novasystems.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: James Li <james.li@novasystems.com>
Date: Mon, 31 May 2027 11:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SIG questionnaire submission + two follow-ups

Karthik — this is helpful, thanks. A couple of very specific follow-ups before our InfoSec review:

- Can you provide an excerpt showing the HSM vendor (Yubico/Thales/CloudHSM) and whether keys are tenant-separated vs multi-tenant within the HSM cluster? Our auditors require tenant separation for customer-managed keys.
- For the KV cache TTL you mentioned: please provide the default TTL value, how it's configured per-customer, and a short statement we can include in the risk form that says whether cached prefixes contain plaintext or only encrypted tokens.

I've attached our Vendor_Risk_Form.pdf with the relevant sections highlighted. If you can drop the two items into the Drive folder by COB today, that would keep us on schedule.

Also, we confirmed internally that Nova will require EU-only Dedicated routing for a pilot set of traffic — can you confirm a test timeline for spinning up an EU-only pool? We expect to start pilot traffic week of 6/14, so needs to be available by 6/10.

Thanks — Elena

Attachments: Vendor_Risk_Form.pdf (pdf)


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Elena Torres <procurement@novasystems.com>, James Li <james.li@novasystems.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 31 May 2027 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SIG questionnaire submission + two follow-ups

Elena — appreciate the quick clarifying notes and the Vendor_Risk_Form. Summary / next steps on our side:

Action items we committed to (recap):
- Karthik: upload HSM vendor & tenancy statement + a short TTL/caching statement to Drive (target: COB today) — artifacts will be in https://drive.redwood.com/shared/NOVA-SIG-2027/artifacts/key-management
- Ben: review procurement/Vendor_Risk_Form and propose contract language for customer-managed KMS (target: 6/3)
- Monica (Ops): confirm EU-only Dedicated pool spin-up timeline and test readiness (target: 6/10); she will also share a brief checklist for network peering and egress controls.
- Aisha: schedule the Wednesday 10:00 PT call and send calendar invite (invite sent).

If anything slips, we'll flag and propose alternate dates. Otherwise, we'll plan to surface the assembled evidence and a 15–20 minute walkthrough during Wednesday's call.

Thanks all — look forward to wrapping this up quickly.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Head of Sales, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
aisha_rahman@redwood.com

Calendar invite: Redwood/Nova SIG clarifying call — Wed 06/02 10:00–10:30 PT

source.subject: SIG / CAIQ annual refresh — request to escrow critical artifacts in Vendr
messages:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jared Lin <jared@cloudco.com>
Cc: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@cloudco.com>, Kevin Ortiz <kevin.ortiz@procure.cloudco.com>, Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 21 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SIG / CAIQ annual refresh — request to escrow critical artifacts in Vendr

Hi Jared / Priya —

Following up on last quarter's intake: CloudCo is in scope for our annual SIG / CAIQ refresh and SOC 2 evidence reconciliation. To avoid last-minute gaps during their audit window, we prefer to have a small set of critical artifacts placed in escrow (read-only, time-limited) so the security reviewers can access them without repeated downloads.

What we're asking for: a one-time upload of the items listed in the attached SIG-CAIQ-Refresh-Checklist.xlsx and the signed evidence escrow form (attached). Specific asks:
- CAIQ answers snapshot (export JSON or Excel)
- SOC 2: latest security monitoring configuration docs + redacted incident log for last 12 months
- Key SSO metadata (IdP SAML certs) and MFA policy screenshots
- Any compensating control narratives for data isolation we can reference

Preferred upload targets (pick one):
1) Vendr profile upload (recommended) — we will grant a time-limited uploader token and then lock the files into a read-only escrow folder.
2) S3 staging bucket (private link) — we will provide a pre-signed URL and verify checksum.

Timeline: can you get the bundle uploaded by EOD Tue, 25 May? Our audit window for the reviewer is 26–28 May and we want everything staged ahead of time.

Attachments: SIG-CAIQ-Refresh-Checklist.xlsx, evidence-escrow-request-form.pdf, vendr-upload-instructions.txt

Thanks — let me know if you need a short walkthrough.

—
Aditya Rao
Head of Sales, Enterprise | Redwood Inference
aditya@redwoodinference.com | (415) 555-0173

From: Jared Lin <jared@cloudco.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@cloudco.com>, Kevin Ortiz <kevin.ortiz@procure.cloudco.com>
Date: Fri, 21 May 2027 10:03:12 -0700
Subject: Re: SIG / CAIQ annual refresh — request to escrow critical artifacts in Vendr

Aditya —

Thanks for the clear checklist. We can do this but need clarification on two items:
1) Escrow vs portal: our policy prefers the vendor portal (Vendr) for questionnaire answers but we typically push SOC2 artifacts to our S3 retention bucket for legal reasons. Will Redwood accept the S3 staging approach (pre-signed URL) for SOC2 PDFs only, while we upload CAIQ answers and the questionnaire snapshot to Vendr?
2) Redaction guidance: do you need the incident log fully redacted for customer PII or is a redacted summary sufficient?

We aim to have the CAIQ snapshot and questionnaire answers in Vendr by Tue 25 May; SOC2 PDFs might slip to Wed 26 May if we must run redaction through legal.

Thanks — Jared
Jared Lin
Security & Compliance Manager | CloudCo Solutions
jared@cloudco.com

On Fri, May 21, 2027 at 09:12 AM Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Following up on last quarter's intake: CloudCo is in scope for our annual SIG / CAIQ refresh and SOC 2 evidence reconciliation.
>

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 24 May 2027 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: RE: SIG / CAIQ annual refresh — request to escrow critical artifacts in Vendr

Aditya / Ben —

Internal playbook we will follow:
- Provision time-limited uploader role on Vendr for CloudCo (72h TTL). Token will be scoped to the "evidence-escrow" folder; once upload completes we'll change ACLs to read-only and attach the escrow-form.pdf.
- For SOC2 PDFs: we accept the S3 staging approach. Procurement (Kevin) will be looped for the transfer; provide pre-signed PUT URLs for the bucket: s3://redwood-escrow/cloudco/2027-05-soc2/ (we'll verify SHA256).
- Redaction: legal will accept a redacted incident log with PII removed plus an unredacted internal index (access-controlled). If CloudCo cannot provide that, we can accept redacted summaries with dates and severity buckets.

I'll generate the Vendr upload token and the S3 pre-signed URLs by EOD today and paste the details into the secure Doc (link below). Ben — please run the checksum verification after upload and confirm with Kevin.

Related links: https://vendr.example.com/orgs/cloudco/profiles/redwood-inference

Naomi
Director, Security & GRC | Redwood Inference
naomi_feldman@redwoodinference.com

> On Fri, May 21, 2027 at 10:03 AM Jared Lin <jared@cloudco.com> wrote:
> > Aditya —
> >
> > Thanks for the clear checklist. We can do this but need clarification on two items...

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jared Lin <jared@cloudco.com>
Cc: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@cloudco.com>, Kevin Ortiz <kevin.ortiz@procure.cloudco.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>, Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 24 May 2027 16:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SIG / CAIQ annual refresh — request to escrow critical artifacts in Vendr

Jared / Priya / Kevin —

Appreciate the quick turn. Confirming the mixed approach is fine: CAIQ and questionnaire snapshot to Vendr; SOC2 PDFs to S3 pre-signed URL. Naomi will post the upload token and the S3 URLs into the DocuSign-secured checklist by EOD. Please note:

- Vendr token: single-use uploader, TTL 72h. Look for an email with the subject "Vendr Uploader Token — Redwood / CloudCo" from naomi_feldman@redwoodinference.com.
- S3 PUT URLs: will be generated for the path s3://redwood-escrow/cloudco/2027-05-soc2/ and should be used only for the files named in the checklist (SOC2-report-redacted.pdf, security-monitoring-config.pdf).
- Once upload is complete, we'll change ACLs to read-only and provide the auditor a short-lived download token (14 days TTL).

Thanks for working with us on this. If redaction causes a slip to Wed 26 May for SOC2 PDFs, that is acceptable — we will still lock the Vendr CAIQ snapshot on Tue 25 May.

Best —
Aditya
Head of Sales, Enterprise | Redwood Inference

Date: Fri, 21 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SIG / CAIQ annual refresh — request to escrow critical artifacts in Vendr

Hi Jared / Priya —

Following up on last quarter's intake: CloudCo is in scope for our annual SIG / CAIQ refresh and SOC 2 evidence reconciliation. To avoid last-minute gaps during their audit window, we prefer to have a small set of critical artifacts placed in escrow (read-only, time-limited) so the security reviewers can access them without repeated downloads.

What we're asking for: a one-time upload of the items listed in the attached SIG-CAIQ-Refresh-Checklist.xlsx and the signed evidence escrow form (attached). Specific asks:
- CAIQ answers snapshot (export JSON or Excel)
- SOC 2: latest security monitoring configuration docs + redacted incident log for last 12 months
- Key SSO metadata (IdP SAML certs) and MFA policy screenshots
- Any compensating control narratives for data isolation we can reference

Preferred upload targets (pick one):
1) Vendr profile upload (recommended) — we will grant a time-limited uploader token and then lock the files into a read-only escrow folder.
2) S3 staging bucket (private link) — we will provide a pre-signed URL and verify checksum.

Timeline: can you get the bundle uploaded by EOD Tue, 25 May? Our audit window for the reviewer is 26–28 May and we want everything staged ahead of time.

Attachments: SIG-CAIQ-Refresh-Checklist.xlsx, evidence-escrow-request-form.pdf, vendr-upload-instructions.txt

Thanks — let me know if you need a short walkthrough.

—
Aditya Rao
Head of Sales, Enterprise | Redwood Inference
aditya@redwoodinference.com | (415) 555-0173


From: Jared Lin <jared@cloudco.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>

Date: Fri, 21 May 2027 10:03:12 -0700

Aditya —

Thanks for the clear checklist. We can do this but need clarification on two items:

Will Redwood accept the S3 staging approach (pre-signed URL) for SOC2 PDFs only, while we upload CAIQ answers and the questionnaire snapshot to Vendr?
2) Redaction guidance: do you need the incident log fully redacted for customer PII or is a redacted summary sufficient?

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>

Subject: RE: SIG / CAIQ annual refresh — request to escrow critical artifacts in Vendr

Aditya / Ben —

Internal playbook we will follow:
- Provision time-limited uploader role on Vendr for CloudCo (72h TTL). Token will be scoped to the "evidence-escrow" folder; once upload completes we'll change ACLs to read-only and attach the escrow-form.pdf.
- For SOC2 PDFs: we accept the S3 staging approach. Procurement (Kevin) will be looped for the transfer; provide pre-signed PUT URLs for the bucket: s3://redwood-escrow/cloudco/2027-05-soc2/ (we'll verify SHA256).
- Redaction: legal will accept a redacted incident log with PII removed plus an unredacted internal index (access-controlled). If CloudCo cannot provide that, we can accept redacted summaries with dates and severity buckets.

I'll generate the Vendr upload token and the S3 pre-signed URLs by EOD today and paste the details into the secure Doc (link below). Ben — please run the checksum verification after upload and confirm with Kevin.

Related links: https://vendr.example.com/orgs/cloudco/profiles/redwood-inference

Naomi

naomi_feldman@redwoodinference.com

- Vendr token: single-use uploader, TTL 72h. Look for an email with the subject \"Vendr Uploader Token — Redwood / CloudCo\" from naomi_feldman@redwoodinference.com.
- S3 PUT URLs: will be generated for the path s3://redwood-escrow/cloudco/2027-05-soc2/ and should be used only for the files named in the checklist (SOC2-report-redacted.pdf, security-monitoring-config.pdf).

source.subject: Scheduling: Dedicated capacity demo — throughput & latency intake
messages:
From: Sophie Lin <sophie.lin@cardinalbio.com>
To: sales@redwood.ai
Date: Tue, 18 May 2027 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Demo request — dedicated capacity for inference (cardinalbio.com)

Hi —

We just self-signed in and kicked the tires with the hosted API. We're evaluating Redwood for a production deployment and want to schedule a walkthrough focused on Dedicated capacity. Primary asks: sustain ~450–600 QPS (text-generation, 4k context) with p95 generation latency target <= 250ms, and predictable tail behavior around burst windows.

Can someone from sales/solutions help us with a short demo and a quick capacity intake call? We're on PT.

Thanks,
Sophie Lin
Senior ML Engineer | Cardinal Bio
Sophie.Lin@cardinalbio.com

[Form submission ID: fs_9f3a]

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Sophie Lin <sophie.lin@cardinalbio.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 18 May 2027 16:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Demo request — dedicated capacity for inference (cardinalbio.com)

Hi Sophie —

Thanks for signing up and for the details. I'm Alex on the Solutions team — happy to run the walkthrough. Ben (SDR) is looping in to coordinate times. High level plan: a 45-minute call covering architecture, capacity sizing, and a short Q&A about integration and security.

Quick asks before scheduling: could you share a short sample of peak QPS windows (estimate) and whether the 4k context is typical vs. occasional? Also any constraints on regions (we have US-west and EU). If helpful, I've attached the Redwood dedicated overview and a quick intake checklist.

Proposed slots (PT):
- Wed May 19, 10:00–10:45am PT
- Wed May 19, 3:00–3:45pm PT
- Thu May 20, 9:00–9:45am PT

If none of these work, tell us a couple of windows that do and Ben will send an invite.

Cheers,
Alex Martinez
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.ai
Attachments: redwood-dedicated-overview.pdf, dedicated-intake-checklist.docx

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Sophie Lin <sophie.lin@cardinalbio.com>
Cc: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 18 May 2027 16:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Demo request — dedicated capacity for inference (cardinalbio.com)

Hi Sophie — quick intro, I'm Ben from SDR. Following up to lock a time. I'll send an invite once you confirm a slot. Also, if you'd like us to include any specific members from engineering or security please tell us now.

Candidate: Wed May 19, 3:00pm PT — shall I send the calendar invite?

Cheers,
Ben
Ben Carter | Sales Development Representative
ben.carter@redwood.ai

---
Attachment: cardinal_qps_requirements.pdf (placeholder — please attach your traffic profile if you have it)

From: Sophie Lin <sophie.lin@cardinalbio.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 19 May 2027 08:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Demo request — dedicated capacity for inference (cardinalbio.com)

Hi Ben/Alex —

Thanks — 3:00pm PT on Wed works for me. Quick clarification: our 450–600 QPS is bursty around biological-data ingestion windows — roughly 45–60 minutes spikes, otherwise baseline ~70 QPS. The 4k context is typical for most batches. Also, we're in US-west (primary) with a DR region in US-east but would prefer the demo to focus on US-west capacity.

One hiccup: my manager needs to join but is only available Thu morning. Is there flexibility to move to Thu May 20, 9:00am PT if needed? If you prefer to keep Wed we can do Wed and have a follow-up with my manager.

Best,
Sophie
Senior ML Engineer | Cardinal Bio

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Sophie Lin <sophie.lin@cardinalbio.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 19 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Demo request — dedicated capacity for inference (cardinalbio.com)

On Wed, May 19, 2027 at 08:05 AM Sophie Lin <sophie.lin@cardinalbio.com> wrote:
> Thanks — 3:00pm PT on Wed works for me. 
> Quick clarification: our 450–600 QPS is bursty around biological-data ingestion windows — roughly 45–60 minutes spikes, otherwise baseline ~70 QPS.
> The 4k context is typical for most batches.

Thanks for the detail — that's very helpful. Thu May 20, 9:00am PT works for us too; Ben, can you reschedule the slot to Thu 9:00am and include a short agenda and the pre-call intake as an attachment?

Sophie — attaching a short capacity checklist and our dedicated overview (PDF) that outlines the autoscale/burst options and expected latency envelopes. For the intake, could you also confirm:
- Typical token output length (avg tokens per request)
- Percentage of requests that hit the 4k context
- Any hard PII constraints in payloads (for security notes)

I'll send a calendar invite once Ben updates the slot.

— Alex

From: Sophie Lin <sophie.lin@cardinalbio.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 20 May 2027 09:43:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Demo request — dedicated capacity for inference (cardinalbio.com)

Hi Alex/Ben —

Thanks for being flexible. Confirming Thu May 20, 9:00–9:45am PT. Answers to your intake questions below:
- Avg tokens per request (output): ~120 tokens
- ~85% of requests use the 4k context; remainder are short prompts (~ <512 tokens)
- We do send de-identified patient metadata; no raw PII. We will share our SOC2 evidence if needed for procurement.

I've attached a short traffic export (cardinal_qps_requirements.pdf) and accepted the calendar invite (calendar-invite-CardinalBio.ics). Looking forward to the walkthrough.

Best,
Sophie
Senior ML Engineer | Cardinal Bio
Attachments: cardinal_qps_requirements.pdf

---
Note: Please include a brief section on warm-up/cold-start behavior during the demo — our infra team will want to see tail latency under burst startup.
